entries.title: Dashboard
entries.overview: The Dashboard is your command centre. Every time you log in, start here to see how your business is performing today — sales, stock health, expenses, and activity across all your branches.

before_you_start.text: You must be logged in to your business.
If you have multiple branches, use the Branch filter to focus on one branch or view all.

elements.name: Branch filter
elements.description: Select a specific branch to filter all dashboard data to that branch only. Choose 'All Branches' to see the combined view.
elements.notes: Changing this filter reloads all KPI cards and charts automatically.

elements.name: Refresh button
elements.description: Reloads all dashboard data from the server to show the latest numbers. Use this if you've just completed a sale or made changes and want to see updated figures.

elements.name: Sales Today card
elements.description: Shows the total gross sales amount for today in TZS. The mini chart below the number shows the sales trend over the last 8 data points.

elements.name: Revenue MTD card
elements.description: Total net revenue from the 1st of this month to today — gross sales minus refunds and credit notes. Compare against your monthly target to gauge trading performance. A detailed daily breakdown is shown in the Revenue / Sales Trend chart below.

elements.name: Expenses MTD card
elements.description: Total expenses recorded via the Expenses module from the 1st of this month to today. Compare against Revenue MTD for a rough profitability check — a detailed category breakdown is in the Expense Breakdown panel below.

elements.name: Stock Value card
elements.description: Shows the total monetary value of all stock currently in your inventory, and how many product variants are being tracked.

elements.name: Low Stock card
elements.description: Shows how many product variants are below the stock alert threshold (5 units). The sub-label shows how many of those are critically low (2 units or below).

elements.name: Active Branches card
elements.description: Shows how many branches recorded at least one sale this month. Sub-label shows transfers made today.

elements.name: Revenue / Sales Trend chart
elements.description: A line chart showing daily sales totals for the last 30 days. Useful for spotting patterns, slow days, or growth.

elements.name: Top Products panel
elements.description: Shows the 5 products that generated the most revenue this week. The bar next to each product shows its revenue relative to the top performer.

elements.name: Low Stock Alerts panel
elements.description: Lists up to 5 products that are running low on stock. Items highlighted in red are critical (2 units or below). Go to Stock to replenish.

elements.name: Sales by Branch panel
elements.description: Shows how much revenue each branch has generated this month, ranked with a bar chart. Useful if you manage multiple locations.

elements.name: Expense Breakdown panel
elements.description: Shows your top expense categories for this month with amounts and percentage of total spend.

elements.name: Recent Activity panel
elements.description: Shows the last 8 business events — sales, transfers, and alerts — with timestamps. Gives you a quick view of what has happened today.

elements.name: Global Search box
elements.description: Type to search across products, customers, receipts, and transfers. Suggestions appear automatically after 2 characters. Press Enter or click Search to run the full search.

elements.name: Search button
elements.description: Runs the search for whatever is typed in the search box and shows results below in two columns: Products, and Other Matches (receipts, customers, transfers).

elements.name: Open Full Search link
elements.description: Takes you to the dedicated Search page where you can run more detailed searches with additional filters.

elements.name: Quick Actions bar
elements.description: Three shortcut buttons at the top: New Sale (opens POS), New Transfer (opens transfer creation), and Stock Count (opens count wizard). Provides fast access to the most common workflows without navigating the sidebar.

elements.name: Daily Summary sentence
elements.description: A single sentence summarising today's activity — for example: 'Today you made 12 sales totaling TZS 450,000, with 3 pending approvals.' Updates when the dashboard data is refreshed.

elements.name: Pending Sync counter
elements.description: Shows the number of offline transactions waiting to be synced. Visible in the toolbar area. If this number is stuck above zero, check your internet connection or go to the Offline page.

elements.name: Open Shifts counter
elements.description: Shows how many shifts are currently open across all branches. Visible in the toolbar area next to Pending Sync.

elements.name: Sales Trend percentage badge
elements.description: A small badge on the Sales Today card showing the day-over-day percentage change (e.g. ▲ 12% or ▼ 5%). Green for positive, red for negative. Helps you quickly see if today is better or worse than yesterday.

elements.name: Margin percentage badge
elements.description: Shown on the Revenue MTD card. Displays the gross margin percentage for the month so far.

elements.name: Critical Low Stock badge
elements.description: A sub-label on the Low Stock card showing how many items are critically low (2 units or below). These items need urgent restocking.

elements.name: Transfers Today counter
elements.description: Shown as a sub-label on the Active Branches card. Shows the number of stock transfers created today.

elements.name: Low Stock severity indicators
elements.description: In the Low Stock Alerts panel, items are colour-coded by severity: red border and pulsing badge for critical (≤2 units), amber for low (≤5 units). Helps prioritise which items to restock first.

elements.name: Expense Breakdown progress bars
elements.description: Each expense category in the Expense Breakdown panel shows a progress bar indicating its percentage of total expenses. The percentage is also displayed as text.

elements.name: Activity type icons
elements.description: Each entry in the Recent Activity panel has a colour-coded icon: green tick for sales, blue arrows for transfers, red exclamation for alerts. Makes it easy to scan activity type at a glance.

elements.name: Recent Sales table
elements.description: A compact table at the bottom showing the most recent completed sales. Columns: receipt number, time, total amount, and status badge. Click a row to navigate to the receipt.

elements.name: Search result summary
elements.description: When using the Global Search box, a summary line appears showing the count of matching Products, Receipts, and Customers. Helps you know which category has results before opening full search.

common_tasks.task: Check today's sales performance
steps: Look at the Sales Today card for the total.
Check the Revenue / Sales Trend chart to see if today's figure is typical.
If you manage multiple branches, use the Branch filter to check each one.

common_tasks.task: Find a low-stock product
steps: Check the Low Stock card to see how many items need attention.
Look at the Low Stock Alerts panel to see which specific products are low.
Go to Stock > Products to take action.

common_tasks.task: Search for a product or customer
steps: Type in the Global Search box — suggestions appear after 2 characters.
Click a suggestion or press Enter to search.
For more options, click 'Open Full Search'.

warnings: Dashboard data does not update automatically while you are on the page — click Refresh to see the latest figures.
The Low Stock threshold is set at 5 units. Items at 2 or below are flagged as critical.
The Sales Today card shows gross sales (before any expenses or returns). For profit figures, look at the Revenue MTD and Expenses MTD cards together.

related_pages.reason: You must be logged in before accessing the Dashboard.
Use Search for detailed lookups across products, customers, and receipts.
For deeper analysis beyond the dashboard summaries.
Act on low-stock alerts by going to Stock.
View the full list of system notifications and alerts.

entries.title: Approvals
entries.overview: Review and act on pending approval requests. Approval requests are created automatically whenever a transaction exceeds a configured threshold — such as a discount above the limit, a large purchase, a stock adjustment, a refund, or a transfer. This page is the single queue where managers and authorised staff process those requests. Approving a request lets the original transaction complete; rejecting it blocks it permanently and records your reason.

elements.name: Loaded items KPI
elements.description: Total number of approval records fetched in the current view. If the count is high, use the Status or Action filters to narrow down what needs immediate attention.

elements.name: Pending now KPI
elements.description: Count of requests still awaiting a decision. Monitor this regularly — expired requests cannot be reactivated, and the original transaction remains blocked until you act.

elements.name: Status filter KPI
elements.description: Shows which status filter is currently active. PENDING shows only unapproved requests; switch to APPROVED or REJECTED to audit past decisions; EXPIRED shows timed-out requests that were never actioned.

elements.name: Action filter KPI
elements.description: Shows which action type filter is active. Use this to focus on a specific category — for example, filter to DISCOUNT to review all pending discount approvals before the end of a shift.

elements.name: Search input
elements.description: Free-text search across request data. Useful for finding approvals linked to a specific receipt number, supplier reference, or product name.

elements.name: Status filter
elements.description: Filter by PENDING, APPROVED, REJECTED, or EXPIRED. EXPIRED means the approval window timed out before anyone acted — the original transaction was automatically blocked and cannot be recovered from this page.

elements.name: Action type filter
elements.description: Filter by the type of action that triggered the request: STOCK_ADJUSTMENT, STOCK_COUNT, REFUND, DISCOUNT, TRANSFER, or PURCHASE_ORDER. Use this when you need to review all requests of one category at once.

elements.name: Date range (From / To)
elements.description: Restricts results to requests created within the selected date window. Useful for end-of-month reviews or when investigating a specific period.

elements.name: Approve button
elements.description: Immediately approves the request. The system notifies the original requester and the blocked transaction is allowed to complete. This action is final and cannot be undone — review the request detail carefully before clicking.

elements.name: Reject button
elements.description: Opens a prompt where you must enter a rejection reason. Once confirmed, the original transaction is permanently blocked and the requester is notified with your reason. The reason is stored in the audit log.

elements.name: ViewToggle
elements.description: Switch between card view (easier to read individual request details) and table view (better for scanning a large queue at once).

elements.name: PaginationControls
elements.description: Navigate between pages of approval records. Combine with filters to work through the queue efficiently — for example, filter to PENDING then page through to action everything.

elements.name: Bulk approve/reject
elements.description: Select multiple approval requests using checkboxes, then click Bulk Approve or Bulk Reject to process them all at once. A 'Select All' checkbox selects everything on the current page.

elements.name: Delegation feature
elements.description: Delegate an approval request to another user. Click the amber Delegate button on a pending item to open the Delegate modal, pick a user, and confirm. The request moves to their queue. ESC or backdrop click closes the modal without delegating.

elements.name: FlipCounter KPIs
elements.description: KPI cards use animated FlipCounter numbers for Loaded Items, Pending Now, and other counts.

elements.name: Action type badges
elements.description: Each approval shows its action type as a badge: Stock Adjustment, Stock Count, Refund, Discount, Transfer, Purchase. Helps quickly identify what kind of approval is pending.

elements.name: StatusBadge
elements.description: Coloured status pills: amber for PENDING, green for APPROVED, red for REJECTED.

elements.name: SortableTableHeader
elements.description: Clickable column headers for sorting in table view.

elements.name: Reference number
elements.description: Each approval request has an auto-generated reference number (e.g. APR-001). Shown alongside the target information in both card and table views.

before_you_start.text: You need the approvals.write permission to approve or reject requests. Without it, the Approve and Reject buttons are visible but disabled.
Approval thresholds (the limits that trigger a request) are configured in Business Settings. If you are unsure why a request was generated, check the threshold settings there.

common_tasks.task: Approve a pending request
steps: Set the Status filter to PENDING to see only unapproved requests.
Find the request — use Search or the Action filter to locate it faster if the queue is long.
Click Approve. The requester is notified automatically and the transaction proceeds.

common_tasks.task: Reject a request with a reason
steps: Set the Status filter to PENDING and locate the request.
Click Reject — a prompt appears asking for a rejection reason.
Enter your reason and confirm. The transaction is blocked and the requester is notified with your reason.

common_tasks.task: Review all approvals for a specific action type
steps: Use the Action type filter to select the category (e.g. DISCOUNT or REFUND).
Optionally add a date range to narrow the period.
Use the Status filter to separate PENDING items from historical decisions.

warnings: Expired requests cannot be reactivated. If a request expires, the original user must retry the transaction — a new approval request will be generated.
Approvals are final. Once you click Approve, the action proceeds immediately with no undo. Double-check the request detail before approving large adjustments or refunds.
Only users with the approvals.write permission can approve or reject. If the buttons are disabled, your role may be branch-scoped and the request belongs to a different branch.

entries.title: Attachments
entries.overview: Upload and manage documents linked to purchases or purchase orders — such as supplier invoices, delivery notes, signed contracts, or proof of payment. Each document you select shows its own attachment list. Files are stored securely and can be retrieved at any time for auditing, dispute resolution, or record-keeping.

elements.name: Target type selector
elements.description: Choose whether to search for a Purchase or a Purchase Order. This controls which records appear in the Target document selector below (inside the modal).

elements.name: Target document selector
elements.description: SmartSelect showing all records of the chosen type. Each option includes the supplier name, reference number, and date to help you identify the right document. The attachment list only loads after you select a target document (inside the modal).

elements.name: File input
elements.description: Opens your device's file browser. Select the document you want to upload. File size limits are set by your subscription plan — check the Business Settings subscription panel for your current limits (inside the modal).

elements.name: "Upload" button (header) + modal
elements.description: An accent-colored button in the page header opens a file upload modal. The modal contains: target type selector (purchase / purchase order), target document selector, drag-and-drop upload zone with "Browse files" fallback, selected-file preview row, and an Upload submit button. Previously this was an inline CollapsibleSection on the page — moved into a modal. **Fix**: the file-input `accept` attribute was narrowed to `.pdf,.jpg,.jpeg,.png` to match the backend's actual allowed MIME types — previously it accepted Word/Excel/CSV/WebP which the backend would reject with a 400 error. Upload flow still uses the 2-step presign pattern (POST /presign → client PUTs to S3 → POST /attachments to register).

elements.name: Attachment list
elements.description: Shows all files linked to the selected document. Columns include filename, file size, upload version, and status (ACTIVE or REMOVED). Click a filename to download the file.

elements.name: Remove button
elements.description: Marks an attachment as REMOVED. The file is no longer shown by default but is retained in the audit log for compliance. This cannot be undone from the UI — contact support if you need to restore a removed file.

elements.name: Load More button
elements.description: Loads the next page of attachment records when there are more files than the initial batch.

elements.name: ViewToggle
elements.description: Switch between card layout and table layout for the attachment list. Table view is better when reviewing many attachments at once.

elements.name: Active count KPI
elements.description: Number of non-removed attachments currently loaded for the selected document type. Use this to confirm an upload succeeded or to spot documents that still need files attached.

elements.name: Total size KPI
elements.description: Combined file size in MB of all loaded attachments. Monitor this against your subscription storage limit.

elements.name: CollapsibleSection (Upload form)
elements.description: The upload form is hidden behind an 'Upload' button. Includes a drag-and-drop zone and file browser (inside the modal).

elements.name: File type filter
elements.description: Filter attachments by type: All, Images (JPEG/PNG/WebP), Documents (PDF/DOC/DOCX), or Spreadsheets (XLS/XLSX/CSV).

elements.name: Drag-and-drop upload zone
elements.description: Drop files directly onto the upload area or click to browse. Supported formats: PDF, JPEG, PNG, WebP, DOC, DOCX, XLS, XLSX, CSV (inside the modal).

elements.name: File versioning
elements.description: Upload a new version of an existing file. The version history is maintained and accessible. Each version shows its version number (v1, v2, etc.) and upload date.

elements.name: Bulk download
elements.description: Select multiple files using checkboxes and download them all at once. A 'Select All' checkbox selects all files matching the current filter.

elements.name: Storage tracking
elements.description: A KPI card showing total storage used in MB. Helps monitor storage consumption against subscription limits.

elements.name: File type icons
elements.description: Each attachment displays an icon based on its file type (PDF icon, image icon, spreadsheet icon, document icon) for quick visual identification.

elements.name: MIME type display
elements.description: The file's MIME type is shown on each attachment card and in table view.

elements.name: Version history panel
elements.description: Click 'View Versions' on an attachment to see all previous versions with download links and upload timestamps.

before_you_start.text: You need the attachments.write permission to upload or remove files.
At least one purchase or purchase order must exist before you can attach files. Create a purchase or PO first from the Purchases page.
You must select a target document before the attachment list loads or the Upload button becomes active.

common_tasks.task: Upload an invoice to a purchase
steps: Set Target type to Purchase.
Select the purchase from the Target document selector (search by supplier or reference).
Click the file input and select the invoice file from your device.
Click Upload. The file appears immediately in the attachment list below.

common_tasks.task: View and download all documents on a purchase order
steps: Set Target type to Purchase Order.
Select the purchase order from the Target document selector.
The attachment list loads. Click any filename to download it to your device.

warnings: Removing an attachment is permanent from a user perspective — there is no restore button in the UI. Only support can recover a removed file.
The Upload button is inactive until you have selected a target document. If it appears greyed out, select a document from the Target document selector first.

entries.title: Audit Logs
entries.overview: An immutable, cryptographically hash-chained log of every action taken in the system — by users, automated jobs, and background processes. Every write operation generates an entry capturing who acted, from which branch and device, with what outcome, and what changed (before/after snapshot). Use audit logs to investigate disputes, verify compliance, trace a specific change, or understand the full sequence of events during an incident. Logs cannot be modified or deleted. All three noise categories — guard checks, auth refreshes, and dashboard reports — are hidden by default to surface only meaningful user-initiated events.

elements.name: KPI — Loaded Events
elements.description: Shows the number of log entries in the current page after noise filters are applied. This reflects the filtered, visible count — not the total audit volume in the system. Enable noise toggles to see guard checks, auth refreshes, and dashboard reports included in the count.

elements.name: KPI — Success
elements.description: Count of SUCCESS-outcome entries among the currently loaded logs. A consistently high success count is normal. Track this against the failure count when investigating incidents.

elements.name: KPI — Failure
elements.description: Count of FAILURE-outcome entries among the currently loaded logs. Spikes here can indicate repeated permission denials, validation errors, failed login attempts, or offline sync issues. Filter to Outcome = FAILURE to drill in.

elements.name: KPI — Active Filters
elements.description: Count of currently active filter values plus any enabled noise toggles. Use this to confirm your filter set at a glance — a count of 0 means you are viewing the unfiltered default view.

elements.name: From Date filter
elements.description: Sets the start of the time window for log retrieval. Always apply a date range when querying — audit logs are stored indefinitely and an unbounded query over a busy system may return a very large result set.

elements.name: To Date filter
elements.description: Sets the end of the time window. Combine with From Date to target a specific incident window — for example, the shift when a discrepancy was reported.

elements.name: Outcome filter
elements.description: Filters by action result: SUCCESS or FAILURE. Filtering to FAILURE is the fastest way to find permission denials, validation errors, and rejected operations. Leave blank to see both outcomes together.

elements.name: Activity Type preset
elements.description: 17 predefined presets that set the resourceType (and sometimes action) in one click: stockAdjust, stockCount, stockMovements, productCreate, productUpdate, variantCreate, variantUpdate, categoryCreate, sales, refunds, purchaseOrders, purchases, receiving, transfers, approvals, users, roles. Selecting a preset clears the raw action/resourceType inputs and sets them automatically.

elements.name: Actor / User filter
elements.description: Dropdown populated from all users in the business. Select a user to see only log entries where that person was the actor. Combine with a date range to investigate a specific shift or time period. Shown by name and email to distinguish users with similar names.

elements.name: More Filters button
elements.description: Toggles the advanced filter panel open or closed. Shows a badge with the count of currently active filter values so you can see at a glance whether advanced filters are applied even when the panel is closed. Closing the panel resets all advanced-only filters (role, trace IDs, resource ID, approval status).

elements.name: Refresh Logs button
elements.description: Re-runs the current query and reloads the log list from the server. Audit logs are served with no-cache headers — this button is the only way to pick up new entries without navigating away. Useful when monitoring a live incident in real time.

elements.name: Export CSV button
elements.description: Downloads a CSV file of the currently filtered log set, capped at 2000 records. The export includes 17 columns: timestamp, action, outcome, resourceType, resourceId, userId, roleId, branchId, deviceId, reason, narrative summary, diff, and metadata. Apply filters before exporting to avoid hitting the 2000-record cap.

elements.name: Export PDF button
elements.description: Downloads a PDF report of the filtered log set, capped at 2000 records. Each entry includes timestamp, action, outcome, and the human-readable narrative. Suitable for sharing with auditors or attaching to compliance reports.

elements.name: Branch filter — advanced
elements.description: Restricts results to log entries generated by users operating within a specific branch. Available in the advanced filter panel. Useful for branch managers who only need to review their own branch's activity or for comparing event volumes across branches.

elements.name: Role filter — advanced
elements.description: Filters by the role the user held at the moment the action was taken. Because role assignments can change over time, this reflects the historical role captured at event time — not the user's current role. Available in the advanced filter panel.

elements.name: Online / Offline filter — advanced
elements.description: Filters to events that were performed while online or while the device was in offline mode. Offline events are synced to the server later and carry the original device timestamp. Use 'Offline only' to audit actions taken during connectivity gaps.

elements.name: Action text filter — advanced
elements.description: Raw action string filter — accepts exact action codes like STOCK_ADJUST, SALE_COMPLETE, APPROVAL_REJECT, PERMISSION_CHECK. Use this when you need a specific action not covered by the Activity Type preset dropdown. Case-sensitive.

elements.name: Resource Type filter — advanced
elements.description: Raw resource type string filter — accepts entity names like Sale, Transfer, StockMovement, PurchaseOrder, User, Role. Setting a resource type also populates the Resource ID datalist with matching records from the API for easier lookup.

elements.name: Resource ID datalist — advanced
elements.description: Filters to all log entries for a single specific record. When Resource Type is set, the input autocompletes from a live API fetch of that entity type (up to 200 items by name). Accepts a raw ID if the record is not in the autocomplete list. Use this to see the full history of one record.

elements.name: Approval Status filter — advanced
elements.description: Shorthand filter that maps to approval action sets on the server: REQUESTED shows APPROVAL_REQUEST and APPROVAL_SELF_APPROVE events; APPROVED shows APPROVAL_APPROVE and APPROVAL_SELF_APPROVE; REJECTED shows APPROVAL_REJECT. Equivalent to the 'approvals' Activity Type preset but with status-level granularity.

elements.name: Trace ID inputs — advanced
elements.description: Four text inputs for forensic tracing: Correlation ID (all logs from one HTTP request chain), Request ID (a single HTTP request), Session ID (all actions within one login session), Device ID (all actions from a registered offline device). Enter a known ID to pull every event associated with that scope.

elements.name: Show Guard Checks toggle
elements.description: When enabled, unhides PERMISSION_CHECK and SUBSCRIPTION_CHECK log entries that are suppressed by default. These are generated on every route or action guard evaluation. Enable this when investigating permission denial patterns or unexpected 403 responses.

elements.name: Show Auth Refresh toggle
elements.description: When enabled, unhides AUTH_REFRESH and AUTH_REFRESH_REUSE log entries. Auth refreshes occur automatically in the background whenever the access token is renewed. Enable this when investigating session or token issues, or suspected token reuse attacks.

elements.name: Show Dashboard Reports toggle
elements.description: When enabled, unhides log entries with auditOrigin = 'dashboard' — generated by background dashboard metric queries. These are suppressed by default because they produce high-volume read-only noise. Enable when auditing what dashboard data has been accessed.

elements.name: Log list
elements.description: The main list of audit entries. Each row shows: an outcome dot (green = SUCCESS, red = FAILURE), a human-readable narrative (e.g. 'Jane updated Product Widget A'), a resource type badge, actor name, and timestamp. Click any row to open its detail panel on the right. Click again to close.

elements.name: Detail panel
elements.description: Sticky right-side panel showing the full context of the selected log entry. Sections: header (outcome, resource type, severity, primary narrative, timestamp), narrative extras (reason, failure message, offline flag, diff summary, impact, approval status), Who (user, role, branch, device in a 2×2 grid). Includes a Technical Trace section (collapsible) showing requestId, sessionId, correlationId, hash, and previousHash — values are truncated with the full ID visible on hover. Also includes a Raw Event JSON collapsible for copy-paste forensics.

elements.name: What Changed — diff highlights
elements.description: Shows up to 8 field-level changes for UPDATE actions inside the detail panel. Each row shows the field name, the old value (struck through), and the new value. Field names are mapped to human-readable labels (e.g. 'unitPrice' → 'Unit Price'). Only changed fields are shown — unchanged fields are omitted.

elements.name: View Chain button
elements.description: Loads the chain-of-custody timeline for the selected log entry. The chain query uses the entry's correlationId first, then requestId, and falls back to resourceId if neither is present. Fetches up to 200 related events. The timeline appears below the log list, sorted chronologically, showing the full sequence of events triggered by the same operation.

elements.name: Related Links
elements.description: Contextual deep-link buttons in the detail panel that navigate to the module page for the log's resource. For example: a Sale log links to Receipts filtered to that sale; a StockMovement log links to Stock Movements; a Transfer log links to Transfers. Use these to jump directly from the audit trail to the affected record.

elements.name: Chain Playback Timeline
elements.description: Appears below the log list after View Chain is clicked. Displays an anchor line showing the triggering event (action, resource type, correlation ID) and a vertical chronological timeline of all related events — each with narrative, timestamp, resource type, and reason. Dismiss with the ✕ button. Supports tracing multi-step operations like sale completion → stock deduction → approval notification.

elements.name: Activity by user section
elements.description: A collapsible section showing a ranked list of users by action count. Click 'Load' to fetch the data. Shows each user's name, avatar, and total number of actions in the filtered period.

elements.name: Activity type presets
elements.description: A dropdown with 17 preset activity filters (Sales, Purchases, Stock Adjustments, Transfers, etc.) for quickly narrowing the log to a specific area of the business.

elements.name: Noise toggles
elements.description: Three toggles to show or hide system noise: Guard Checks (permission verifications), Dashboard Reports (dashboard data loads), and Auth Refresh (token refreshes). Hidden by default to keep the log focused on business actions.

elements.name: Card view (ViewToggle)
elements.description: A card view option alongside the default table view. Cards show an audit narrative summary with diff highlights for each event.

elements.name: No auto branch filter
elements.description: The audit logs page loads all events across all branches by default. Business-level operations like imports, settings changes, and subscription events don't belong to any specific branch — they only appear when no branch filter is active. Use the branch filter to narrow results to a specific branch if needed.

before_you_start.text: You need the 'audit.read' permission to access this page. Users without this permission cannot view any audit log entries.
Audit logs are append-only and immutable — no one can edit, delete, or backdate an entry. This is by design for compliance. Any apparent gap in the hash chain should be escalated immediately.

Guard checks (PERMISSION_CHECK, SUBSCRIPTION_CHECK), auth refresh events (AUTH_REFRESH, AUTH_REFRESH_REUSE), and dashboard report reads are hidden by default. The loaded count and KPIs reflect only visible events — enable the noise toggles in the advanced panel to include them.
Export is capped at 2000 records. Apply a date range and relevant filters before exporting to avoid truncated results on busy systems.

common_tasks.task: Find all actions taken by a specific user
steps: Select the user from the Actor / User filter dropdown.
Set From and To dates to the relevant period.
Review the log list — use the Outcome filter (FAILURE) to spot any denied or failed actions.
Click any row to open the detail panel and see the full before/after diff.

common_tasks.task: Filter logs by activity type
steps: Click the Activity Type preset dropdown.
Select a preset such as 'stockAdjust', 'sales', 'approvals', or 'transfers'.
The resourceType (and action where applicable) is set automatically.
Combine with a date range or user filter to narrow results further.

common_tasks.task: Investigate what changed on a specific record
steps: Set Resource Type to the entity type (e.g. Product, Sale, Transfer).
Use the Resource ID datalist to search by name and select the record.
Review UPDATE entries — open each to see the What Changed diff highlights.
Click View Chain on any entry to see if the change was part of a larger operation.

common_tasks.task: Trace the full sequence of an operation (chain of custody)
steps: Find the primary log entry for the operation (e.g. a sale completion).
Open the detail panel and click 'View Chain'.
The Chain Playback Timeline loads below, showing all related events in chronological order.
Review each event in the timeline to understand every step triggered by that operation.

common_tasks.task: Show hidden system events (guard checks, auth refreshes)
steps: Click 'More Filters' to open the advanced panel.
Check 'Show Guard Checks' to reveal PERMISSION_CHECK and SUBSCRIPTION_CHECK entries.
Check 'Show Auth Refresh' to reveal AUTH_REFRESH and AUTH_REFRESH_REUSE entries.
Click Refresh Logs to reload with the noise toggles applied.

common_tasks.task: Filter to offline-only events
steps: Click 'More Filters' to open the advanced panel.
Set the Online / Offline filter to 'Offline only'.
Apply a date range if investigating a specific connectivity gap.
Review entries — offline events carry the original device timestamp and include a deviceId.

common_tasks.task: Filter approval events by status
steps: Click 'More Filters' to open the advanced panel.
Set Approval Status to REQUESTED, APPROVED, or REJECTED.
The backend maps each status to the corresponding action set automatically.
Combine with a User filter to see approvals actioned by a specific manager.

common_tasks.task: Navigate from a log entry to the affected record
steps: Click any log row to open the detail panel.
Scroll to the Related Links section at the bottom of the detail panel.
Click the relevant link (e.g. 'View Sale', 'View Transfer') to open the record in its module.
Use the browser back button to return to the audit log with your filters intact.

common_tasks.task: Export filtered logs to CSV or PDF
steps: Apply all relevant filters (date range, activity type, user, branch, etc.).
Click 'Export CSV' for a spreadsheet with 17 columns including narrative and diff, or 'Export PDF' for a formatted report.
The export applies your current filters and noise-toggle settings, capped at 2000 records.
If the download is empty, verify your filters are not too narrow.

common_tasks.task: Forensic trace by correlation ID or request ID
steps: Click 'More Filters' to open the advanced panel.
Paste the known Correlation ID or Request ID into the corresponding input.
Click Refresh Logs — all events sharing that ID are returned.
This groups every log entry produced within a single HTTP request chain, regardless of resource type.

warnings: The three noise toggles (Show Guard Checks, Show Auth Refresh, Show Dashboard Reports) are OFF by default. The KPI counts and log list exclude those events unless the toggles are enabled — the loaded count will not represent total audit volume on the system.

Each log entry is cryptographically hash-chained to the previous one. A gap or mismatch in the hash or previousHash fields is a serious integrity signal that should be escalated immediately rather than ignored.

Chain playback groups events using correlationId first, then requestId — logs that have neither will fall back to resourceId matching, which may include unrelated events that happened to touch the same record.

Export is capped at 2000 records per request. If your filtered result set is larger, the download will be silently truncated. Always apply a date range and verify the Active Filters KPI shows a meaningful scope before exporting.

entries.title: Categories
entries.overview: Manage the category taxonomy used to organise your product catalog. Categories can be flat or nested — each category may optionally have a parent, allowing you to group related products into a hierarchy (e.g. Beverages → Soft Drinks). This page combines the category list with an inline create form and inline editing, so no navigation away is required. The list supports search and status filtering, and all filter state is URL-persisted.
before_you_start: You need the Catalog Read permission to view categories and Catalog Write permission to create or edit them.
Categories cannot be deleted — to retire a category, change its status to INACTIVE or ARCHIVED. Note: status changes cannot currently be made from this UI; use the API directly if needed.
A category name is required to create or save. Leaving the name field blank will block submission.

elements.label: Categories
elements.description: Total count of category records loaded in the current view. Reflects the current page only — press Load More to include additional records.

elements.label: Active
elements.description: Count of loaded categories whose status is ACTIVE. Calculated client-side from the current page of results, not the total across all pages.

elements.label: Parent Groups
elements.description: Count of top-level categories (those with no parent assigned). Subcategories are excluded from this count.

elements.label: Filtered Status
elements.description: Displays the status filter currently applied, or 'All Statuses' when no status filter is active. Changes as you select a status from the filter.

elements.label: Search
elements.description: Free-text search on category name. Input is debounced 350 ms before triggering a new fetch. Case-insensitive. Clears when Reset Filters is pressed.

elements.label: Status Filter
elements.description: Filter the list to categories of a specific status: All Statuses, ACTIVE, INACTIVE, or ARCHIVED. Selecting a status updates the URL and refetches immediately.

elements.label: Reset Filters
elements.description: Clears the search and status filter, returning the list to the default unfiltered state.

elements.label: Advanced / Less
elements.description: Shows or hides the Status Filter control. The search box is always visible regardless of this toggle.

elements.label: View Toggle (Cards / Table)
elements.description: Switches between card view (default) and table view. Cards show the category name and parent with an inline edit form. Table shows Name, Parent, Status, and an Edit button in a compact grid. Preference is not persisted across sessions.

elements.label: Category Name
elements.description: Required field for creating a new category. Must be non-empty — the backend rejects blank names with a 400 error. Located in the New Category panel above the list.

elements.label: Parent Category
elements.description: Optional. Select a parent to make this a subcategory. The list is populated from all currently loaded categories. Leave blank to create a top-level category. Clearable — click the × to remove the parent selection.

elements.label: Create Category
elements.description: Submits the new category form. Disabled without Catalog Write permission (shows a tooltip explaining the restriction). Displays a spinner and 'Creating…' label while the request is in flight. On success, the form clears and the list reloads.

elements.label: Category List
elements.description: The main data view. In table mode: Name, Parent (resolved from local data), Status, and Edit button per row. In card mode (default): each card shows Name and Parent, with an Edit button that expands an inline edit form within the card. Only one card can be in edit mode at a time. 25 records are loaded per page.

elements.label: Load More
elements.description: Appends the next 25 categories to the current list. Only visible when more records exist beyond the current page. Shows a spinner while fetching. Changing any filter resets the list to page one.

elements.name: CollapsibleSection (New Category form)
elements.description: The create form is hidden behind a 'New Category' button. Click to expand the form, which includes Category Name and Parent Selector fields. The form closes automatically after successful creation and remembers its open/closed state during the session.

elements.name: StatusBadge
elements.description: Coloured status pills shown next to each category in both card and table views. Green for ACTIVE, amber for INACTIVE, grey for ARCHIVED.

elements.name: ActionButtons (table view)
elements.description: Icon buttons in the actions column of the table view. Includes Edit to open inline editing for that category.

elements.name: SortableTableHeader
elements.description: Clickable column headers in table view that sort the list. Available columns: Name, Parent, Products, Status. Click a header to sort ascending, click again for descending.

elements.name: Bulk selection checkboxes
elements.description: Each category row in table view has a checkbox. Select multiple categories to enable bulk actions. A counter banner appears showing how many are selected.

elements.name: Bulk Activate / Bulk Archive buttons
elements.description: Appear when categories are selected via checkboxes. Activate sets all selected categories to ACTIVE. Archive sets them to ARCHIVED. A Cancel button clears the selection.

elements.name: Product count bar chart
elements.description: Each category shows a small horizontal bar indicating how many products it contains, relative to the largest category. Helps visualise which categories are most populated.

common_tasks.title: Create a new top-level category
steps: In the New Category panel, type the category name in the Category Name field.
Leave the Parent Category field blank (or clear it if pre-filled).
Click Create Category.
The form clears and the list reloads — your new category appears at the top ordered by creation date.

common_tasks.title: Create a subcategory under an existing category
steps: Ensure the parent category already exists in the list (create it first if needed).
In the New Category panel, type the subcategory name.
Open the Parent Category select and choose the parent category.
Click Create Category. The subcategory appears in the list with the parent name shown.

common_tasks.title: Edit a category name or change its parent
steps: Find the category in the list. In card view, click Edit on the card. In table view, click Edit in the row.
Update the name or select a different parent from the Parent Category select.
Click Save. The list reloads with the updated values.
Click Cancel to discard changes without saving.

common_tasks.title: View only active categories
steps: Click Advanced to reveal the Status Filter.
Select 'ACTIVE' from the dropdown.
The list refetches showing only active categories. The Filtered Status KPI updates to confirm.

common_tasks.title: Switch to table view for a compact overview
steps: Click the View button in the top-right of the page header.
Select Table. The list switches to a compact grid showing Name, Parent, Status, and Edit per row.
Switch back to Cards at any time. The preference resets to Cards on the next page load.

warnings: Category status (ACTIVE, INACTIVE, ARCHIVED) cannot be changed from this page — there is no status field in the create or edit forms. To change a category's status, use the API directly.
Categories cannot be deleted. Retiring a category requires setting it to INACTIVE or ARCHIVED via the API.
The backend prevents circular parent references — you cannot set a category as a child of one of its own descendants, and a category cannot be its own parent. Attempting this will return a 400 error.

entries.title: Catalog › Products
entries.overview: Browse, create, and manage products in your catalog. Products are the top-level container — they hold a name, description, status, category, and images. Stock levels and pricing live on variants beneath each product. Use the quick-create form for a bare product shell, or open the Product Wizard for a guided flow that creates variants, sets pricing, and uploads images in one session.
before_you_start: Categories must exist before you can create a product — if the category list is empty, go to Catalog › Categories first.
The quick-create form creates a bare product shell with no variants, stock, or pricing. Use the Product Wizard for a complete setup.
Image upload is only available after a product already exists — you cannot attach images during quick creation.
The Additional Images upload zone is disabled until the product has at least one primary image.
You need the catalog.write permission to create, edit, or manage images. Without it, all write controls are disabled.

elements.label: Total Products
elements.description: System-wide product count returned from the API on first page load. Falls back to the count of products on the current page if no total is available.

elements.label: Active Products
elements.description: Count of ACTIVE-status products on the current page. Updates when you change page or filters. Does not reflect a system-wide total.

elements.label: Products With Images
elements.description: Count of products on the current page that have at least one ACTIVE image attached. Helps identify products missing media before a catalog publish.

elements.label: Catalog Focus
elements.description: Shows the name of the category currently applied as a filter, or 'All Categories' when no category filter is active. Useful orientation when browsing a large catalog.

elements.label: View Toggle
elements.description: Switches the product list between Cards view (full detail per product with images, notes, and related records) and Table view (compact multi-column layout). The active mode is remembered within the session.

elements.label: Search
elements.description: Debounced text search (350 ms delay) that filters products by name. Fires automatically as you type. Also available via the Enter key.

elements.label: Advanced Filters
elements.description: Expands or collapses the advanced filter row containing the Category, Status, Has Variants, and Has Images filters.

elements.label: Reset Filters
elements.description: Clears all active filters — search text, category, status, has variants, has images — and reloads the full product list from the first page.

elements.label: Category Filter
elements.description: Typeahead selector that filters the product list by category. Searches live against GET /categories as you type. Clear the selection to show all categories.

elements.label: Status Filter
elements.description: Filters the product list by status. Options: All, Active, Inactive, Archived. Archived products are hidden from sales flows but remain in the catalog.

elements.label: Has Variants Filter
elements.description: Filters by whether a product has at least one variant. 'Yes' shows only products with variants; 'No' shows bare shells that still need variants added.

elements.label: Has Images Filter
elements.description: Filters by whether a product has at least one active image attached. Use 'No' to find products that are missing media.

elements.label: Open Product Wizard
elements.description: Navigates to the Product Creation Wizard — a multi-step guided flow that creates the product, adds variants with pricing and cost, and uploads images in a single session. Recommended over the quick-create form for new products.

elements.label: Product Name (Create)
elements.description: Name for the new product. Required — the Create Product button remains disabled until this field has a non-empty value. Must be unique within the business.

elements.label: Description (Create)
elements.description: Optional short description for the new product. Displayed under the product name in both card and table views.

elements.label: Category (Create)
elements.description: Typeahead category selector for the new product. Required — the Create Product button is disabled without a category selection. Searches GET /categories as you type.

elements.label: Create Product
elements.description: Submits the quick-create form (POST /products). Creates a bare product shell with no variants or images. The form resets on success and the list refreshes to page 1.

elements.label: Edit
elements.description: Opens the inline edit form for a specific product card. Multiple products can be in edit mode simultaneously. Only visible to users with catalog.write permission.

elements.label: Product Name (Edit)
elements.description: Inline text field replacing the product name in edit mode. Required — Save is disabled while empty.

elements.label: Description (Edit)
elements.description: Inline text field for editing the product description. Optional.

elements.label: Category (Edit)
elements.description: Typeahead category selector in the inline edit form. Searches GET /categories as you type.

elements.label: Status (Edit)
elements.description: Status selector in the inline edit form. Options: Active, Inactive, Archived. Changing to Archived immediately removes the product from active sales flows for all users.

elements.label: Save
elements.description: Saves inline edits (PUT /products/:id). Disabled while the name field is empty or while saving is in progress. The card returns to read mode on success.

elements.label: Cancel
elements.description: Discards all unsaved inline edits and returns the card to read mode. The product is not modified.

elements.label: View / Hide Related
elements.description: Toggles the related records panel for a product. First open fetches up to 6 variants, their stock snapshots (branch-scoped if an active branch is selected), and the 5 most recent stock movements per variant. Subsequent toggles use cached data.

elements.label: Related Records Panel
elements.description: Inline panel showing three columns: Variants (up to 6), Stock on Hand (quantity per variant, filtered by active branch), and Recent Movements (type and quantity, up to 5 per variant). Helps verify stock without leaving the catalog.

elements.label: Notes Panel
elements.description: Embedded RelatedNotesPanel component showing notes linked to this product (resourceType: Product). Notes can be added, viewed, and managed inline without navigating to the Notes page.

elements.label: No Primary Image Warning
elements.description: Amber warning banner shown on a product card when no primary image is set. Prompts you to upload a primary image. Disappears as soon as a primary image is added.

elements.label: Primary Image Upload Zone
elements.description: Click-to-upload zone for the product's primary image. Accepts PNG and JPEG only. Upload is a two-step process: the app gets a presigned S3 URL, then uploads the file directly to S3, then registers the image record. Replaces the existing primary if one exists.

elements.label: Additional Images Upload Zone
elements.description: Click-to-upload zone for non-primary images. Disabled until the product has a primary image. Uploaded images appear in the gallery alongside the primary. Same two-step S3 presign flow.

elements.label: Image Gallery
elements.description: Grid of active image thumbnails for the product. The primary image is labeled with a 'PRIMARY' badge. Hover over a thumbnail to reveal the Make Primary and Remove action buttons.

elements.label: Make Primary
elements.description: Hover-overlay button on non-primary gallery thumbnails. Promotes the selected image to primary (POST /products/:id/images/:imageId/primary). The previous primary becomes a regular additional image.

elements.label: Remove Image
elements.description: Hover-overlay button on any gallery thumbnail. Soft-deletes the image (POST /products/:id/images/:imageId/remove) — it disappears immediately and cannot be restored from the UI.

elements.label: Product Table
elements.description: Compact table view of products with columns: thumbnail, name and description, category, status badge, active image count, and a View Related link. The View Related link switches to Cards view and opens the related records panel for that product.

elements.label: Pagination Controls
elements.description: Cursor-based navigation bar with previous and next page buttons, a page size selector (number of products per page), and a total product count display. Changing page size resets to page 1.

elements.label: Create product button (page header)
elements.description: Header action button with Plus icon. Opens the Create Product modal. Only shown to users with the catalog.write permission.

elements.label: Create Product modal
elements.description: Centered modal dialog opened from the Create product header button. Contains 'Open wizard' and 'Bulk import' shortcut buttons at the top, plus three fields: name, description, category. ESC or backdrop click closes without saving. Closes automatically after a successful create.

elements.label: Edit Product modal
elements.description: Centered modal dialog opened by clicking the Pencil action on a product card or table row. Shows the primary image thumbnail alongside editable fields: name, description, category, status. ESC or backdrop click closes without saving.

elements.label: Image drag-and-drop upload
elements.description: A drop zone on each product card where you can drag and drop image files or click to browse. Supports JPEG, PNG, and WebP. The first image uploaded becomes the primary product image automatically.

elements.label: Image gallery
elements.description: Shows all uploaded images for a product. The primary image has a 'Primary' badge. Hover over any image to see 'Make Primary' and 'Remove' buttons. Removing the primary image promotes the next image automatically.

elements.label: RelatedNotesPanel
elements.description: A notes thread attached to each product card. Team members can add timestamped notes visible to everyone — useful for recording supplier info, quality issues, or restocking reminders.

elements.label: Duplicate Product action
elements.description: Creates a copy of the product with all its variants, pricing, and settings. The copy is named '[Product Name] (Copy)'. Saves time when creating similar products.

elements.label: Bulk Import link
elements.description: A button that navigates to the Exports/Imports page with the Products import type pre-selected. Use this for importing many products at once via CSV.

elements.label: Last Sold timestamp
elements.description: Shown on each product card and in the table view. Displays when the product was last sold. Helps identify slow-moving or dormant products.

elements.label: Related Records panel
elements.description: Expandable panel on each product card showing three sections: Variants (list of all variants), Stock on Hand (current quantities per branch), and Recent Movements (latest stock changes). Click 'View Related' to expand.

elements.label: SortableTableHeader
elements.description: Clickable column headers in table view for sorting by Name, Category, Status, and Last Sold date.

elements.label: StatusBadge
elements.description: Coloured status pills on each product. Green for ACTIVE, amber for INACTIVE, grey for ARCHIVED.

elements.label: ActionButtons (table view)
elements.description: Icon buttons in the actions column: Edit, View Related, and Duplicate.

elements.label: Variant count badge
elements.description: A small badge on each product card showing how many variants it has.

elements.label: Image thumbnail (table view)
elements.description: A small 32x32 product image preview in the first column of the table view.

common_tasks.title: Create a product using the quick form
steps: In the New Product panel, enter the product name (required).
Optionally add a description.
Select a category from the typeahead dropdown (required).
Click Create Product — the product shell is created and the list refreshes.
Click Edit Permissions on the new product to add variants and images.

common_tasks.title: Create a product using the Product Wizard
steps: Click Open Product Wizard in the New Product panel.
Follow the multi-step wizard to set the product name, category, variants, pricing, costs, and images.
Submit the wizard — the product and its variants are created in one operation.
The new product appears in the list when you return to this page.

common_tasks.title: Edit a product's name, description, category, or status
steps: Locate the product in the Cards view.
Click the Edit button on the product card.
Update the name, description, category, or status fields inline.
Click Save — changes are applied immediately (PUT /products/:id).
Click Cancel to discard changes without saving.

common_tasks.title: Upload a primary image for a product
steps: Locate the product card in Cards view.
In the Images section, click the Primary Image upload zone.
Select a PNG or JPEG file from your device.
The file uploads directly to S3 via a presigned URL — wait for the spinner to clear.
The primary thumbnail appears on the card.

common_tasks.title: Upload additional images
steps: Ensure the product already has a primary image (Additional Images zone is disabled otherwise).
Click the Additional Images upload zone.
Select a PNG or JPEG file.
The image appears in the gallery alongside the primary.

common_tasks.title: Change which image is the primary
steps: In the Images section, hover over the gallery thumbnail you want to promote.
Click Make Primary in the hover overlay.
The selected image becomes the primary — the previous primary stays in the gallery as an additional image.

common_tasks.title: Remove an image
steps: Hover over the gallery thumbnail you want to remove.
Click Remove in the hover overlay.
The image is soft-deleted and disappears from the gallery immediately.

common_tasks.title: View related variants, stock, and movements
steps: Click View Related on the product card (or the View Related link in Table view — this switches to Cards view first).
The related records panel opens and fetches variants, stock, and recent movements.
Stock quantities are filtered by your active branch if one is selected.
Click Hide Related to collapse the panel.

common_tasks.title: Filter and search the product list
steps: Type in the Search box to filter by product name (debounced — results update automatically).
Click Advanced Filters to reveal Category, Status, Has Variants, and Has Images filters.
Apply one or more filters — the list reloads from page 1 each time.
Click Reset Filters to clear all filters and return to the full list.

common_tasks.title: Switch between card and table view
steps: Click the View Toggle in the page header to switch between Cards and Table.
Table view shows a compact list with image count and a View Related link per row.
Cards view shows full detail including images, notes, and related records per product.

warnings: Removing an image is a soft delete — it disappears immediately and cannot be restored from the UI. Contact support if an image was removed by mistake.
The Active and With Images KPI counts reflect the current page only, not all products in the system. Use the total shown in the Pagination Controls for a system-wide count.
Changing a product's status to Archived removes it from active sales flows immediately — sales staff will not be able to select it at the point of sale.
This page does not permanently delete products. Use the Archived status to retire a product from active use.

Product images must be PNG or JPEG. Files are uploaded directly to S3 via a presigned URL — they never pass through the application server. A failed upload will show an error but will not create a broken image record.

entries.title: New Product Wizard
entries.overview: A guided 4-step form for creating a new product with one or more variants, optional barcodes, and optional opening stock — all in a single flow. Step 1 captures product details, Step 2 defines variants (pricing, units, VAT mode, barcode), Step 3 sets initial stock quantities per branch, and Step 4 presents a summary before final submission. All data is held locally until Finish is pressed — nothing is saved to the server until the final step.
before_you_start: You need the Catalog Write permission to use this wizard. Without it you cannot access the create product flow.
At least one category must exist before you can create a product — the category field is required. Create a category first at Catalog › Categories if none exist.
You can move freely between steps 0–2 using Back and Continue without losing your data. Validation only runs when you press Finish on the Review step.

elements.label: Current Step
elements.description: Displays the name of the active wizard step. Updates as you move through the wizard.

elements.label: Variants Drafted
elements.description: Total number of variant rows added, including unnamed ones. Only variants with a non-empty name are submitted.

elements.label: Stock Lines
elements.description: Count of stock line entries — one per variant. Lines with no quantity or no branch are skipped silently at submission.

elements.label: Ready Variants
elements.description: Count of variants that have a non-empty name and will be submitted. If this is 0 when you press Finish, submission is blocked.

elements.label: Step Indicator
elements.description: A horizontal pill bar showing all four steps: Product Details, Variants, Initial Stock, Review. The active step is highlighted. The pills are not clickable — use Back and Continue to navigate.

elements.label: Product Name
elements.description: Required. The name of the product (e.g. 'Mineral Water'). Validated at submission — leaving this blank blocks the Finish button with an error toast.

elements.label: Description
elements.description: Optional. A free-text description of the product. Not required to proceed.

elements.label: Category
elements.description: Required. An async searchable select — type to search existing categories or pick from the pre-loaded list of up to 50. Required at submission; leaving it blank blocks Finish.

elements.label: Variant Name
elements.description: Required per variant. Defines a specific variant of the product (e.g. '500ml', 'Red'). Variants with no name are filtered out at submission and not sent to the server.

elements.label: SKU
elements.description: Optional. A stock-keeping unit code for the variant. Must be unique across the business if provided — the backend enforces uniqueness.

elements.label: Default Price / Min Price / Default Cost
elements.description: Three currency inputs per variant. Default Price is the standard selling price. Min Price is the floor below which the variant cannot be sold (backend rejects if Min Price > Default Price). Default Cost is the purchase cost for margin calculations. All are optional.

elements.label: VAT Mode
elements.description: Per-variant VAT treatment. Options: INCLUSIVE (price includes VAT), EXCLUSIVE (VAT added on top), EXEMPT (no VAT). Defaults to INCLUSIVE.

elements.label: Barcode
elements.description: Optional. Enter a barcode code manually or use Scan & Assign to capture it via camera or USB/Bluetooth scanner. Must be unique across the business — duplicate barcodes are rejected by the backend with a 400 error.

elements.label: Scan & Assign
elements.description: Opens the camera scanner panel for the target variant. Prefers the rear (environment) camera; falls back to the first available camera. Also listens for USB or Bluetooth barcode scanner hardware (minimum 6 characters). On a successful scan the barcode field is filled and the panel closes automatically. Use Start Camera / Restart / Stop Camera buttons within the panel to control the live video feed.

elements.label: Track Stock
elements.description: Checkbox per variant. When checked (default), the variant participates in stock tracking and movements. When unchecked, no stock movements are recorded for this variant.

elements.label: Base Unit / Sell Unit / Conversion Factor
elements.description: Three unit controls per variant. Base Unit is the storage unit (e.g. 'case'). Sell Unit is the unit used in sales (e.g. 'bottle'). When Base = Sell, Conversion Factor is locked at 1. When they differ, a Conversion Factor > 0 is required — the backend enforces this. Both default to 'piece' on load.

elements.label: Add Variant
elements.description: Appends a new blank variant row and a corresponding stock line entry. Use this to add colour, size, or packaging variants of the same product. The Remove button (shown on each row when more than one variant exists) deletes that row and its stock line.

elements.label: Cancel / Back / Continue / Finish
elements.description: Cancel shows a confirmation dialog before discarding all unsaved data and returning to the Products list. Back moves to the previous step. Continue advances to the next step without validation. Finish (Step 4 only) runs validation and submits — shows a spinner and 'Creating…' while the server calls are in flight. On success the wizard resets to Step 1; it does not navigate away.

elements.name: Visual ProgressBar
elements.description: A progress indicator showing your current step: Product Info, Variants, Initial Stock, Review.

elements.name: MetricComparison component
elements.description: In the review step, shows side-by-side comparison of variant prices, costs, and margins.

elements.name: Max variant limit warning
elements.description: Warning shown when approaching the maximum number of variants allowed per product.

elements.name: Duplicate SKU error
elements.description: Error shown when a variant SKU already exists in the system.

elements.name: Initial Stock step (INITIAL_STOCK reason)
elements.description: Set opening stock per variant per branch. Stock added here creates a stock purchase cost expense.

elements.name: Unit Help Panel
elements.description: An expandable 'How do units work?' panel that appears next to the unit selectors. Explains base unit, sell unit, and conversion factor with a concrete example. Includes a tip that if you don't plan on using conversions, leave both units as Piece.

common_tasks.title: Create a simple single-variant product
steps: Step 1: Enter the product name and select a category. Description is optional. Click Continue.
Step 2: Fill in the variant name (e.g. 'Standard'), set a default price, choose VAT mode, and confirm the base and sell units. Leave SKU and barcode blank if not needed. Click Continue.
Step 3: Select the branch, enter the opening quantity, and confirm the unit. Leave quantity blank to skip initial stock. Click Continue.
Step 4: Review the summary. Click Finish. On success the wizard resets to Step 1 and a success toast appears.

common_tasks.title: Create a product with multiple variants (e.g. sizes)
steps: Step 1: Enter the product name and category. Click Continue.
Step 2: Fill in the first variant row. Click Add Variant to add a second row and fill in its details.
Repeat Add Variant for each size or packaging option. Each variant can have its own price, unit, and VAT mode.
Click Continue to proceed to Step 3. Each variant has its own stock line row.
Step 3: Set the opening quantity and branch for each variant. Click Continue, then Finish.

common_tasks.title: Assign a barcode via camera scanner
steps: In Step 2, click Scan & Assign next to the target variant's barcode field.
The scanner panel opens. Click Start Camera — the live video feed appears.
Point the camera at the barcode. On a successful read, the barcode field fills automatically and the panel closes.
If the camera does not open, check browser permissions for camera access. USB and Bluetooth scanners also work — scan the barcode while the panel is open without clicking Start Camera.

common_tasks.title: Create a product without setting opening stock
steps: Complete Steps 1 and 2 as normal.
On Step 3, leave all quantity fields blank.
Click Continue then Finish. The product and variants are created; no stock movements are recorded. You can add stock later via Stock › Adjustments.

common_tasks.title: Set a variant that is bought by the case but sold by the bottle
steps: In Step 2, for the variant, set Base Unit to 'case' and Sell Unit to 'bottle'.
The Conversion Factor field becomes enabled. Enter the number of bottles per case (e.g. 24).
The system stores stock in cases but records sales in bottles, converting automatically using the factor.

warnings: Submission is not atomic — if variant creation succeeds but barcode creation fails (e.g. duplicate barcode), the variant is already saved and must be managed manually. Check the Products list if you see a partial-creation error.
If you close or navigate away from the wizard without pressing Finish, all entered data is lost. The Cancel button shows a confirmation dialog first.
Min Price cannot exceed Default Price — the backend rejects this with a 400 error. Set Min Price lower than or equal to Default Price.
When Base Unit and Sell Unit differ, a Conversion Factor greater than 0 is required. Submitting without one returns a 400 error from the server.

entries.title: Variants
entries.overview: Manage SKU-level records for your products. Each variant holds its own pricing, cost, unit configuration, barcodes, branch availability, and image. This page also handles barcode scanning and reassignment, SKU reassignment, and label printing for both A4 and thermal printers.

before_you_start.text: Parent products must exist before variants can be created — set them up at /catalog/products.
Units (base and sell) must be configured in settings before you can assign them here.
catalog.read is required to browse variants; catalog.write is required for all create, update, barcode, and image operations.
Branch availability management requires at least one branch to be set up in settings.
Camera barcode scanning requires the browser to have permission to access the device camera — approve the permission prompt when it appears.

elements.name: KPI – Variants
elements.description: Total variant count matching the current filter state. This is also the number available for label printing when using Select All. If the count does not match your expected catalogue size, check the Status filter — INACTIVE and ARCHIVED variants are hidden by default.

elements.name: KPI – Active
elements.description: Count of ACTIVE variants in scope. Only ACTIVE variants appear in POS search and stock flows. If a variant is missing from the POS, set the Status filter to ACTIVE and verify it appears here — if not, the variant may be INACTIVE.

elements.name: KPI – With Barcode
elements.description: Number of variants that have at least one active barcode assigned. Variants without barcodes cannot be found by scanning at the POS — use this count to identify how many variants still need a barcode added or printed.

elements.name: KPI – Branches
elements.description: Number of branches loaded into the branch availability panel. Each variant can be marked available or unavailable per branch. If branches are missing here, check that they are active in the Branches settings page.

elements.name: Product Selector (Create form)
elements.description: Required. Chooses the parent product this variant belongs to. Only ACTIVE and INACTIVE products appear in the dropdown. Once a variant is saved, its parent product cannot be changed — if you select the wrong product you will need to delete the variant and create a new one under the correct product.

elements.name: Variant Name (Create form)
elements.description: Required. Display name for the variant as it appears on receipts, the POS product list, and stock reports (e.g. '1 Litre', 'Red – Large', '500ml Blue'). Be specific enough that cashiers can distinguish between variants of the same product at a glance.

elements.name: SKU (Create form)
elements.description: Optional internal stock-keeping unit code used for purchase orders, imports, and inventory tracking. If left blank, the system auto-generates one. Reassigning an SKU after creation requires a written reason and may trigger an approval workflow depending on your business policy.

elements.name: Barcode (Create form)
elements.description: Optional barcode value to assign at creation time. Can be filled by typing the code or by using the Scan Assign New button. Barcodes enable instant product lookup at the POS by scanner — variants without barcodes must be found by name search.

elements.name: Scan Assign New Button (Create form)
elements.description: Opens the device camera and scans a barcode, then automatically fills the Barcode field in the Create form. Use this instead of typing the barcode manually to eliminate transcription errors. Click Stop when done scanning.

elements.name: Default Price (Create form)
elements.description: Standard selling price for this variant in TZS. The POS uses this as the default price when the variant is added to a sale. If a customer price list applies, the price list value overrides this at the point of sale. Leave blank to set the price later.

elements.name: Min Price (Create form)
elements.description: Floor selling price in TZS. If a cashier tries to sell this variant below this price, the POS shows a warning and may require manager approval depending on your pricing rule configuration. Set this to protect against accidental underpricing.

elements.name: Default Cost (Create form)
elements.description: Cost price of this variant in TZS — what the business pays to acquire one unit. Used to calculate gross margin in reports. Does not affect what the customer is charged. If left blank, margin calculations for this variant will show as unavailable in reports.

elements.name: VAT Mode (Create form)
elements.description: Controls how VAT is applied to this variant's price. INCLUSIVE: the displayed price already includes VAT (customer sees the final price). EXCLUSIVE: VAT is calculated and added on top of the price at checkout. EXEMPT: no VAT applies to this variant. This setting affects receipt line items and tax reports.

elements.name: Track Stock (Create form)
elements.description: When enabled, the system records stock movements for this variant and prevents selling below zero stock (subject to business settings). Disable for service items or products where physical stock tracking is not needed — untracked variants can be sold without a stock balance.

elements.name: Base Unit (Create form)
elements.description: The unit in which stock is tracked internally (e.g. Each, Kg, Litre, Box). All stock movements, adjustments, and counts are recorded in this unit. Choose the unit that matches how you physically count and store the item. Cannot be changed after stock movements exist.

elements.name: Sell Unit (Create form)
elements.description: The unit in which this variant is sold to customers (e.g. Pack, Carton, Piece). Can be the same as or different from the Base Unit. If different, set the Conversion Factor to define how many base units make one sell unit.

elements.name: Conversion Factor (Create form)
elements.description: How many base units are equivalent to one sell unit. Required when Base Unit and Sell Unit differ — the field is locked to 1 and disabled when they are the same. For example, if the base unit is Litre and the sell unit is 5-Litre Jerry Can, the conversion factor is 5.

elements.name: Create Variant Button
elements.description: Saves the new variant and adds it to the catalogue. The variant starts with zero stock on hand. After creating, make an opening stock adjustment at /stock or receive a purchase order to add initial stock before the variant appears with a usable balance in the POS.

elements.name: Barcode Reassign – Barcode Selector
elements.description: Selects the existing barcode to move to a different variant. The dropdown shows each barcode value and the variant it currently belongs to. Confirm you have selected the correct barcode before proceeding — this cannot be undone without a second reassignment.

elements.name: Barcode Reassign – New Variant Selector
elements.description: The variant that will receive ownership of the barcode after reassignment. The barcode is unlinked from its current variant and attached here. The original variant will have no barcode until one is manually added or reassigned back.

elements.name: Barcode Reassign – Reason
elements.description: Required. A written explanation for why this barcode is being moved (e.g. 'Old product discontinued — barcode reassigned to replacement variant'). Stored in the audit log alongside the reassignment event. This field cannot be left blank.

elements.name: Submit Reassignment Button
elements.description: Submits the barcode reassignment. If the business policy requires approval, the result panel shows 'Approval required' and the change is held pending a manager review. If no approval is needed, the reassignment takes effect immediately and the barcode list on both affected variant cards updates.

elements.name: Scan Mode Indicator
elements.description: Read-only label showing the current scanner mode: Lookup (scan to find which variant owns a barcode), Assign Existing (scan to fill the reassignment barcode field), or Assign New (scan to fill the new barcode field in the Create form). The mode changes automatically depending on which action you initiated.

elements.name: Scan Message
elements.description: Read-only status text displayed below the camera feed. Shows instructions ('Point camera at barcode'), a success message when a code is detected, or an error message if the code was not recognised. Helps confirm whether a scan was picked up correctly.

elements.name: Scan Result Panel
elements.description: Displays the product name, variant name, and SKU matched to the most recently scanned barcode. Appears after a successful lookup scan. Use this to quickly verify which variant a physical barcode belongs to without opening the variant card.

elements.name: Scan Start / Restart Button
