elements.description: Activates the device camera for barcode scanning. Click Restart if the camera freezes, loses focus, or fails to detect codes after several attempts. The scan session stays open until you click Stop — you can scan multiple codes in one session.

elements.name: Scan Stop Button
elements.description: Ends the active camera scan session and returns the scanner to idle. The last scan result stays visible in the Scan Result Panel. Click Stop when you have finished scanning to free up the camera for other applications.

elements.name: Camera Video Feed
elements.description: Live camera preview displayed while a scan session is active. Centre the barcode within the frame for the fastest detection. Poor lighting or a damaged barcode may prevent detection — try adjusting distance or lighting if scans are failing.

elements.name: Print A4 Labels Button
elements.description: Generates a printable barcode label sheet sized for A4 paper and opens the browser print dialog. Only the variants whose checkboxes are ticked are included in the print job. Use this for desktop printers or when you need full-size labels.

elements.name: Print Thermal Labels Button
elements.description: Generates a barcode label sheet formatted for thermal label printers and opens the print dialog. Labels are sized for standard thermal roll widths. Only ticked variants are included. Use this when printing small adhesive labels for shelves or packaging.

elements.name: Labels Ready Indicator
elements.description: Read-only count showing how many variant labels are queued for the next print job based on current checkbox selections. Check this number matches your expectation before clicking either Print button — if it reads 0, no variants are selected and the print output will be blank.

elements.name: Search Input
elements.description: Filter the variant list by variant name or SKU code. Results update as you type. Use this to quickly locate a specific variant for editing, barcode assignment, image upload, or to verify details before printing labels. Clear the field to return to the full list.

elements.name: Status Filter
elements.description: Filter variants by status. ACTIVE: variants visible in the POS and stock flows. INACTIVE: hidden from the POS but retained in the system. ARCHIVED: soft-deleted, removed from all live flows. Defaults to showing all statuses. If a variant is missing from the POS, set this to ACTIVE to confirm it is correctly enabled.

elements.name: Advanced Filters Toggle
elements.description: Expands or collapses the Branch Filter and Availability Filter controls. Off by default — turn on when you need to filter variants by their availability at a specific branch. Closing the panel does not clear the branch or availability filter values.

elements.name: Reset Filters Button
elements.description: Clears all active filters (search text, status, branch, availability) and returns the list to the default view showing all variants across all branches. Use this when you have applied multiple filters and want to start fresh.

elements.name: Branch Filter
elements.description: Visible only when Advanced Filters is enabled. Narrows the variant list to the availability context of a specific branch. Select a branch before using the Availability Filter to see which variants are or are not available at that location.

elements.name: Availability Filter
elements.description: Visible only when Advanced Filters is enabled. Filter to variants marked Available or Unavailable at the selected branch. Set to Unavailable to identify products blocked from sale at a specific location and decide whether to re-enable them.

elements.name: View Toggle (Cards / Table)
elements.description: Switches the variant list between the card grid view and the table view. Cards show the full management interface per variant (image, barcodes, units, availability). The table view is read-only and suited for comparing pricing, margins, and status across many variants at once.

elements.name: Variant Checkbox (per card)
elements.description: Selects this variant for inclusion in the next label print job. Tick the variants you need labels for, then click Print A4 Labels or Print Thermal Labels. Clear all checkboxes between different print batches to avoid accidentally including unwanted variants.

elements.name: Image Thumbnail (per card)
elements.description: Displays the current product image assigned to this variant. If no image has been uploaded, a placeholder is shown. Click the upload area or the upload button beside it to replace or add an image. Images are stored on S3 and served via CDN.

elements.name: Image Upload Button (per card)
elements.description: Opens a file picker to upload a new image for this variant. Supported formats are JPEG, PNG, and WebP. The image is uploaded to S3 in three steps: the system requests a presigned URL, uploads the file directly to S3, then confirms the URL with the backend. Upload progress is indicated on the card.

elements.name: Track Stock Toggle (per card)
elements.description: Enables or disables stock tracking for this variant inline without opening a separate edit form. When disabled, the variant can be sold regardless of stock balance. Changes take effect immediately and are recorded in the audit log.

elements.name: Status Selector (per card)
elements.description: Changes this variant's status inline: ACTIVE (available in POS and flows), INACTIVE (hidden from POS), or ARCHIVED (soft-deleted). Archiving a variant with open stock should be done with care — it will no longer appear in stock flows or sales. The change is saved immediately on selection.

elements.name: Base Unit Selector (per card)
elements.description: Inline editor for the variant's base stock unit. Changing the base unit after stock movements have been recorded may make historical data inconsistent — only change this if no movements exist or after reconciling your stock records.

elements.name: Sell Unit Selector (per card)
elements.description: Inline editor for the variant's sell unit. Changing the sell unit alters how the POS presents the variant at checkout. If the sell unit differs from the base unit, ensure the Conversion Factor is correct after changing.

elements.name: Conversion Factor Input (per card)
elements.description: Inline editor for the number of base units per sell unit. Active only when the base unit and sell unit differ. An incorrect conversion factor will cause stock to be deducted in the wrong ratio when sales are processed — verify this value carefully when units differ.

elements.name: Barcode Chips List (per card)
elements.description: Read-only display of all barcodes currently assigned to this variant, shown as dismissible chips. Each chip shows the barcode value. Click the × on a chip to remove that barcode from this variant. A variant can have multiple barcodes, all of which resolve to the same variant at the POS.

elements.name: Add Barcode Input (per card)
elements.description: Type a new barcode value to add to this variant. Press Enter or click the add icon to save. Barcodes must be unique across the entire business — if the barcode is already assigned to another variant, the system will reject it with an error message. Use the Scan Assign button to fill this from a camera scan instead.

elements.name: Scan Assign Button (per card)
elements.description: Opens the camera scanner in Assign Existing mode and fills the Add Barcode Input on this card with the scanned code. Use this to avoid typing barcode values manually when adding a new barcode to an existing variant.

elements.name: Generate Barcode Button (per card)
elements.description: Automatically generates a unique barcode value and assigns it to this variant without needing a physical label to scan. Useful when a product does not have a printed barcode — you can then print the generated barcode as a label using the label print buttons.

elements.name: SKU Reassign Input (per card)
elements.description: Allows the SKU of this variant to be changed inline. A written reason is required in a confirmation prompt before the change is saved. The reassignment is logged in the audit trail. If approval workflows are enabled for SKU changes, the new SKU takes effect only after a manager approves.

elements.name: Branch Availability Checkboxes (per card)
elements.description: A row of checkboxes — one per branch — showing whether this variant is available at each branch. Check a branch to make the variant available for sale there; uncheck to hide it from that branch's POS. Changes take effect immediately. Use the Branch Filter and Availability Filter to find variants that need their availability updated across the business.

elements.name: Variants Table (table view)
elements.description: Read-only tabular view of all variants matching the current filters. Columns: product image, product name, variant name, SKU, default price, default cost, gross margin %, VAT mode, status, and track-stock flag. Sort by any column header. Use this view to audit pricing and margins across your catalogue — switch to card view to make edits.
elements.notes: Table view is read-only. To edit a variant, switch back to card view.

elements.name: Pagination Controls
elements.description: Navigate between pages of variants and adjust the number of variants shown per page. The system uses cursor-based pagination — navigating forwards and backwards is fast but jumping directly to a specific page number is not supported. Use the Search or Status filter to narrow results instead of paging through many pages.

elements.name: Create variant modal
elements.description: Opened from the 'Create variant' button in the page header. Centered modal dialog with the full create form (product, name, SKU, barcode, pricing, VAT mode, units, branch availability, track-stock toggle). ESC or backdrop click closes without saving. Closes automatically after a successful save.

elements.name: Edit details button (per card)
elements.description: Each variant card has an 'Advanced' edit button that opens the Variant edit modal. Inside: base unit / sell unit / conversion factor, SKU reassign field, and image upload. Barcodes and branch availability remain inline on the card so day-to-day operations stay quick.

elements.name: Variant edit modal
elements.description: Centered modal dialog for detailed variant edits. Contains the Unit help panel, unit selectors and conversion factor, SKU reassign field (requires a reason prompt, may route to approval), and image upload. Saves happen live per field — there is no Save button. ESC or backdrop click closes.

elements.name: Generate Barcode button
elements.description: Auto-generates a unique barcode for the variant. The generated code is immediately assigned and displayed as a barcode chip on the card.

elements.name: Stock-on-hand display
elements.description: Shows the current stock quantity for each variant, visible in both card and table views. Helps identify which variants need restocking.

elements.name: Low stock indicator
elements.description: A warning icon shown next to variants whose stock is below the alert threshold. Red for critical (≤2 units), amber for low (≤5 units).

elements.name: Gross margin column (table view)
elements.description: Shows the gross margin percentage calculated from price minus cost. Green for positive margins, red for negative. Helps identify unprofitable variants at a glance.

elements.name: Track Stock toggle (per card)
elements.description: An inline toggle on each variant card to enable or disable stock tracking. Changes take effect immediately when toggled.

elements.name: Branch availability checkboxes
elements.description: A grid of checkboxes on each variant card showing which branches the variant is available at. Check or uncheck to control where the variant can be sold.

elements.name: Image upload (per variant)
elements.description: Each variant card has an image upload button. Click to browse for an image file (JPEG, PNG, WebP). The image is displayed as a thumbnail on the card and in the table view.

elements.name: Barcode chips
elements.description: Assigned barcodes are shown as dismissible chips on each variant card. Click the × on a chip to remove that barcode. Multiple barcodes can be assigned to a single variant.

elements.name: StatusBadge
elements.description: Coloured status pills on each variant. Green for ACTIVE, amber for INACTIVE, grey for ARCHIVED. In card view, the status can be changed via an inline dropdown.

elements.name: SortableTableHeader
elements.description: Clickable column headers in table view for sorting. Available columns include Product, Variant, SKU, Price, Cost, Margin, Status.

elements.name: ActionButtons (table view)
elements.description: Icon buttons in the actions column of table view. The edit button opens the Variant edit modal for that row.

elements.name: Unit Help Panel
elements.description: An expandable explanation panel in both the create form and inline card edit sections, explaining how base unit, sell unit, and conversion factor work with examples.

common_tasks.task: Create a new variant
steps: In the Create form, select the parent product from the Product Selector dropdown.
Enter the Variant Name.
Optionally enter SKU, Default Price, Min Price, and Default Cost.
Select VAT Mode and check or uncheck Track Stock.
Select Base Unit and Sell Unit; set Conversion Factor if they differ.
Optionally enter a Barcode or click Scan Assign New to scan one.
Click Create Variant. The card appears in the grid with zero stock on hand.
Go to /stock to make an opening stock adjustment if needed.

common_tasks.task: Create a variant and assign a barcode by scanning
steps: Fill in the Create form fields (product, name, price, units).
Click Scan Assign New — the camera opens in Assign New mode.
Point the camera at the barcode until the code is detected and fills the Barcode field.
Click Stop to close the camera.
Click Create Variant to save.

common_tasks.task: Add a barcode to an existing variant
steps: Find the variant card using the Search Input or filters.
In the Barcode section of the card, either type the barcode in the Add Barcode Input and press Enter, or click Scan Assign to scan it from the camera.
The new barcode appears as a chip on the card immediately.
If the barcode is already assigned to another variant, an error message appears — use the lookup scanner to find which variant currently owns it.

common_tasks.task: Look up which variant a barcode belongs to
steps: Click Scan Start in the scanner panel — the camera opens in Lookup mode.
Point the camera at the barcode.
The Scan Result Panel shows the product name, variant name, and SKU associated with that barcode.
Click Stop when done.

common_tasks.task: Reassign a barcode to a different variant
steps: In the Barcode Reassignment panel, select the barcode to move from the Barcode Selector dropdown.
Select the destination variant in the New Variant Selector.
Enter a written reason in the Reason field.
Click Submit Reassignment.
If the result shows 'Approval required', wait for a manager to approve before the change takes effect.

common_tasks.task: Reassign a variant's SKU
steps: Find the variant card.
In the SKU section of the card, clear the existing SKU and type the new value in the SKU Reassign Input.
Press Enter — a confirmation prompt appears asking for a written reason.
Enter the reason and confirm.
If approval is required, the change is held pending manager review.

common_tasks.task: Print barcode labels
steps: Tick the Variant Checkbox on each card you need labels for. Check the Labels Ready Indicator to confirm the count is correct.
Click Print A4 Labels for a desktop printer or Print Thermal Labels for a thermal label printer.
Complete the browser print dialog.
Clear all checkboxes after printing to reset for the next batch.

common_tasks.task: Upload or replace a variant image
steps: Find the variant card.
Click Image Upload Button on the card.
Select a JPEG, PNG, or WebP file from your device.
The image uploads to S3 automatically — a progress indicator appears on the card.
The Image Thumbnail updates once the upload is confirmed.

common_tasks.task: Update branch availability for a variant
steps: Find the variant card using Search or filters.
In the Branch Availability section of the card, check the branches where this variant should be available and uncheck those where it should not.
Changes take effect immediately — no save button is required.
To review availability across all variants at a specific branch, use the Branch Filter and Availability Filter.

common_tasks.task: Change a variant's status (activate or deactivate)
steps: Find the variant card.
In the Status Selector on the card, choose ACTIVE, INACTIVE, or ARCHIVED.
The change saves immediately. INACTIVE removes the variant from the POS; ARCHIVED soft-deletes it from all flows.
To see INACTIVE or ARCHIVED variants, update the Status Filter above the list.

common_tasks.task: Compare pricing and margins across variants
steps: Apply Search or Status filters to narrow the list to the variants you want to compare.
Click the View Toggle to switch to Table view.
Review the price, cost, and margin % columns. Sort by any column header.
Switch back to Cards view to edit a variant.

common_tasks.task: Filter variants by branch availability
steps: Enable the Advanced Filters Toggle.
Select the target branch from the Branch Filter.
Set the Availability Filter to Unavailable to see which variants are blocked at that branch.
Update Branch Availability Checkboxes on the relevant cards to re-enable any variants that should be available.

warnings: Barcode reassignment may require manager approval — the result panel will indicate if the change is pending review before it takes effect.
SKU reassignment requires a written reason and may trigger an approval workflow. The current SKU remains active until approval is granted.
Archiving a variant with stock on hand removes it from all live flows but does not zero out the stock balance — reconcile stock before archiving.
Changing the Base Unit after stock movements have been recorded will cause historical quantities to be expressed in the wrong unit — only change units when no movements exist.
Label printing includes only checked variants — always verify the Labels Ready Indicator count before printing to avoid blank or incomplete label sheets.

related_pages.reason: Products must be created before variants can be attached to them.
The Product Creation Wizard creates the first variant of a product in a guided flow — use Variants page to manage additional variants.
After creating variants, go here to set opening stock balances.
Categories group products — variants inherit category context through their parent product.
Use stock adjustments to record opening balances or correct discrepancies for newly created variants.

permissions_required: catalog.write (create, update, barcode assign/reassign, image upload, SKU reassign)

entries.title: Customers
entries.overview: Manage your customer directory. Create and maintain customer profiles, assign price lists so the POS applies their negotiated prices automatically, track outstanding credit balances, and archive or anonymise customers when needed. Customers linked at POS also appear in receipt history, enabling credit settlement and return lookups.

before_you_start.text: You need the customers.create permission to add new customers, customers.update to edit or archive, and customers.anonymize to permanently remove personal data.
Price lists must be created first on the Price Lists page before they can be assigned to customers here.

elements.name: KPI — Customers
elements.description: Count of customer records in the current page of results (up to 25). This reflects the current filter set — it is not a business-wide total. To see all customers, clear all filters.

elements.name: KPI — Active
elements.description: Count of customers with ACTIVE status within the current page of results. Only ACTIVE customers appear in POS customer selectors — if a customer is missing at POS, check their status here.

elements.name: KPI — Price list linked
elements.description: Count of customers in the current page who have a price list assigned. These customers automatically get their custom prices applied at POS when they are selected during a sale.

elements.name: KPI — Current filter
elements.description: Shows which status filter is currently active, or 'All statuses' if no filter is set. Use this as a quick reminder of what segment of your customer list you are viewing.

elements.name: Search bar
elements.description: Filter customers by name, phone, email, or TIN as you type. Results update after a short pause (350 ms debounce). Your search term is saved in the page URL so you can share or bookmark filtered views.

elements.name: Status filter
elements.description: Narrow the list to ACTIVE, INACTIVE, or ARCHIVED customers. ARCHIVED customers are hidden from POS selectors but their purchase history and credit records are preserved.

elements.name: Balance due filter
elements.description: Filter to customers with an outstanding credit balance (Yes) or a clear balance (No). Use Yes to identify all customers who currently owe money — useful before making collection calls or running credit settlement sessions.

elements.name: Reset button
elements.description: Clears all active search and filter inputs and reloads the full customer list. Use this to start a fresh search after narrowing results too far.

elements.name: View toggle (Cards / Table)
elements.description: Switch between card layout (shows all details per customer) and table layout (better for scanning many customers at once). Both layouts show the same data.

elements.name: Name field (create form)
elements.description: Required. The customer's display name — shown in POS customer selectors, receipts, and reports. Use the name your staff will recognise them by.

elements.name: Phone field (create form)
elements.description: Optional. The customer's phone number. Stored for contact purposes. Staff without the customers.view_sensitive permission will see this field masked.

elements.name: Email field (create form)
elements.description: Optional. The customer's email address. Staff without the customers.view_sensitive permission will see this field masked.

elements.name: TIN field (create form)
elements.description: Optional. The customer's Tax Identification Number. Required for issuing formal tax invoices to business customers in compliance with local tax regulations. Staff without the customers.view_sensitive permission will see this field masked.

elements.name: Notes field (create form)
elements.description: Optional. Free-text notes about the customer — for example preferred payment method, delivery preferences, or VIP status. Visible to all staff who can view the customer record.

elements.name: Default price list (create form)
elements.description: Optionally link the customer to a price list. When this customer is selected at POS, their linked price list is automatically applied, overriding the branch's default prices. Type to search available price lists. Price lists must be created first on the Price Lists page.

elements.name: Create Customer button
elements.description: Saves the new customer record. The customer is immediately available in POS customer selectors. Requires the customers.create permission.

elements.name: Customer list
elements.description: Displays matching customers as cards or rows (depending on view toggle). Each customer shows their name, status badge, phone, email, TIN, price list, notes, and any outstanding balance. 25 records are loaded at a time.

elements.name: Edit button (per customer)
elements.description: Opens an inline edit form for that customer's name, phone, email, TIN, notes, price list, and status. Save commits changes; Cancel discards them. Requires the customers.update permission.

elements.name: Archive button (per customer)
elements.description: Sets the customer status to ARCHIVED after a confirmation prompt. The customer is removed from POS selectors but all their purchase history and credit records are preserved. Requires the customers.update permission.

elements.name: Anonymise button (per customer)
elements.description: Permanently removes all personal data (name, phone, email, TIN, notes) from the customer record. The transaction history is preserved but is no longer linked to an identifiable person. This action is irreversible. Requires the customers.anonymize permission.

elements.name: Load More button
elements.description: Appends the next 25 customers to the list. If a customer does not appear in the initial results, use Load More or adjust your filters — they may be on a later page.

elements.name: Create customer button (page header)
elements.description: Header action button with UserPlus icon. Opens the Customer create modal. Only shown to users with the customers.create permission.

elements.name: Customer create modal
elements.description: Centered modal with name, phone, email, TIN, notes, and default price list fields. ESC or backdrop click closes without saving. Closes automatically after a successful create.

elements.name: Customer edit modal
elements.description: Centered modal opened from the Pencil action. Same fields as create plus a status selector (ACTIVE / INACTIVE / ARCHIVED). Save commits and refreshes the list; ESC or backdrop cancels.

elements.name: Activity Timeline
elements.description: Click 'View Activity' on a customer card to see their purchase and refund history in chronological order. Each entry shows the date, type (sale or refund), amount, and receipt number. Useful for understanding customer buying patterns.

elements.name: WhatsApp button
elements.description: A quick action button that opens WhatsApp with the customer's phone number pre-filled. Only appears when the customer has a phone number on file.

elements.name: AvatarInitials
elements.description: A circular avatar showing the customer's initials, displayed next to their name in both card and table views. Provides visual identity at a glance.

elements.name: SortableTableHeader
elements.description: Clickable column headers in table view for sorting by Name, Phone, Email, and Status.

elements.name: StatusBadge
elements.description: Coloured status pills on each customer. Green for ACTIVE, amber for INACTIVE, grey for ARCHIVED.

elements.name: Sensitive data warning
elements.description: A gold warning banner at the top of the page reminding users that customer data is sensitive and should be handled carefully.

common_tasks.task: Add a new customer
steps: Fill in the Name (required) and optionally phone, email, TIN, and notes.
If this customer has negotiated prices, select their price list from Default price list.
Click Create Customer. The customer is immediately available at POS.

common_tasks.task: Edit a customer's details or status
steps: Find the customer and click Edit.
Update the relevant fields (name, phone, email, TIN, notes, price list, or status).
Click Save to commit or Cancel to discard.

common_tasks.task: Archive a customer
steps: Find the customer and click Archive.
Confirm the action. The customer is hidden from POS selectors but all history and credit records are kept.
To restore them, click Edit and set the status back to ACTIVE.

common_tasks.task: Anonymise a customer (privacy or legal request)
steps: Find the customer and click Anonymise.
Read the warning carefully — this action is permanent and cannot be undone.
Confirm. All personal data is removed immediately. Transaction history is retained but is no longer personally identifiable.

common_tasks.task: Find customers with outstanding balances
steps: Set the Balance due filter to Yes.
The list shows only customers who currently owe money.
Use this list to run credit settlement sessions or contact customers about outstanding payments.

warnings: The KPI cards at the top count records from the current page (up to 25 matching results), not your entire customer database. For accurate business-wide totals, clear all filters before reading the counts.
Phone, email, and TIN fields are masked for staff without the customers.view_sensitive permission. If sensitive fields appear as asterisks, ask your administrator to grant that permission.
Anonymisation is permanent and cannot be undone. Once personal data is removed it cannot be recovered. Confirm with your manager before using this feature.
There is no permanent delete for customers — use Archive to hide them from POS, or Anonymise to remove personal data while preserving transaction history.

related_pages.reason: Create price lists here before assigning them to customers.
View and settle credit sales linked to specific customers.
Select customers during a sale to apply their price list and track credit.

entries.title: Expenses

related_pages.reason: TRANSFER_FEE expenses are auto-created when a transfer is approved — review transfers to understand fee entries.
Branches must exist before you can record expenses against them.
For business-wide expense totals across all pages and periods — the Expenses page only totals the current page.

entries.overview: Record and track business expenses that are not goods purchases — such as rent, utilities, payroll, bank fees, and shipping costs. Expenses are categorised and linked to a branch for cost tracking. Transfer-fee expenses (TRANSFER_FEE) are created automatically when a stock transfer is approved — you do not need to create these manually. Expenses cannot be edited or deleted after saving, and creating expenses requires an internet connection.

elements.name: KPI — Rows on this page
elements.description: Shows how many expense records are loaded on the current page. This reflects the page size, not the total number of expenses in the business.

elements.name: KPI — Total amount this page
elements.description: Sums the amounts of all expense records currently visible on this page. This is not a business-wide total — if you need the full total, use the Reports page or export the filtered list.

elements.name: KPI — Active category
elements.description: Displays the currently selected category filter label. Shows 'All' when no category filter is active.

elements.name: KPI — Branch scope
elements.description: Displays the active branch filter. Automatically reflects your branch scope if you are restricted to specific branches.

elements.name: Search bar
elements.description: Filter expenses by keyword. Searches note text, receipt reference, and branch name. Updates the list after a short pause as you type (350ms debounce). Does not search by amount or date — use the date range filters for date-based lookups.

elements.name: Branch filter
elements.description: Restrict the expense list to a specific branch. Automatically pre-filled with your active branch scope if you are limited to specific branches. Select 'All' to see expenses across all branches you have access to.

elements.name: Category filter
elements.description: Filter the list to a single expense category — GENERAL, TRANSFER_FEE, SHIPPING, UTILITIES, RENT, PAYROLL, or OTHER. Use this when you need to total a specific cost type for a reporting period.

elements.name: Status filter
elements.description: Filter by how the expense was created. 'Transfer' shows only expenses generated automatically by the transfers workflow (TRANSFER_FEE entries created when a transfer is approved). 'Direct' shows only expenses you created manually. Leave blank to see both.

elements.name: From date
elements.description: Start of the date range filter. Filters on the expense date (the date the expense was incurred, not when it was recorded). Part of the Advanced filter panel — click the Advanced toggle to reveal it.

elements.name: To date
elements.description: End of the date range filter. Use together with From date to restrict results to a specific period — for example a calendar month or financial quarter.

elements.name: Reset button
elements.description: Clears all active filters (search, branch, category, status, date range) and reloads the full expense list.

elements.name: Advanced toggle
elements.description: Reveals or hides the date range and status filters. Click to expand if you need to filter by date period or by transfer vs direct expenses.

elements.name: Branch selector (create form)
elements.description: Choose which branch this expense belongs to. Auto-fills from your active branch scope. You must have access to the selected branch. Required — every expense must be linked to a branch.

elements.name: Category selector (create form)
elements.description: Choose the expense category. Options: GENERAL (default), TRANSFER_FEE, SHIPPING, UTILITIES, RENT, PAYROLL, OTHER. Do not use TRANSFER_FEE for manually recorded expenses — these are created automatically by the transfers workflow.

elements.name: Amount input
elements.description: The full amount of the expense. Required — must be greater than zero. For installment payments, record each payment as a separate expense entry.

elements.name: Currency input
elements.description: The currency the expense was paid in. Auto-fills from your business locale settings. Must be a valid ISO currency code (e.g. TZS, USD, EUR). The system stores the amount as-entered without currency conversion.

elements.name: Expense date picker
elements.description: The date the expense was incurred. Defaults to today. Adjust this if recording a past expense — the date determines which reporting period the expense falls into.

elements.name: Receipt reference input
elements.description: Optional reference number from a physical receipt or supplier invoice. Helps link the system record to a paper document for audit and reconciliation. Searchable from the search bar.

elements.name: Note input
elements.description: Optional free-text description of what the expense was for. Be specific — for example 'April rent – Main Street branch' or 'Electricity bill ref INV-2024-04'. Appears in the expense card and is searchable.

elements.name: Create Expense button
elements.description: Saves the new expense record. Requires the expenses.write permission and an active internet connection — creating expenses is not available in offline mode. If your business has expense approval thresholds configured, the submission may be intercepted and routed for approval instead of being saved immediately.

elements.name: Expense card
elements.description: Displays a single expense record showing the branch name, category, expense date, amount with currency, note, and receipt reference. There are no edit or delete actions — expenses are permanent once created.

elements.name: Pagination controls
elements.description: Navigate between pages of expense records. Includes a page size selector (default 30). The total amount KPI only sums the current page — apply filters before paginating for meaningful subtotals.

elements.name: "Create expense" button (header)
elements.description: An accent-colored button in the page header opens a modal for creating an expense. The modal has three grouped sections: Details (branch, category, title), Financial (amount, expense date, receipt ref), and Notes (free-text note). Previously this was an inline collapsible-section form on the page — it has been moved into a modal to reduce page clutter.

elements.name: Status filter (transfer/direct)
elements.description: Filter expenses by source: 'transfer' shows auto-created transfer fee expenses, 'direct' shows manually created expenses. Helps separate operational costs from transfer fees.

elements.name: SortableTableHeader
elements.description: Clickable column headers for sorting by Date, Branch, Category, and Amount.

elements.name: STOCK_COST category
elements.description: A new expense category for stock purchase costs. Created automatically when stock is added via adjustments with purchase-type reasons (Unrecorded Purchase, Initial Stock) or opening stock imports.

elements.name: Reference number
elements.description: Each expense has an auto-generated reference number (e.g. EXP-001). Shown under the branch and category so you can refer to a specific expense.

before_you_start.text: You need expenses.read to view expenses and expenses.write to create them.
At least one branch must exist before you can record an expense — create branches in Settings > Branches.

common_tasks.task: Record a direct expense (e.g. monthly rent)
steps: Select the branch the expense belongs to.
Choose the appropriate category (e.g. RENT).
Enter the amount and confirm the currency.
Set the expense date to the date the expense was incurred.
Optionally add a receipt reference and a note.
Click Create Expense.

common_tasks.task: Filter expenses by category
steps: Open the Category filter and select the category you want (e.g. UTILITIES).
The list updates to show only expenses in that category.
The Total amount KPI shows the sum for the current page of filtered results.

common_tasks.task: View transfer-fee expenses
steps: Click the Advanced toggle to expand the extra filters.
Set Status filter to 'Transfer'.
The list shows only TRANSFER_FEE expenses auto-created by the transfers workflow.
To trace a specific fee back to its transfer, check the note — it is auto-populated with the transfer reference.

common_tasks.task: Filter expenses by date range
steps: Click the Advanced toggle to expand the date filters.
Set the From date to the start of your reporting period.
Set the To date to the end of your reporting period.
Combine with Category or Branch filters for a more targeted result.

common_tasks.task: Find expenses for a specific branch
steps: Open the Branch filter and select the branch.
Optionally set a date range and category to further narrow results.
The Total amount KPI reflects the current page total — paginate through all pages or use Reports for a true branch total.

warnings: The Total amount KPI sums only the expenses visible on the current page — it is not a business-wide total. For a full total across all pages and periods, use the Reports page.

TRANSFER_FEE expenses are created automatically when a stock transfer is approved. Do not record these manually — you will end up with duplicate fee entries. Use the Status filter set to 'Transfer' to see system-generated fee expenses.

If your business has expense approval thresholds configured, clicking Create Expense may route the record for manager approval instead of saving it immediately. You will see a warning message and no expense will appear in the list until the approval is granted.
Creating expenses requires an active internet connection. The Create Expense button is blocked in offline mode — note down the expense details and record them when back online.

entries.title: Exports & Imports
entries.overview: Create bulk data export jobs (CSV or ZIP files) for use in spreadsheets, accounting systems, or external reporting tools. Also supports CSV bulk imports for categories, products, opening stock, price updates, status changes, suppliers, branches, users, customers, units, and stock counts. Import preview runs automatically when you upload a CSV or paste content — results appear in a detailed panel showing valid/invalid row counts and a scrollable error table with a copy-to-clipboard button. Exports run as background jobs and are available for download once complete.

elements.name: Export type selector
elements.description: Choose what data to export. STOCK — current stock levels by variant and branch. PRODUCTS — full product and variant catalogue. OPENING_STOCK — opening stock movement records. PRICE_UPDATES — price change history. SUPPLIERS — full supplier directory. BRANCHES — branch list with details. CUSTOMERS — customer directory. RECEIPTS — sales receipt records. PURCHASES — purchase records. AUDIT_LOGS — full system audit trail (requires acknowledgement checkbox). USERS — user list. ROLES — role and permission definitions (inside the modal).

elements.name: Branch selector (export)
elements.description: Optionally restrict the export to a specific branch. Leave blank to export data across all branches. Stock and receipt exports are especially useful filtered by branch for branch-level reviews (inside the modal).

elements.name: Audit acknowledgement checkbox
elements.description: Required before you can create an AUDIT_LOGS export. By ticking this, you confirm that you understand the compliance implications of exporting a full audit trail, which contains sensitive operational history (inside the modal).

elements.name: "Create export" button (header) + modal
elements.description: An accent-colored button in the page header opens a modal for requesting a new export job. The modal contains type selector (Stock / Products / Audit Logs / Full Data Dump, etc.), format selector (CSV / Excel / PDF), branch selector (hidden for EXPORT_ON_EXIT type), and an audit acknowledgement checkbox (shown only for AUDIT_LOGS type). Previously this was an inline card on the page — moved into a modal to reduce clutter. Submission queues an async background job that the worker picks up and runs; results appear in the Export Jobs list below.

elements.name: Import type selector
elements.description: Choose what type of data to import. Categories — bulk-create product categories. Products — bulk-create products and variants. Opening stock — set initial stock quantities for existing variants. Price updates — bulk-update variant prices. Status updates — change the status of existing records in bulk.

elements.name: CSV textarea (import)
elements.description: Paste your CSV content here. The CSV must match the expected format for the chosen import type — download a template or check the documentation for the required column headers and format. Incorrect headers or formatting will cause validation errors.

elements.name: Preview button
elements.description: Validates your CSV content and shows a preview before importing. Displays the number of valid rows, invalid rows, and detailed error messages with row numbers for any problems. Fix all errors before clicking Apply.

elements.name: Preview table
elements.description: Shows the first rows of valid data and a list of errors with row numbers. Read each error carefully — common issues include missing required columns, duplicate SKUs, or unrecognised category names. Fix the CSV and re-paste it before previewing again.

elements.name: Apply button
elements.description: Imports all validated rows from the CSV. This is irreversible — records are created or updated immediately. Only click Apply after the Preview shows zero errors and the data looks correct.

elements.name: Search input (job history)
elements.description: Filter the export job history by keyword, matching against the export name, type, and description. Useful when looking for a specific export you ran previously among many job records.

elements.name: Status filter (job history)
elements.description: Filter export jobs by status. PENDING — queued but not yet running. RUNNING — currently being processed. COMPLETED — finished and ready to download. FAILED — encountered an error; check the worker status panel and retry.

elements.name: Type filter (job history)
elements.description: Narrows the job list to a specific export type (e.g. STOCK, SALES, CUSTOMERS, AUDIT). Use this when you need to find all exports of one kind — for example, every stock snapshot exported this quarter.

elements.name: Branch filter (job list)
elements.description: Narrows the job list to exports that were scoped to a specific branch. Use this when reviewing export history for a particular location or confirming that a branch-level report was generated.

elements.name: Date range (From / To)
elements.description: Limits the job history to exports created within the selected date window. Set this to find exports from a specific reporting period without scrolling through the full history.

elements.name: Download button (per job)
elements.description: Downloads the completed export file (CSV or ZIP) for that job. Only available once the job status is COMPLETED.

elements.name: ViewToggle
elements.description: Switch between card layout (more detail per job including status badge, file size, and download link) and table layout (more compact, better for scanning many jobs at once).

elements.name: PaginationControls
elements.description: Numbered page navigation for the export job history list. Each page loads 25 job records.

elements.name: Worker status panel
elements.description: Shows the current state of the export processing queue: how many jobs are pending, how many are running, and how many have failed. If jobs are stuck in PENDING for a long time, or if FAILED count is rising, the export worker may need to be restarted — contact support.

elements.name: Import Results Panel
elements.description: Appears after a preview completes. Shows four stat cards (total rows, valid, invalid, errors), a progress bar, and a scrollable table listing every error by row number and issue description. Stays visible until dismissed — does not disappear like a toast notification.

elements.name: Copy Errors button
elements.description: Copies all import errors to your clipboard as text (Row X: message format). Useful for pasting into a spreadsheet or sharing with someone who prepared the CSV file.

elements.name: Back to Edit button
elements.description: Clears the preview results and returns to the CSV editor so you can fix issues and re-upload.

elements.name: Import description panel
elements.description: A detailed explanation panel that appears below the import type selector. Explains what the import does, how the template works, how multi-variant products appear, what happens when applied, and practical tips. Updates automatically when the import type changes.

elements.name: Pre-filled templates
elements.description: For Opening Stock, Price Updates, Status Updates, and Stock Counts, the Download Template button fetches your actual data pre-filled with product names, variant names, SKUs, and current values. You modify what you need and re-upload. Other import types download a blank template with headers only.

elements.name: Change detection
elements.description: For Price Updates, Status Updates, Categories, Suppliers, Customers, and Users imports, unchanged rows are automatically skipped. Only rows where values actually changed are processed. The summary shows how many rows were changed vs unchanged.

before_you_start.text: You need the exports.manage permission to create exports or run imports.
Large exports run asynchronously — the job appears in the history list below and can take several minutes before the Download button becomes available.
For imports, prepare your CSV carefully and match the exact column headers for the import type. Use the Preview step to catch errors before committing.

common_tasks.task: Export a stock snapshot
steps: Set Export type to STOCK.
Optionally select a branch to restrict the export to one location.
Click Create Export. The job appears in the history list below.
Wait for the status to change to COMPLETED, then click Download.

common_tasks.task: Import products from a CSV file
steps: Set Import type to Products.
Paste your prepared CSV into the CSV textarea.
Click Preview. Review the validation results — fix any errors in your CSV and re-paste if needed.
When Preview shows zero errors, click Apply to import all valid rows.

common_tasks.task: Download a previous export
steps: In the export job history, set Status filter to COMPLETED.
Find the export you want using the Type filter or Date range.
Click Download to save the CSV or ZIP file.

warnings: Apply (import) is irreversible — imported records are created or updated immediately. Preview carefully and ensure zero errors before clicking Apply.
AUDIT_LOGS export requires the acknowledgement checkbox to be ticked — this is a compliance safeguard. The exported file contains sensitive operational history.
If export jobs are stuck in PENDING status or the Failed count is rising in the worker status panel, the export processing worker may be down. Contact support to investigate.

entries.title: Accept Invitation
entries.overview: Use this page if you have been invited to join a business by an owner or manager. The invitation link sent to your email will take you here automatically with the token pre-filled. Enter your name and set a password to create your account.

before_you_start.text: You must have received an invitation email from a business owner or manager.
If you are registering a new business (not joining one), use the Sign Up page instead.

elements.name: Token field
elements.description: The invitation token from your email. If you arrived via the invitation link, this is pre-filled and cannot be edited. If you are entering the token manually, paste it from the email.

elements.name: Full Name field
elements.description: Enter your full name. This is how you will appear to other users in the system.

elements.name: Password field
elements.description: Set a password for your new account. Must meet the minimum requirements shown below.

elements.name: Show / Hide Password button
elements.description: Toggles whether your password is visible or hidden as you type.

elements.name: Create Account button
elements.description: Submits the invitation token, your name, and your password to create your account. If successful, you will be redirected to Login after a moment.

elements.name: Token read-only state
elements.description: When the invitation token is provided in the URL, the token field is pre-filled and read-only.

elements.name: Password requirements hint
elements.description: Shows the minimum password requirements below the password field.

elements.name: Auto-redirect on success
elements.description: After successful invitation acceptance, you are automatically redirected to the login page after a brief delay.

common_tasks.task: Accept an invitation and create your account
steps: Click the invitation link in the email you received.
Confirm the token field is filled (it should be automatic).
Enter your full name.
Set a password that meets the requirements.
Click Create Account.
You will be redirected to Login — sign in with the email the invitation was sent to and your new password.

warnings: Invitation tokens expire after a set period. If your token has expired, ask the business owner to send a new invitation.
The email you use to log in after this step is the email the invitation was sent to — not one you enter on this page.
You cannot use this page to join a business if you already have an account. Contact the business owner to add you from Settings.

related_pages.reason: After creating your account, log in here.

entries.title: Login
entries.overview: This is the sign-in page. Enter your email and password to access your business account. If your account is linked to more than one business, you will be asked to choose which business to open.

before_you_start.text: You need a registered account. If you don't have one, sign up first.
If you were invited by a business, use the Accept Invite page instead.

elements.name: Email field
elements.description: Enter the email address you registered with.

elements.name: Password field
elements.description: Enter your account password.

elements.name: Show / Hide Password button
elements.description: Toggles whether your password is visible or hidden as you type.

elements.name: Forgot password link
elements.description: Takes you to the Password Reset page where you can request a reset link by email.

elements.name: Sign In button
elements.description: Submits your email and password. If correct, you are taken directly to the Dashboard.

elements.name: Business selector
elements.description: Appears only if your email is linked to more than one business. Choose which business you want to open.
elements.notes: The system remembers the last business you used and pre-selects it for you.

elements.name: Continue button
elements.description: Appears after the business selector. Confirms your business choice and completes the login.

elements.name: Create Business link
elements.description: Takes you to the Sign Up page if you want to register a new business account.

elements.name: Two-stage login flow
elements.description: Login has two stages: first enter email and password, then select which business to log into if you belong to multiple businesses.

elements.name: Suspended business indicator
elements.description: In the business selector, suspended businesses show a SUSPENDED label and are disabled — you cannot log into a suspended business.

elements.name: Last business memory
elements.description: The system remembers which business you last logged into and pre-selects it in the dropdown.

elements.name: Return-to redirect
elements.description: If you were redirected to login from another page, you will be sent back to that page after successful login.

common_tasks.task: Log in to your account
steps: Enter your email address.
Enter your password.
Click Sign In.
If prompted, select your business and click Continue.

warnings: If you enter the wrong password too many times, your account may be temporarily locked.
If you belong to multiple businesses, you must select one to proceed — you cannot be logged into more than one business at the same time.

related_pages.reason: Email verification is required before you can log in for the first time.
First-time login redirects here to complete business setup.
You land here after a successful login.

entries.title: Notes
entries.overview: Create, manage, and search freeform notes across your business. Notes can be scoped to a branch or kept private, tagged for retrieval, and linked directly to specific records such as products, customers, suppliers, and orders. Each note supports scheduled reminders delivered via in-app notification, email, or WhatsApp depending on your subscription plan.

before_you_start.text: notes.write is required to create notes, edit your own notes, and schedule reminders.
notes.manage is required to archive notes authored by other users.
Notes can be linked to: Product, Variant, Branch, Supplier, Customer, Purchase Order, Purchase, or Transfer records.
EMAIL and WHATSAPP reminder channels require your subscription plan to include them — those checkboxes will appear disabled if not available on your plan.
BUSINESS visibility notes are visible to all users in the business regardless of their active branch scope.

elements.name: KPI – Total Notes
elements.description: Count of notes currently loaded in the list based on active filters and the current page. This reflects the number on screen, not the total across all pages. Check the Total Count display at the bottom of the page for the full count matching your filters.

elements.name: KPI – Active Notes
elements.description: Count of notes with ACTIVE status in the current loaded set. Archived notes are not included in this count. If you expect more active notes, try clearing filters or loading more pages.

elements.name: KPI – With Links
elements.description: Count of notes in the current set that have at least one linked record attached. Use this to gauge how many notes are connected to specific business records. Notes without links are general-purpose and findable only by search or tag.

elements.name: KPI – Branch Scope
elements.description: Shows the currently active branch context for this session. If a branch is active, the list defaults to filtering notes for that branch. Shows 'All' when no branch scope is active. This is informational — use the Branch Filter to explicitly override it.

elements.name: Title Input
elements.description: Required. The headline for this note as it appears in the card header and the table view. Keep titles descriptive enough to identify the note at a glance without reading the body (e.g. 'Supplier X credit terms — March 2026' rather than 'Note 1'). The Save button is disabled if this field is empty.

elements.name: Visibility Selector
elements.description: Controls who can see this note. PRIVATE: only the author can see it — not even managers with notes.manage can read private notes. BRANCH: visible to all users operating in the selected branch. BUSINESS: visible to all users in the business regardless of branch. Choosing BRANCH activates the Branch Selector on the form.

elements.name: Body Textarea
elements.description: Required. The full content of the note. Supports plain text only — no formatting. Use this for instructions, observations, follow-up actions, or any freeform information you want to attach to a context. The Save button is disabled if this field is empty.

elements.name: Branch Selector (form)
elements.description: Links the note to a specific branch when visibility is set to BRANCH. Disabled and ignored when visibility is PRIVATE or BUSINESS. Select the branch this note applies to before saving — a BRANCH-visibility note without a branch selected may not filter correctly from the Branch Filter.

elements.name: Tags Input
elements.description: Optional comma-separated tags to categorise this note (e.g. 'stock, urgent, Q1'). Tags are parsed on save — spaces around commas are trimmed automatically. Use consistent tag names across notes so the Tag Filter can group related notes reliably. Tags are displayed as hashtag chips on the note card.

elements.name: Link Type Selector
elements.description: Selects the type of record to link to this note. Options: Product, Variant, Branch, Supplier, Customer, Purchase Order, Purchase, Transfer. Once selected, the Link Search Input searches records of this type. Changing the link type clears the current search query and results.

elements.name: Link Search Input
elements.description: Typeahead search for records of the chosen link type. Type part of the record name, ID, or reference and matching records appear as suggestions. For Variants the result shows 'Product · Variant name'; for Transfers it shows source branch, destination branch, and status. Select a suggestion or click Add Link to attach the top result.

elements.name: Add Link Button
elements.description: Attaches the top result from the Link Search to this note as a linked record chip. If no results are available or the search is still loading, the button is disabled. Duplicate links (same type and record ID) are silently ignored — the same record cannot be linked twice to the same note.

elements.name: Linked Records Chips
elements.description: Displays all records currently linked to this note as removable chips. Each chip shows the record name or a formatted identifier. Click a chip to remove that link from the note. Links are saved when you click Save / Create Note — removing a chip before saving discards that link without affecting the actual record.

elements.name: Clear Edit Button
elements.description: Appears only when an existing note is loaded into the form for editing. Resets the form back to create mode and discards all unsaved changes to the note. Does not delete or archive the note — it simply cancels the edit session and returns the form to a blank state.

elements.name: Save / Create Note Button
elements.description: Creates a new note (POST /notes) when the form is in create mode, or saves changes to the loaded note (PUT /notes/:id) when in edit mode. Disabled if Title or Body is empty, or if you lack notes.write permission. The button label changes between 'Create note' and 'Update note' to reflect the current mode.

elements.name: Search Input (filters)
elements.description: Filters the note list by title or body text. Results update automatically after a short pause as you type (debounced at 350 ms). Use this to find notes containing a specific word or phrase across both their title and body. Clear the field to return to the unfiltered list.

elements.name: Tag Filter Input
elements.description: Filters notes to only those that include the typed tag. Debounced at 350 ms — results update shortly after you stop typing. Tag matching is exact — if a note uses 'urgent' and you type 'urg', it will not match. Use the same tag names consistently when creating notes to make this filter reliable.

elements.name: Visibility Filter
elements.description: Filters the note list by visibility level: All (default), PRIVATE, BRANCH, or BUSINESS. Use PRIVATE to see only your own private notes. Use BUSINESS to see all notes shared with the whole organisation. Combine with the Branch Filter to narrow to branch-specific notes.

elements.name: Branch Filter
elements.description: Filters notes to those associated with a specific branch. Defaults to your active branch scope at page load. Set to 'All' to see notes from all branches. Combine with Visibility Filter set to BRANCH to audit which notes are scoped to a particular location.

elements.name: View Toggle (Cards / Table)
elements.description: Switches the note list between the card grid view and the table view. Card view shows the full note content, tags, links, and reminder controls. Table view is read-only and shows a compact summary (title, visibility, branch, tags, link count, status) suited for scanning many notes quickly.

elements.name: Note Header Display
elements.description: Read-only header section of each note card showing the note title, visibility level, and branch name (if branch-scoped). This is the identifying summary for the note at a glance. Click the Edit button beside it to load the note into the form for editing.

elements.name: Edit Button (per card)
elements.description: Loads this note's content into the Create / Edit form at the top of the page and switches the form to edit mode. Automatically switches from table view to card view if table view is active. Scroll up to the form after clicking — the form is positioned above the note list.

elements.name: Archive Note Button
elements.description: Triggers a confirmation dialog before archiving. On confirm, marks the note as ARCHIVED via POST /notes/:id/archive. Archived notes disappear from the list immediately and cannot be retrieved from the UI — there is no unarchive or filter for ARCHIVED status. Requires notes.manage permission to archive another user's note; the author can archive their own.

elements.name: Note Body Display
elements.description: Read-only display of the note's full body text on the card. Plain text only. To edit the body, click the Edit button to load the note into the form. Long bodies are displayed in full — there is no truncation in card view.

elements.name: Tag Chips Display
elements.description: Read-only display of all tags assigned to this note, shown as hashtag chips (e.g. #stock, #urgent). Only visible if the note has at least one tag. To edit tags, load the note into the form via the Edit button and update the Tags Input.

elements.name: Links Display (per card)
elements.description: Read-only list of all records linked to this note, showing the record name or reference. Shows 'No links' if none are attached. To add or remove links, load the note into the form via the Edit button. Links are navigational references only — clicking a link name does not navigate to the linked record.

elements.name: View Reminders Button
elements.description: Fetches and displays the reminder list for this note. Click this to see all reminders that have been scheduled, sent, failed, or cancelled for this note. The reminder list appears below this button within the note card. Reminders are not loaded automatically on page load — click this button to reveal them.

elements.name: Add Reminder Toggle
elements.description: Expands or collapses the reminder scheduling form within the note card. Shows '+ Add reminder' when collapsed and '−' when expanded. The form stays expanded until you collapse it or navigate away. Requires notes.write permission to see this control.

elements.name: Reminder Date/Time Picker
elements.description: Sets the date and time when the reminder will fire. Required — the Schedule Reminder button is disabled if this is empty. Use a future date and time; past-dated reminders will be rejected by the backend. The picker uses your business's configured timezone.

elements.name: Reminder Channel Checkboxes
elements.description: Selects which channels the reminder is sent through: IN_APP (always available), EMAIL, and WHATSAPP. EMAIL and WHATSAPP are disabled if your subscription plan does not include them. At least one channel must be checked — the Schedule Reminder button is disabled if all channels are unchecked.

elements.name: Schedule Reminder Button
elements.description: Creates the reminder via POST /notes/:id/reminders with the selected date/time and channels. Disabled if the date/time is empty, no channels are selected, or you lack notes.write permission. After scheduling, the reminder list refreshes automatically to show the new SCHEDULED entry.

elements.name: Reminder List Display
elements.description: Shows all reminders for this note after clicking View Reminders. Each row displays the channel (IN_APP / EMAIL / WHATSAPP), status (SCHEDULED / SENT / FAILED / CANCELLED), and the scheduled date/time formatted in your business timezone. A Cancel button appears next to each SCHEDULED reminder.

elements.name: Cancel Reminder Button
elements.description: Cancels a SCHEDULED reminder via POST /notes/reminders/:id/cancel. Only visible on reminders in SCHEDULED status — SENT, FAILED, and CANCELLED reminders cannot be cancelled. After cancellation the reminder status updates to CANCELLED in the list.

elements.name: Notes Table
elements.description: Read-only table view of notes matching current filters. Columns: title, visibility, branch, tags, number of linked records, status. Each row has a View button that loads the note into the edit form and switches back to card view. Use this view to scan titles, statuses, and link counts across many notes without expanding individual cards.
elements.notes: Table view is read-only. All edits must be made through the form in card view.

elements.name: Load More Button
elements.description: Appends the next page of notes to the list using cursor-based pagination. Appears only when more notes exist beyond the currently loaded set. If filters are active, Load More returns the next page of filtered results — clear filters first if you need to browse the full note corpus.

elements.name: Total Count Display
elements.description: Read-only text showing the total number of notes matching the current filters across all pages. Appears after the first page loads. If filters are active this reflects the filtered total, not the business-wide note count. Clear all filters to see the full note count.

elements.name: CollapsibleSection (New Note form)
elements.description: The create form is hidden behind a 'New Note' button. Closes after successful creation.

elements.name: Rich text editor
elements.description: The note body uses a Tiptap rich text editor with a toolbar for Bold, Italic, Bullet List, and Heading formatting. Supports markdown shortcuts.

elements.name: Body preview toggle
elements.description: Toggle between editing mode and preview mode. Preview shows the formatted output of your note content including headings, lists, and bold/italic text.

elements.name: Note templates
elements.description: Save a note as a template for reuse. Apply a saved template to pre-fill the note form. Delete templates you no longer need. Useful for recurring note types like shift handovers or stock check reports.

elements.name: Reminder system
elements.description: Schedule reminders on any note with a date, time, and delivery channel: In-App, Email, or WhatsApp. Active reminders show an indicator on the note card. Cancel reminders individually.

elements.name: Note sharing
elements.description: Share a note with specific users. Shared users can view the note. Unshare to revoke access. The sharing list shows who currently has access.

elements.name: Pin/unpin notes
elements.description: Pin important notes to keep them at the top of the list. Pinned notes show a pin indicator. Unpin when they are no longer priority.

elements.name: Edit mode for existing notes
elements.description: Click Edit on any note card to load it into the form for editing. The form switches to edit mode with a 'Clear Edit' button to cancel.

elements.name: Markdown preview
elements.description: When preview mode is active, the note body renders formatted markdown/HTML content instead of raw text.

common_tasks.task: Create a new business-wide note
steps: In the Create / Edit form, enter a descriptive Title.
Set Visibility to BUSINESS.
Enter the note content in the Body Textarea.
Optionally add comma-separated tags in the Tags Input.
Click Create Note. The new note appears at the top of the card list.

common_tasks.task: Create a private note
steps: Enter a Title and Body.
Set Visibility to PRIVATE.
Click Create Note.
The note is visible only to you — other users including managers cannot see it.

common_tasks.task: Create a branch-scoped note
steps: Enter a Title and Body.
Set Visibility to BRANCH — the Branch Selector activates.
Select the target branch from the Branch Selector.
Click Create Note. The note is visible to all users whose active branch matches the selected branch.

common_tasks.task: Link a note to a record
steps: While creating or editing a note, select the record type from the Link Type Selector (e.g. Customer).
Type part of the record name in the Link Search Input — matching records appear as suggestions.
Click a suggestion to add it as a link chip, or click Add Link to attach the top result.
Repeat for additional records if needed.
Click Save / Create Note to persist the links.

common_tasks.task: Edit an existing note
steps: Find the note card in the list using Search or filters.
Click the Edit button on the note card.
The note loads into the form at the top of the page — scroll up if needed.
Update the Title, Body, Visibility, Branch, Tags, or Links as required.
Click Update Note to save. The card in the list refreshes with the changes.

common_tasks.task: Archive a note
steps: Find the note card.
Click Archive Note on the card.
Read the confirmation dialog and confirm.
The note is archived immediately and disappears from the list.
Warning: archived notes cannot be retrieved from the UI — confirm you no longer need the note before archiving.

common_tasks.task: Schedule a reminder on a note
steps: Find the note card and click Add Reminder to expand the reminder form.
Select the date and time in the Reminder Date/Time Picker.
Check the channels you want the reminder sent through (IN_APP, EMAIL, WHATSAPP).
Click Add Reminder / Schedule.
Click View Reminders to confirm the reminder appears in SCHEDULED status.

common_tasks.task: Cancel a scheduled reminder
steps: Find the note card and click View Reminders to load the reminder list.
Find the reminder with SCHEDULED status.
Click the Cancel button on that row.
The reminder status updates to CANCELLED immediately.

common_tasks.task: Filter notes by tag
steps: In the filter panel, type the exact tag name in the Tag Filter Input.
Wait for the list to update (debounced — about 350 ms after you stop typing).
Notes with that tag are shown. Clear the tag field to return to the full list.
Tip: tag names are case-sensitive — use the same capitalisation you used when creating the notes.

common_tasks.task: Filter notes by visibility or branch
steps: In the filter panel, select the desired option from the Visibility Filter (e.g. BRANCH).
Optionally also select a specific branch from the Branch Filter.
The list updates to show only notes matching both criteria.
Click the dropdowns back to 'All' to clear those filters.

common_tasks.task: Use table view to scan notes
steps: Click the View Toggle to switch to Table view.
Review the title, visibility, branch, tags, and link count columns across all loaded notes.
Click the View button on any row to load that note into the edit form and return to card view.
Use Search or filters to narrow results before switching to table view for a large corpus.

warnings: Archived notes disappear from the list permanently — there is no unarchive action and no filter to reveal ARCHIVED notes in the UI. Confirm you no longer need a note before archiving it.
PRIVATE notes are visible only to the author — notes.manage does not grant read access to another user's private notes.
The Branch Selector on the form is only active when Visibility is BRANCH — if you set a branch and then switch visibility to BUSINESS or PRIVATE, the branch value is sent but ignored by the filter.
Reminder cancellation is only possible while the reminder is in SCHEDULED status — once a reminder has been sent or has failed it cannot be cancelled or rescheduled; create a new reminder instead.
Search and tag filters are debounced at 350 ms — type your query and pause briefly before assuming no results exist.

related_pages.reason: Link notes to specific products to attach context, instructions, or observations to catalogue items.
Link notes to customer records to track credit terms, disputes, or special handling instructions.
Link notes to supplier records to document agreements, complaints, or delivery preferences.
IN_APP reminders scheduled on notes appear here when they fire.
Note creation, edits, archiving, and reminder events are recorded in the audit log.

permissions_required: notes.write (create notes, edit own notes, schedule reminders)

entries.title: Notifications
entries.overview: Your personal notification inbox. The system delivers in-app alerts here when events you are subscribed to occur — low stock warnings, approval decisions, sale completions, security alerts, and more. The page maintains a live Server-Sent Events (SSE) connection so new notifications appear at the top of page 1 in real time without a manual refresh. Bulk tools let you mark read or archive multiple items at once.
before_you_start: This is your personal inbox — it shows only notifications addressed to you based on your role and user ID, not all business notifications.
New notifications arrive at the top of page 1 automatically via a live stream. The stream reconnects on its own if it drops, with a delay of up to 30 seconds.
Archived notifications are permanently removed from your inbox view — there is no way to retrieve them from this page.
Mark All Read marks every notification in your full inbox as read across all pages, not just the current page.
SECURITY priority notifications (unusual logins, refresh token reuse) always appear in a separate red-highlighted section at the top, regardless of other filters.

elements.label: Inbox Size
elements.description: Count of notifications on the current page matching the active filters. Not a system-wide total — change or clear filters to see different counts.

elements.label: Unread
elements.description: Count of UNREAD notifications on the current page. Does not include notifications on other pages. Navigate or remove filters to see a more complete picture.

elements.label: Action Required
elements.description: Count of ACTION_REQUIRED priority notifications on the current page. These are notifications that need your direct response — such as pending approvals waiting on you.

elements.label: Selected
elements.description: Count of notifications currently checked for bulk action. Only notifications on the current page can be selected. Clearing the page (navigating or filtering) resets the selection.

elements.label: Search
elements.description: Debounced text search (350 ms) that filters notifications by title and message content. Results update automatically as you type.

elements.label: Advanced Filters
elements.description: Expands or collapses the advanced filter row containing Status, Priority, From Date, and To Date filters.

elements.label: Reset Filters
elements.description: Clears all active filters — search text, status, priority, from date, and to date — and reloads the full inbox from page 1.

elements.label: Status Filter
elements.description: Filters by read status. Options: All, Unread, Read. Archived notifications are never shown — the inbox does not expose an 'include archived' option.

elements.label: Priority Filter
elements.description: Filters by notification priority. Options: All, Action Required, Warning, Info, Security. Security notifications also appear in the dedicated Security Alerts section at the top of the page.

elements.label: From Date
elements.description: Date picker that sets the start of the date range filter. Only notifications created on or after this date are shown. Leave blank for no lower bound.

elements.label: To Date
elements.description: Date picker that sets the end of the date range filter. Only notifications created on or before this date are shown. Leave blank for no upper bound.

elements.label: Select All
elements.description: Checks or unchecks all notifications on the current page for bulk action. Does not select notifications on other pages. The Selected KPI reflects how many are checked.

elements.label: Mark Selected Read
elements.description: Marks all currently selected notifications as read (POST /notifications/read-bulk). Disabled until at least one notification is selected. The bell icon count in the top navigation updates immediately.

elements.label: Archive Selected
elements.description: Permanently removes selected notifications from your inbox after a confirmation prompt (POST /notifications/archive-bulk). Archived items cannot be retrieved from the UI. Disabled until at least one notification is selected.

elements.label: Mark All Read
elements.description: Marks your entire inbox as read after a confirmation prompt (POST /notifications/read-all). Applies across all pages and all filters — not just what is currently visible. The bell icon count in the top navigation resets to zero.

elements.label: Security Alerts Section
elements.description: A red-bordered panel shown above the regular notification list whenever you have any SECURITY priority notifications. Covers unusual login activity and refresh token reuse events. Security items can be selected and marked read from here. The section disappears when no security notifications remain.

elements.label: Notification Item
elements.description: Each notification row shows: a selection checkbox, the priority label (Action Required / Warning / Info), a formatted timestamp using your business timezone setting, the notification title, and the message body (system metadata resolved into readable text). UNREAD items are visually distinct from READ ones.

elements.label: Mark as Read
elements.description: Per-notification button that marks a single item as read (POST /notifications/:id/read). Only visible on UNREAD notifications — it disappears once the item is read. The bell icon count in the top navigation decrements immediately.

elements.label: Pagination Controls
elements.description: Cursor-based navigation with previous and next page buttons and a page size selector. Navigating to a new page clears the current selection. The total inbox count is shown when available.

elements.name: Sound toggle
elements.description: Enable or disable an audio notification sound when new notifications arrive via the real-time stream. Toggle via the sound icon button.

elements.name: Security alerts section
elements.description: A separate panel with a red border at the top of the page showing only SECURITY priority notifications (login detections, permission changes, failed authentication). Always visible above the regular notification list.
