elements.name: Bulk mark read
elements.description: Select multiple notifications via checkboxes, then click to mark them all as read at once.

elements.name: Archive selected
elements.description: Archive multiple selected notifications to remove them from the active inbox.

elements.name: Selected count display
elements.description: Shows how many notifications are currently selected for bulk actions.

elements.name: SSE real-time streaming
elements.description: New notifications appear automatically without refreshing the page, delivered via server-sent events (SSE). The connection reconnects automatically if interrupted.

elements.name: Notification preferences link
elements.description: Navigates to Business Settings where you can configure which notification types you receive.

common_tasks.title: Mark a single notification as read
steps: Locate the notification in the list — unread items are visually distinct.
Click the Mark as Read button on the notification.
The button disappears and the Unread KPI count decreases.
The bell icon count in the top navigation bar also updates.

common_tasks.title: Mark multiple notifications as read at once
steps: Check the checkboxes on the notifications you want to mark, or use Select All to select everything on the current page.
Click Mark Selected Read in the bulk action bar.
All selected notifications are marked read and the selection is cleared.

common_tasks.title: Archive notifications you no longer need
steps: Check the checkboxes on the notifications you want to archive.
Click Archive Selected — a confirmation prompt appears.
Confirm the action. The selected notifications disappear from your inbox immediately.
Note: archived notifications cannot be retrieved from the UI.

common_tasks.title: Mark your entire inbox as read at once
steps: Click the Mark All Read button in the bulk action bar.
A confirmation prompt appears — confirm to proceed.
All notifications across your entire inbox (all pages) are marked read.
The bell icon count in the top navigation resets to zero.

common_tasks.title: Filter by status or priority
steps: Click Advanced Filters to reveal the filter row.
Select Unread from the Status Filter to see only unread items.
Select a priority from the Priority Filter to focus on a specific urgency level.
Click Reset Filters to return to the full inbox.

common_tasks.title: Filter notifications by date range
steps: Click Advanced Filters.
Set a date in the From Date picker to show only notifications from that date onward.
Set a date in the To Date picker to show only notifications up to that date.
Both dates can be used together to narrow to a specific time window.
Click Reset Filters to clear the date range.

common_tasks.title: Search for a specific notification
steps: Type keywords from the notification title or message into the Search box.
Results filter automatically after 350 ms.
Combine with the Priority or Status filter to narrow further.
Click Reset Filters to clear the search.

common_tasks.title: Review and dismiss security alerts
steps: Security alerts (unusual login, refresh token reuse) appear in the red Security Alerts section at the top of the page.
Read the alert details — each shows a priority label, timestamp, title, and message.
Click Mark as Read on each security alert to acknowledge it.
Or check the checkboxes and use Mark Selected Read to dismiss them in bulk.
The Security Alerts section disappears when no security notifications remain.

warnings: Archiving is irreversible from the UI — the frontend has no 'show archived' filter, so archived notifications cannot be seen or recovered once dismissed.
Mark All Read is a full-inbox action that spans all pages — it will mark every notification as read, including ones not currently visible due to filters or pagination.
The Unread, Action Required, and Selected KPI counts reflect only the current page, not your full inbox. Navigate with filters cleared to get accurate totals.

If the live stream disconnects (network drop, token expiry), the page reconnects automatically but may take up to 30 seconds. Notifications sent during a reconnection window will appear only after a manual reload or when the stream reconnects and the next notification arrives.
The Select All checkbox covers only the current page. Notifications on other pages are not included in bulk actions — paginate and repeat if you need to process multiple pages.

entries.title: Offline Mode
entries.overview: Register and manage this device for offline operation. When offline, the app queues sales, stock adjustments, and purchase drafts in local IndexedDB storage and replays them to the server when connectivity is restored. This page shows the device's registration status, pending queue depth, queue storage usage, last sync timestamp, and subscription-imposed limits. Use it to register the device, trigger a manual sync, set a local access PIN, or revoke offline access. Offline mode requires a BUSINESS or ENTERPRISE subscription — STARTER tier has all limits set to zero.

elements.name: KPI — Device Status
elements.description: Shows the server-reported status of this device: ACTIVE (registered and permitted), REVOKED (access removed), EXPIRED (offline session duration limit exceeded), or 'Not Registered' if no device record exists. Sourced from GET /offline/status on page load — only populated when the device is online.

elements.name: KPI — Pending Actions
elements.description: Count of queued offline actions waiting to be synced, read directly from local IndexedDB. This reflects what is stored on the device — it may differ from the server-side pending count if a previous sync partially succeeded. A value greater than 0 means unsynced work exists that could be lost if the device is revoked.

elements.name: KPI — Queue Load
elements.description: Percentage of the local queue capacity used, calculated as (queued item count ÷ max items) × 100, capped at 100%. When this approaches 100%, new offline actions may be rejected. Sync promptly to clear the queue. The raw counts and byte sizes are visible in the Device Info panel.

elements.name: KPI — Last Sync
elements.description: Timestamp of the most recent successful sync, stored as a local IndexedDB flag. Shows 'Never' if this device has not yet successfully synced. Use this to judge how stale the device's data may be — the server marks a device as 'stale' if it has not been seen for more than 2 hours.

elements.name: Device info panel
elements.description: Displays the device's full status at a glance: device ID (auto-generated and persisted in localStorage), current device status, last sync timestamp, pending action count, and queue usage (item count / max items, and KB used / max KB). Loaded from both local IndexedDB and the server on page open. Only the server-side fields (status, limits) require an internet connection to populate.

elements.name: Sync Blocked indicator
elements.description: Red warning text that appears in the Device Info panel when the previous sync attempt failed. The syncBlocked flag is set in local IndexedDB on any sync failure and cleared on the next successful sync. If this is visible, click Sync Now to retry — repeated failures may indicate a server-side conflict that needs resolution on the Conflicts page.

elements.name: Cache Truncated indicator
elements.description: Amber warning text that appears when the offline cache hit a hard entity cap during the last build. Hard caps are: Products 5,000 · Variants 10,000 · Barcodes 15,000 · Customers 5,000 · Stock Snapshots 20,000. If truncated, records added after the cap was reached will not be available offline. Contact support to archive inactive records and reduce catalog size.

elements.name: Device Name input
elements.description: A human-readable label for this device (e.g. 'Front Counter iPad', 'Warehouse Android'). Sent to the server when registering. Appears in the audit log and in the admin's Offline Devices list, making it easy to identify which physical device a record came from.

elements.name: Register Device button
elements.description: Calls POST /offline/register-device with the device name and auto-generated device ID. If the device ID already exists, the registration is an upsert (re-activates the device and refreshes the permission snapshot). If new, the server checks the subscription's offlineDevices limit before creating. Requires offline.write. Device registration captures a snapshot of your current permissions — if your roles change later, re-register to refresh them.

elements.name: Sync Now button
elements.description: Manually triggers the offline sync process, replaying all queued actions to the server via POST /offline/sync. On success: clears the syncBlocked flag, updates the Last Sync KPI, and reduces Pending Actions to 0. On failure: sets syncBlocked to true and shows an error. Actions that conflict (price variance, approval required, inactive records) are flagged as CONFLICT rather than failing the whole batch. Requires offline.write.

elements.name: View Conflicts link
elements.description: Navigates to /{locale}/offline/conflicts, where CONFLICT and REJECTED offline actions can be reviewed and resolved. Available at all times — use this after a sync to check whether any actions were held back rather than applied.

elements.name: Revoke Device button
elements.description: Only visible when device.status is ACTIVE. Shows a confirmation dialog before proceeding. On confirm: calls POST /offline/revoke-device, then locally rotates the offline encryption key and clears ALL local IndexedDB data — pending actions, cache, receipt history, and sync flags are permanently erased. Use this when decommissioning a device or responding to a lost/stolen device. Requires offline.write. This action cannot be undone.

elements.name: Subscription limits display
elements.description: Shows the offline limits enforced by the business's subscription tier: maximum offline duration in hours, maximum number of offline sales, and maximum total offline sales value. Defaults by tier — STARTER: 0/0/0 (offline disabled); BUSINESS: 72 hours / 200 sales; ENTERPRISE: 168 hours / 2,000 sales. Limits can be customised per business via POS Policies settings. If the duration limit is exceeded at sync time, the device is marked EXPIRED and sync is blocked.

elements.name: Encryption and risk safeguards
elements.description: Informational panel explaining that offline data is encrypted locally and warning about the risk of unsynced data on a lost or stolen device. Reiterates that revoking a device clears local storage but does not recover already-queued data that was never synced.

elements.name: Offline PIN input
elements.description: Password-type input for setting a local access PIN. The PIN is hashed and stored in IndexedDB only — it is never sent to the server. When a PIN is set, users must enter it before entering offline mode on this device. Provides a local access control layer for shared devices.

elements.name: Enable PIN button
elements.description: Saves the entered PIN to local IndexedDB via setOfflinePin(). Once set, the pinRequired flag is enabled and the Clear PIN button appears. The PIN field must be non-empty before this button has any effect. Requires offline.write.

elements.name: Clear PIN button
elements.description: Only visible when a PIN is currently active. Calls clearOfflinePin() to remove the PIN from IndexedDB, disabling the local PIN gate. Requires offline.write. Use this if the PIN holder is no longer responsible for the device.

elements.name: Synced Receipts History
elements.description: List of receipts that were created offline and subsequently synced. Each row shows: the provisional local receipt number (assigned at sale time offline), the final server-assigned receipt number (populated after sync), and the syncedAt timestamp. Use this to cross-reference a customer's offline receipt number against the system's final receipt number.

elements.name: Queue usage percentage
elements.description: Shows the offline queue usage as a percentage of maximum capacity — items count and total bytes used.

elements.name: Pending count in queue
elements.description: Number of offline actions waiting to be synced to the server.

elements.name: Last sync timestamp
elements.description: Shows when the device last successfully synced with the server.

elements.name: Receipt history panel
elements.description: Lists receipts created while offline, showing the local receipt number and the final receipt number assigned after sync.

elements.name: Cache truncated warning
elements.description: A warning shown when the offline data cache has been truncated to save space.

elements.name: Sync blocked warning
elements.description: A warning shown when sync is blocked due to version mismatch or server issues.

elements.name: View Conflicts link
elements.description: Navigates to the Offline Conflicts page to review and resolve actions that failed during sync.

elements.name: PIN input form
elements.description: Set or clear the offline PIN that protects POS and stock adjustment access when offline. Includes Enable PIN and Clear PIN buttons.

elements.name: Offline limits display
elements.description: Shows configured offline limits: maximum hours, maximum transactions, and maximum total value allowed before sync is required.

elements.name: Safeguards section
elements.description: Information panel explaining offline data encryption and risk warnings about working without connectivity.

before_you_start.text: Offline mode requires a BUSINESS or ENTERPRISE subscription. STARTER tier has all offline limits set to zero — registration will succeed but syncing will be blocked by the duration and transaction limits. Confirm the subscription tier before deploying offline devices.

You need 'offline.write' to register, sync, revoke, or set a PIN. You need 'offline.read' to view the device status and limits. Without offline.write, the Register Device, Sync Now, and Revoke Device buttons are disabled.

Device registration captures a snapshot of your current permissions. If your roles or permissions change after registration (e.g. a manager removes a permission), the device continues to operate with the snapshotted permissions until you re-register. Always re-register after a role change.
The Revoke Device action permanently clears all local offline data — pending unsynced actions are lost and cannot be recovered. Sync before revoking if there are any pending actions.

common_tasks.task: Register this device for offline mode
steps: Ensure you are online and that offline.write permission is assigned to your role.
Enter a descriptive Device Name (e.g. 'Warehouse Tablet 1').
Click Register Device — the server validates the subscription limit and registers the device.
Verify the Device Status KPI changes to ACTIVE.

common_tasks.task: Sync queued offline actions to the server
steps: Ensure the device is online and Pending Actions is greater than 0.
Click Sync Now — the app replays all queued actions to POST /offline/sync.
On success, Pending Actions drops to 0 and Last Sync updates to now.
If any actions conflict (price variance, inactive record, approval required), they remain queued as CONFLICT — click View Conflicts to resolve them.

common_tasks.task: Set a local offline PIN for this device
steps: Enter a PIN in the Offline PIN input field in the Safeguards panel.
Click Enable PIN — the PIN is hashed and stored in local IndexedDB only.
The Clear PIN button appears to confirm the PIN is active.
Users must now enter this PIN before entering offline mode on this device.

common_tasks.task: Clear the offline PIN
steps: Click the Clear PIN button in the Safeguards panel (visible only when a PIN is active).
The PIN is removed from IndexedDB immediately.
The offline PIN gate is disabled on this device.

common_tasks.task: Revoke this device's offline access
steps: Sync any pending actions first — revoking will permanently erase all local data.
Click Revoke Device (visible only when status is ACTIVE).
Confirm the action in the confirmation dialog.
The device is marked REVOKED on the server, the local encryption key is rotated, and all IndexedDB data is cleared.

common_tasks.task: Check subscription offline limits
steps: View the Subscription Limits panel on the right side of the page.
Note the maximum offline duration (hours), maximum offline sales count, and maximum total sales value.
If limits show 0, the current subscription tier does not support offline mode.
Contact the account owner to upgrade to BUSINESS or ENTERPRISE if offline is needed.

common_tasks.task: Cross-reference an offline receipt with its final server number
steps: Scroll to the Synced Receipts History panel at the bottom of the page.
Find the local receipt number the customer was given at the time of the offline sale.
The corresponding row shows the final server-assigned receipt number and sync timestamp.
Use the final receipt number to look up the sale in the Sales or Receipts module.

warnings: Revoking a device permanently erases all local IndexedDB data on that device — including pending unsynced actions that have not yet been sent to the server. There is no recovery path. Always sync before revoking.

If the Cache Truncated warning is visible (amber), the offline cache is incomplete — records added after the entity cap was reached will not be available for offline sales or adjustments. Reduce catalog size by archiving inactive products, variants, or customers, then re-sync.

The Sync Blocked indicator (red) means the last sync failed. Repeated sync failures may indicate a server-side conflict or an expired device session. Click Sync Now to retry, then check the Conflicts page if actions remain unresolved.

Offline duration limits are enforced at sync time by the server. If the device has been offline longer than the subscription allows (BUSINESS: 72h, ENTERPRISE: 168h), the device is marked EXPIRED and further syncs are blocked until re-registration.

entries.title: Offline Conflicts
entries.overview: Review and resolve offline actions that failed to apply when your device synced back online. Each conflict represents a queued action — a sale, purchase draft, or stock adjustment — that could not be automatically committed due to a price variance, a pending approval, a VAT change, or another error. The list is strictly scoped to the current device and the signed-in user. You must be online to load or resolve conflicts.
before_you_start: You must be online to access this page. If the device is offline when you navigate here, a banner appears and no data is loaded.
You need the Offline Read permission to view conflicts and Offline Write permission to resolve them. Resolution buttons are disabled and show a tooltip when you lack write access.
Conflicts are device-specific — actions queued on another device do not appear here. Use the Offline main page to see overall device status and pending counts.

elements.label: Open Conflicts
elements.description: Total count of conflict and rejected offline action records loaded in the current view. Reflects the current page only — press Load More for additional records.

elements.label: Approval Blocks
elements.description: Count of loaded conflicts where the reason is APPROVAL_REQUIRED — actions that were held because they needed manager approval before committing.

elements.label: Price Variance
elements.description: Count of loaded conflicts where the reason is PRICE_VARIANCE — sales where the offline price differed from the current price list by more than the configured threshold.

elements.label: Next Page
elements.description: Indicates whether more conflict records exist beyond the current page. 'Yes' means the Load More button will fetch the next 20 records.

elements.label: Conflict List
elements.description: Each conflict card shows: action type (Sale Complete, Purchase Draft, or Stock Adjustment), current status badge (PENDING / APPLIED / REJECTED / CONFLICT / FAILED), conflict reason, error message if present, and approval reference ID if the conflict involved an approval. Cards are styled in red to signal they require attention. Ordered newest first.

elements.label: Override Price
elements.description: Available only on PRICE_VARIANCE conflicts for Sale Complete actions. Re-executes the sale bypassing the price variance threshold, committing it at the offline price. Use when a supervisor has authorised the price difference. Not available for Purchase Draft or Stock Adjustment conflicts.

elements.label: Sync Approval
elements.description: Available only on APPROVAL_REQUIRED conflicts. Checks the linked approval record. If the approval is now APPROVED, the action is re-executed with elevated permissions. If still PENDING, the conflict remains in place with 'Approval still pending.' If the approval was REJECTED, the action is permanently rejected.

elements.label: Retry
elements.description: Re-attempts the original action using your current permissions. If your permissions have changed since the action was queued, the retry may be rejected with PERMISSION_REVOKED. Available for all conflict reasons except VAT_CHANGED (which allows Dismiss only).

elements.label: Dismiss
elements.description: Permanently marks the action as rejected with the message 'Dismissed by user.' The conflict is removed from the list. This is the only resolution available for VAT_CHANGED conflicts. Dismissal cannot be undone — the underlying action (e.g. a sale) will not be recorded.

elements.label: Load More
elements.description: Appends the next 20 conflict records to the current list without resetting scroll position. Only shown when more records exist (Next Page = Yes). Displays a spinner while fetching.

elements.name: Conflict card layout
elements.description: Each conflict shows the action type (SaleComplete, PurchaseDraft, StockAdjustment), status badge, error message, and resolution buttons.

elements.name: Resolution options
elements.description: Each conflict has resolution buttons that vary by conflict type. For example, VAT_CHANGED conflicts only offer Dismiss. Other conflicts may offer Retry or Override.

elements.name: Approval ID display
elements.description: If a conflict involves a pending approval, the approval ID is shown for cross-referencing with the Approvals page.

elements.name: Conflict status badges
elements.description: Coloured badges showing conflict status: Applied, Conflict, Failed, Pending, Rejected.

elements.name: Load More pagination
elements.description: Load additional conflict records beyond the initial page.

common_tasks.title: Resolve a price variance conflict
steps: Locate the conflict card with reason PRICE_VARIANCE.
If a manager has authorised the offline price, click Override Price. The sale is committed at the offline price and the conflict is removed from the list.
If the price should not be accepted, click Dismiss. The sale is not recorded — you will need to re-enter it manually at the correct price.

common_tasks.title: Resolve an approval-required conflict
steps: Locate the conflict card with reason APPROVAL_REQUIRED. Note the Approval ID shown on the card.
Ask a manager to review and approve the request in the Approvals page.
Once approved, return to this page and click Sync Approval on the conflict.
If the approval is confirmed, the action is applied and the conflict disappears. If still pending, the card stays with an updated error message.

common_tasks.title: Retry a failed or rejected action
steps: Find the conflict card for the action you want to retry.
Click Retry. The system re-runs the full action pipeline with your current permissions.
If successful, the conflict is removed from the list. If a new conflict arises (e.g. a new price variance), the card updates with the new reason.

common_tasks.title: Clear a VAT-changed conflict
steps: Locate the conflict card with reason VAT_CHANGED.
Click Dismiss — this is the only option available for VAT changes.
The conflict is removed. Re-enter the transaction manually using the updated VAT settings.

warnings: Dismissing a conflict is permanent and irreversible. The underlying action (sale, adjustment, or purchase draft) will not be applied — it must be re-entered manually if the transaction still needs to be recorded.
RETRY re-executes the action with your current permissions. If your role has been modified since the action was queued, the retry may fail with PERMISSION_REVOKED.
VAT_CHANGED conflicts can only be dismissed — there is no retry or override path. The original transaction must be re-entered manually with the correct VAT mode applied.

entries.title: Business Setup
entries.overview: This guided setup runs once the first time you log in after registering. It walks you through 3 steps: setting up your business profile, configuring your first branch, and optionally inviting your team. You cannot skip to the main system until both the business profile and branch setup are marked complete.

before_you_start.text: You must have verified your email address before reaching this page.
You need the business.update and settings.write permissions to save changes. As the business owner, you have these by default.

elements.name: Step indicator pills
elements.description: Three pills at the top show your progress: Business Profile → Branch Setup → Team. The currently active step is highlighted.

elements.name: Business Name field (Step 1)
elements.description: Your business name. Pre-filled from registration — update it here if you want to change it.

elements.name: Currency field (Step 1)
elements.description: The currency your business operates in. Default is TZS. Enter the 3-letter currency code (e.g. TZS, USD).

elements.name: Timezone field (Step 1)
elements.description: Your business timezone. This is read-only and set to Africa/Dar_es_Salaam. Contact support if you need to change it.

elements.name: Date Format field (Step 1)
elements.description: Controls how dates are displayed throughout the system on receipts, reports, and all date fields. Set this to your country's standard (e.g. DD/MM/YYYY for Tanzania) before inviting staff — it affects how all dates are read and entered across the platform.

elements.name: Save & Continue button (Step 1)
elements.description: Saves your business name, contact details, currency, and date format, then advances to Step 2 (Branch Setup). You can return to these settings any time via Business Settings → Profile.

elements.name: Branch list (Step 2)
elements.description: Shows all branches currently created for your business. A badge marks the default branch.

elements.name: Branch selector (Step 2)
elements.description: Select one of the pre-created branches to review or update its details. Your first branch was created automatically when you signed up — select it here to set its display name, address, and contact phone.

elements.name: Branch Name / Address / Phone fields (Step 2)
elements.description: The branch's display name appears in reports, receipts, and navigation menus. Address and phone are optional but print on receipts if provided. Set these accurately now — staff and customers will see this information on every receipt.

elements.name: Save Branch Details button (Step 2)
elements.description: Saves the name, address, and phone for the selected branch and marks it as configured. You can add more branches later in Settings → Branches.

elements.name: Add Another Branch section (Step 2)
elements.description: A separate form to create a new branch. Fill in Name (required), Address, and Phone, then click Add Branch.

elements.name: Next button (Step 2)
elements.description: Marks branch setup as complete and moves you to Step 3. You must have at least one branch before this button works.

elements.name: Open Users button (Step 3)
elements.description: Navigates to Settings → Team Members where you can invite staff by email and assign roles. Complete roles setup first so each person can be assigned the correct access level from day one.

elements.name: Open Roles button (Step 3)
elements.description: Navigates to Settings → Roles where you can create custom roles and configure permission sets. Set up your roles before inviting users — without roles configured, invited staff will have no permissions.

elements.name: Finish Later button (Step 3)
elements.description: Skips the team setup step and takes you directly to the Dashboard. You can invite team members any time from Settings.

elements.name: Receipt Template field
elements.description: Choose the default receipt format: THERMAL (for receipt printers) or A4 (full-page). Set during Step 2 POS Setup.

elements.name: Receipt Header and Footer fields
elements.description: Customise the text printed at the top and bottom of every receipt. The footer has a default 'Thank you' message.

elements.name: Team Invite step
elements.description: Step 3 allows you to invite team members by email and assign them a role. Invitations are sent immediately.

elements.name: Ready screen
elements.description: Step 4 shows a completion message with rotating tips. Click 'Go to Dashboard' to start using the system.

elements.name: Timezone prioritization
elements.description: The timezone selector lists African timezones first for convenience. Defaults to Africa/Dar_es_Salaam.

common_tasks.task: Complete business setup
steps: Step 1: Check and update your Business Name, Currency, and Date Format, then click Save & Continue.
Step 2: Review or edit your default branch details. Add more branches if needed, then click Next.
Step 3: Click Open Users to invite staff, or click Finish Later to go to the Dashboard.

warnings: You cannot proceed to the system until Step 1 (Business Profile) and Step 2 (Branch Setup) are both marked complete.
At least one branch is required before you can complete Step 2.
The Timezone field is read-only. If your timezone is wrong, contact support.
If the system shows a Read Only banner, your account is temporarily restricted. Contact support.

workflow.step: Step 3: Open Users/Roles or finish later, then route back to app dashboard.
workflow.expected_result: Onboarding exits cleanly and app access continues with setup flags persisted.

common_errors.likely_cause: Branch setup completion was submitted with zero active/non-archived branches.
fix_steps: Create at least one branch before pressing next on branch setup.
Reload branch list and verify the branch is not archived.
Retry onboarding completion after branch exists.

common_errors.error_symptom: Onboarding save actions fail with PERMISSION_IS_REQUIRED.
common_errors.likely_cause: User role lacks required permissions for business/settings update actions.
fix_steps: Ask admin to grant business.update and settings.write permissions.
Retry onboarding action after permission update takes effect.
If role is branch-scoped, verify scope allows current setup actions.

common_errors.likely_cause: Session lost business context or token/session state is stale.
fix_steps: Re-login to refresh session and business context.
Confirm active business is selected before returning to onboarding.
Retry onboarding save operations after session refresh.

related_pages.reason: Onboarding starts after successful Login when setup is incomplete.
Business settings is a direct follow-up for deeper policy/profile adjustments.
Branch settings is the operational follow-up for managing branch records.
Users setup is the next step for team configuration after onboarding core setup.

entries.title: Reset Password
entries.overview: Use this page if you have forgotten your password. Enter your email address and the system will send you a password reset link. If your email is linked to more than one business, you will need to choose which business to reset the password for.

before_you_start.text: You need access to the email address you registered with.

elements.name: Email field
elements.description: Enter the email address your account is registered under.

elements.name: Business selector
elements.description: Appears only if your email is linked to more than one business. Choose which business you want to reset the password for.
elements.notes: This appears after you submit your email the first time.

elements.name: Send Reset Link button
elements.description: Submits your email and sends a password reset link to your inbox. Check your email (and spam folder) after clicking.

elements.name: Two-stage flow
elements.description: First enter your email to receive a reset link. If you belong to multiple businesses, a business selector appears to specify which business context.

elements.name: Business selector (optional)
elements.description: Appears only if your email is associated with multiple businesses. Select which business to reset the password for.

common_tasks.task: Request a password reset
steps: Enter your email address.
Click Send Reset Link.
If prompted, choose your business from the dropdown and click Send Reset Link again.
Check your inbox for the reset email and click the link inside it.

warnings: The reset link expires after a short time. Use it promptly after it arrives in your email.
If you do not receive the email within a few minutes, check your spam or junk folder.
This page only sends the reset link — to actually set your new password, follow the link in the email.

related_pages.reason: You arrive here from Login when you forget your password.
After clicking the email link, you land here to set your new password.

entries.title: Set New Password
entries.overview: This page is where you create your new password after clicking the reset link in your email. The reset token is filled in automatically from the link. Enter your new password, then submit.

before_you_start.text: You must have received and clicked the password reset link sent to your email.

elements.name: Token field
elements.description: The reset token from your email. Usually filled in automatically when you arrive via the email link. If it is blank, paste the token from the email manually.

elements.name: New Password field
elements.description: Your new password. Must meet the minimum requirements shown below the field.

elements.name: Show / Hide Password button
elements.description: Toggles whether your new password is visible or hidden as you type.

elements.name: Set Password button
elements.description: Submits the token and new password. If successful, you will be redirected to the Login page after 1.5 seconds.

elements.name: Token auto-fill
elements.description: The reset token is automatically filled from the URL. You do not need to enter it manually.

elements.name: Auto-redirect on success
elements.description: After setting your new password, you are automatically redirected to the login page after 1.5 seconds.

common_tasks.task: Set your new password
steps: Confirm the token field is filled (it should be automatic).
Type your new password.
Click Set Password.
You will be redirected to Login — sign in with your new password.

warnings: Reset tokens expire after a short time. If you see a token expired error, go back to the Password Reset page and request a new link.
Your new password must meet the minimum requirements. A password hint is shown below the field.
After setting your new password, you will be redirected to Login automatically.

related_pages.reason: You must request a reset link here before you can use this page.
After setting your new password, sign in here.

entries.title: Point of Sale (POS)
entries.overview: The POS is a full-screen checkout interface that overlays the rest of the app. It handles product selection, cart management, discounts, multi-method payments, receipts, and offline queuing. Sales flow through two backend steps: draft (validates cart, checks discounts) then complete (processes payment and deducts stock). Three device layouts adapt the interface to phone, tablet, or desktop screens.

before_you_start.text: At least one active product variant with a configured default price must exist and be in stock at the selected branch.
If shift tracking is enabled in POS settings, an open shift must exist for the branch before any sale can be completed — use the Open Shift button in the top bar.
Your role needs sales.write to complete sales. Opening a shift also requires the shifts.open permission.

elements.name: Branch Selector
elements.description: Dropdown in the top bar showing only branches within your assigned branch scope. Defaults to the active branch stored in your session. Locked (disabled) while the cart contains items — clear the cart before switching branches. If your scope contains only one branch it is selected automatically.

elements.name: Device Mode Switcher
elements.description: Three-button toggle in the top bar: Phone, Tablet, Desktop. Switches the screen layout immediately and saves the choice to localStorage. Auto-detected from screen width on first visit — change it at any time to suit your hardware.

elements.name: Shift Indicator & Open Shift Button
elements.description: Shows a green pulsing dot and 'Shift Open' when an open shift exists for the active branch. If shift tracking is enabled but no shift is open, an amber warning strip appears below the top bar and sale completion is blocked. Click Open Shift to launch the shift modal and enter opening cash.

elements.name: Session Stats & Live Clock
elements.description: Live clock (HH:MM, ticks every second) visible in the top bar. After the first completed sale, a session summary appears showing the count of sales and total revenue for this login session. A pending sync count badge (blue) shows how many offline sales are queued.

elements.name: Stats Strip
elements.description: Always-visible bar below the top bar showing: Items (total units in cart), Total (formatted running cart total in business currency), Status (Online/Offline with a colour dot), and Branch name on larger screens.

elements.name: Offline PIN Gate
elements.description: Appears as a red strip when offline mode is active and a PIN is required but not yet verified. Enter the offline PIN and click Unlock to enable sales. After 5 failed attempts the PIN is locked until a specific time shown in the message. While locked, the input and button are disabled.

elements.name: Barcode / Text Search Bar
elements.description: Typeahead input at the top of the product panel. Type a variant name, SKU, or barcode — a dropdown suggests matches. Selecting a result or pressing Enter adds the variant to the cart. USB/Bluetooth barcode scanners work automatically without clicking here — the page always listens for scan events of 6 or more characters.

elements.name: Camera Scan Toggle
elements.description: Starts or stops the device camera barcode scanner. Automatically selects the rear-facing camera if available. When active the button turns red and a video feed appears in the product panel. A successful scan adds the matching variant to the cart. If the barcode matches multiple variants a disambiguation message is shown.

elements.name: Product Grid Filter
elements.description: Text input above the product tile grid. Filters the visible tiles in real time by variant name, product name, SKU, or barcode. Works offline using the cached snapshot. Variants with confirmed zero stock (hasStock = false) are always hidden from the grid regardless of filter.

elements.name: Product Grid
elements.description: Tile grid of available variants (2 columns on phone, 3 on tablet and desktop). Each tile shows the product image (or initials if no image), product name, variant name, and price. A gold badge on the tile shows the quantity already in the cart. Clicking a tile adds one unit; if already in the cart the quantity increments. The price shown uses the active customer price list if one is applied, otherwise the variant default price.

elements.name: Customer Typeahead
elements.description: Optional customer search at the top of the cart panel. Filters the local customer list immediately by name or TIN, then runs a debounced API search (300 ms) for wider results. Selecting a customer attaches them to the sale (name and TIN are snapshotted on the receipt) and activates their assigned price list for all cart items. Remove the customer by clicking the × on their badge.

elements.name: Cart Items
elements.description: Each cart row contains: variant name (with a red min-price warning if the price was edited below the variant minimum), a quantity stepper (−/+ buttons, minimum 1), a unit selector (base or sell unit — switching unit recalculates the unit price using the conversion factor), a unit price input (read-only unless priceEditEnabled is on in POS settings), a line discount input, and a Remove (×) button.

elements.name: Cart Discount
elements.description: Currency input at the bottom of the cart panel. Applies a discount to the entire cart total on top of any per-line discounts. Combined with line discounts, if the total discount percentage or amount exceeds the configured POS threshold, the draft step will trigger a discount approval request.

elements.name: Amount Due Display
elements.description: Large bold display of the cart total. Below it, a breakdown row shows subtotal, total discounts (line + cart combined), and VAT amount. VAT is calculated per line based on each variant's VAT mode (INCLUSIVE, EXCLUSIVE, or EXEMPT) and the business VAT rate from settings.

elements.name: Payment Method Selector
elements.description: Five visual card buttons per payment row: Cash, Card, Mobile Money, Bank Transfer, Other. Selecting 'Other' reveals a custom method label input. Multiple payment rows are supported for split payments — each row has its own method selection.

elements.name: Payment Amount Input
elements.description: Numeric field formatted with commas. Accepts the amount tendered for this payment row. For overpayment in cash the change is calculated implicitly — the system allows overpayment only when at least one payment method is Cash.

elements.name: Quick-Cash Presets
elements.description: Shown only for Cash payment rows. 'Exact' fills the remaining balance. Up to four round denomination buttons (from 500 to 1,000,000) above the remaining balance are shown for one-tap entry of common note values (500, 1K, 2K, 5K, 10K, 20K, 50K, 100K, 200K, 500K, 1M).

elements.name: Add Payment Button
elements.description: Adds a new payment row to support split payments across multiple methods. There is no limit on the number of rows. Each row can be removed individually using its Remove link (except the first row).

elements.name: Credit Sale Toggle
elements.description: Checkbox visible only when credit sales are enabled in POS settings. When checked, the payment total is allowed to be less than the cart total — the remaining amount becomes an outstanding credit balance for the customer. A due date picker appears to set the repayment deadline.

elements.name: Complete Sale Button
elements.description: Gold CTA that triggers the two-step sale process: POST /sales/draft (validates cart, creates DRAFT) then POST /sales/complete (processes payments, deducts stock, issues receipt). Disabled if the cart is empty or a completion is already in progress. If offline (and PIN verified, sync not blocked), the sale is queued to IndexedDB as SALE_COMPLETE and a provisional receipt number is generated.

elements.name: Connect ESC/POS Printer
elements.description: Connects to a USB or Bluetooth thermal receipt printer via the Web Bluetooth / WebUSB API. A green dot appears when connected. Enable the 'Hardware print' checkbox to auto-print receipts after each sale. Requires a browser that supports Web Bluetooth (Chrome/Edge on desktop).

elements.name: Receipt Preview & Print
elements.description: Appears after the first completed sale. Opens the receipt preview modal showing the last receipt in Compact or Detailed mode. From the modal, click Print to send to the ESC/POS printer (if connected) or trigger the browser print dialog for A4/thermal layout.

elements.name: Open Shift Modal
elements.description: Modal triggered by the Open Shift button. Contains: Opening Cash input (required — the amount of float in the cash drawer at shift start) and Shift Notes (optional free text). Submits to POST /shifts/open. Dismiss with Cancel or press Escape.

elements.name: Receipt Preview Modal
elements.description: Full receipt preview overlay. Toggle between Compact (summary) and Detailed (itemised) modes. Print button sends to the connected ESC/POS printer or opens the browser print dialog. The receipt renders in the correct thermal (80mm) or A4 format based on the business receipt template setting.

elements.name: Parked Sales feature
elements.description: Park a sale in progress to serve another customer, then resume it later. Parked sales are stored locally and shown in a list. Click Resume to continue where you left off.

elements.name: Session sale counter
elements.description: Displays how many sales you have completed and the total revenue in this login session. Resets when you log out.

elements.name: Pending sync badge
elements.description: Shows the count of offline sales waiting to be synced to the server. If this stays above zero, check your internet connection.

elements.name: PIN lock and attempt counter
elements.description: If the offline PIN is enabled, you must enter it to unlock the POS when offline. After 5 failed attempts, the PIN is locked for a cooldown period.

elements.name: Popular items section
elements.description: Shows your most frequently sold items for quick adding to the cart. Based on your recent sales history.

elements.name: Barcode scan sound toggle
elements.description: Enable or disable an audio beep when a barcode is successfully scanned. Toggle via the sound icon in the toolbar.

elements.name: Cart persistence
elements.description: Your cart is automatically saved to localStorage. If you close the browser or refresh, the cart is restored when you return.

elements.name: Mobile tab navigation
elements.description: On phone-sized screens, the POS uses tab navigation: Products (browse/search), Cart (view items), Pay (complete sale). Swipe or tap tabs to switch.

elements.name: Customer price list
elements.description: When a customer with an assigned price list is selected, product prices automatically update to reflect their special pricing.

elements.name: Quick cash amounts
elements.description: Pre-set cash amount buttons (formatted with K/M for thousands/millions) for fast cash payment entry without typing.

elements.name: Payment method icons
elements.description: Five payment methods with unique icons: Cash (notes & coins), Card, Mobile Money, Bank Transfer, and Other. Each has a description to help cashiers select correctly.

elements.name: Live clock
elements.description: A real-time clock display in the POS header, ticking every second. Useful for shift reference without leaving the POS screen.

elements.name: Receipt template selector
elements.description: Choose between THERMAL (receipt printer) and A4 (full-page) receipt formats.

elements.name: Unit conversion hint
elements.description: A dynamic hint below the unit selector on each cart item showing the stock conversion. For example: '2 boxes = 10 pieces in stock'. Only appears when the variant uses different base and sell units.

common_tasks.task: Complete a sale online
steps: Select a branch from the top bar if not already set.
Search for a product in the search bar or browse the product grid and click tiles to add items to the cart.
Optionally attach a customer using the Customer Typeahead.
In the payment panel, select the payment method and enter the amount tendered.
Click Complete Sale — the draft is validated, then the sale is completed, stock is deducted, and a receipt number is shown.

common_tasks.task: Handle a discount approval block
steps: If Complete Sale returns a discount approval notice, the sale is saved as a DRAFT but is blocked.
A manager with approvals.write permission must approve the SALE_DISCOUNT request on the Approvals page.
Once approved, return to the POS — the draft may need to be re-submitted or will complete automatically depending on the approval flow.
If you reduce the discount to below the threshold and re-submit, no approval is needed.

common_tasks.task: Complete a sale offline
steps: When offline, the top bar shows an OFFLINE badge and a status dot turns amber.
If a PIN is required, enter your offline PIN in the PIN gate strip and click Unlock.
Build the cart as normal — product data is served from the offline cache (snapshot).
Click Complete Sale — the sale is queued to IndexedDB with a provisional receipt number (OFFLINE-{timestamp}) and the cart clears.
When you go back online, the queued sale syncs automatically and stock updates.

common_tasks.task: Open a shift before selling
steps: If shift tracking is enabled and no shift is open, click Open Shift in the top bar.
Enter the opening cash amount (the float in the till at shift start).
Optionally add shift notes.
Click Open Shift — the shift status indicator turns green and sale completion is unblocked.

common_tasks.task: Record a split payment across multiple methods
steps: In the payment panel, set the first payment method and enter a partial amount.
Click Add Payment to add a second payment row.
Select the second method and enter the remaining amount.
For Cash rows, use the Quick-Cash Presets or the Exact button to fill the remaining balance.
Click Complete Sale when the total payment equals or exceeds the cart total.

common_tasks.task: Apply line and cart discounts
steps: For a per-item discount, enter the discount amount in the Line Discount field on the relevant cart row.
For an overall cart discount, enter the amount in the Cart Discount input at the bottom of the cart panel.
Watch the Amount Due display — the breakdown row shows total discounts applied.
If the combined discount exceeds the POS policy threshold, Complete Sale will return an approval request instead of completing.

common_tasks.task: Print a receipt after a sale
steps: After a completed sale, click Preview & Print in the printer controls area.
In the receipt modal, toggle between Compact and Detailed view.
If an ESC/POS printer is connected, click Print to send directly to the printer.
If no printer is connected, click Print to open the browser print dialog — the receipt renders in the correct thermal or A4 layout.

warnings: The branch cannot be changed while the cart has items — clear the cart first by removing all lines, then switch branches.
If shift tracking is enabled, sale completion is blocked at both the frontend and the backend until an open shift exists for the active branch.
Offline sales queue as SALE_COMPLETE and sync when you reconnect — stock and the ledger do not update until sync completes. The provisional receipt number changes to the final number after sync.
Unit price cannot be edited below a variant's minimum price — the draft step will return a 400 error. A red warning label appears on the cart item when the price is too low.

If cart or line discounts exceed the POS discount threshold configured in settings, the draft step returns a discount approval request — the sale sits as a DRAFT and cannot be completed until a manager approves it.
Negative stock (default policy): if a tracked variant has insufficient quantity at the branch, the complete step fails with a stock error even if the draft was accepted.

related_pages.reason: Open and close shifts here before using the POS when shift tracking is enabled.
Ensure products have prices, active status, and stock before they appear in the POS grid.
View completed sale history, receipts, and payment breakdowns here after checkout.
Verify stock levels have decreased correctly after completing sales.

entries.title: Price Lists
entries.overview: Create named price lists and assign custom prices to individual product variants. When a price list is assigned to a customer or branch, the POS applies those custom prices automatically during a sale instead of the product's default price. Use the Bulk Update Wizard to apply a percentage, fixed-amount, or flat-rate adjustment across many variants at once.

before_you_start.text: The price-lists.manage permission is required for everything on this page — including viewing it. Staff without this permission cannot access Price Lists at all.
Price lists must be created here before you can assign them to customers on the Customers page or to branches in branch settings.

elements.name: KPI — Price lists
elements.description: Count of price lists loaded so far on this page. Increases as you click Load More. This is not a business-wide total until all lists have been loaded.

elements.name: KPI — Active lists
elements.description: Count of ACTIVE price lists among those currently loaded. Note that status does not prevent a list from being applied at POS — see Warnings.

elements.name: KPI — Price overrides
elements.description: Total number of individual variant price entries across all currently loaded price lists. This count grows as you Load More lists. Use this to gauge how many price overrides are in use across your catalogue.

elements.name: KPI — Variant pool
elements.description: Total number of product variants available to assign prices to. Loaded once when the page opens (up to 200 variants). This reflects your full catalogue size.

elements.name: Open Wizard button
elements.description: Opens the Bulk Update Wizard — a 4-step guided flow that lets you apply a percentage, fixed-amount, or flat-rate price adjustment across an entire price list or just its existing items. Use this instead of updating prices one variant at a time.

elements.name: Name field (create form)
elements.description: Required. The display name for the new price list — for example 'Wholesale', 'VIP', or 'Branch B Prices'. This name appears in customer and branch assignment selectors.

elements.name: Create button
elements.description: Creates the new price list in ACTIVE status. The list appears immediately in the list below with no price items yet. Requires the price-lists.manage permission.

elements.name: Price list selector (item form)
elements.description: Choose which price list to add or update a price on. All loaded price lists are available. Select this first before choosing a variant and entering a price.

elements.name: Variant selector (item form)
elements.description: Choose the product variant to set a custom price for. Supports live search. If the variant already has a price on the selected list, saving will overwrite that price.

elements.name: Price input (item form)
elements.description: The custom price to assign for this variant on the selected list. Entered in your business currency. Must be greater than zero. This price overrides the product's default price at POS when this list is active for a customer or branch.

elements.name: Save Price button
elements.description: Saves the variant price to the selected list. If a price already exists for that variant on that list it is overwritten silently — no confirmation is shown. Requires the price-lists.manage permission.

elements.name: Price list card
elements.description: Displays a price list with its name, status badge, and all current price overrides listed inline (variant name · price). Cards are ordered newest first. Lists with no overrides show a 'No overrides' message.

elements.name: Edit button (per list)
elements.description: Opens an inline edit form for that list's name and status. Use this to rename a list or change it to INACTIVE or ARCHIVED. Requires the price-lists.manage permission.

elements.name: Name field (edit mode)
elements.description: Editable name field for the price list. Change this to rename the list across all customers and branches that have it assigned.

elements.name: Status selector (edit mode)
elements.description: Change the price list status to ACTIVE, INACTIVE, or ARCHIVED. Status is organisational only — it does not prevent the list from being applied at POS. To stop a list from being used at POS, you must unassign it from every customer and branch that references it.

elements.name: Save / Cancel buttons (edit mode)
elements.description: Save commits the name and status changes immediately. Cancel discards all changes and closes the inline edit form.

elements.name: Remove button (per price item)
elements.description: Deletes a single variant price entry from the list. The variant reverts to its default product price at POS. The removal is immediate — no confirmation dialog is shown.

elements.name: Load More button
elements.description: Appends the next 25 price lists to the page. There are no search or filter controls on this page — use Load More to browse all lists if you have more than 25.

elements.name: CollapsibleSection (New Price List form)
elements.description: The create form is hidden behind a 'New Price List' button. Closes after creation.

elements.name: Table view (ViewToggle)
elements.description: Toggle between card and table views. Table shows columns: Name, Status, Item Count, Customer Count, and Actions.

elements.name: Undo last change
elements.description: Reverts the most recent price change or item addition to a price list. Only available immediately after a change is made.

elements.name: Archive list action
elements.description: Archives a price list, making it inactive. Archived lists no longer apply to customers or branches.

elements.name: Default vs list price comparison
elements.description: For each item in a price list, shows both the default variant price (with strikethrough) and the list override price side by side for easy comparison.

elements.name: Variant image thumbnails
elements.description: Each price list item shows a small variant image for visual identification.

elements.name: Customer count per list
elements.description: Shows how many customers are assigned to each price list.

common_tasks.task: Create a new price list
steps: Enter a name in the Name field (e.g. 'Wholesale' or 'VIP').
Click Create. The list is created in ACTIVE status with no price items yet.
Add variant prices using the item form below, or use the Bulk Update Wizard for many variants at once.

common_tasks.task: Set a custom price for a product variant
steps: In the item form, select the price list from the Price list selector.
Select the product variant from the Variant selector.
Enter the custom price in the Price input.
Click Save Price. The price is saved immediately. If the variant already had a price on this list, it is overwritten.

common_tasks.task: Edit a price list name or status
steps: Find the price list card and click Edit.
Update the name or change the status.
Click Save. The name change takes effect immediately across all assignments.

common_tasks.task: Remove a price override
steps: Find the price list card containing the variant price you want to remove.
Click Remove next to that variant. The price is deleted immediately.
The variant will now use its default product price at POS for this list.

common_tasks.task: Bulk-adjust prices using the Wizard
steps: Click Open Wizard in the page header.
Step 1 — Scope: select the price list and choose ALL variants or EXISTING items only.
Step 2 — Adjust: choose an adjustment mode (% change, fixed add/subtract, or flat SET) and enter the value.
Step 3 — Preview: review the current → new price for up to 12 variants. Click Show More to expand. Go Back to adjust if needed.
Step 4 — Apply: confirm the count and click Apply Now. Prices are written in batches of 20 — do not navigate away until complete.

warnings: Setting a price for a variant that already exists on the list overwrites it immediately with no confirmation. Double-check the selected list and variant before clicking Save Price.

Price list status (ACTIVE, INACTIVE, ARCHIVED) has no effect on POS pricing. If a customer or branch has a list assigned, its prices are applied regardless of the list's status. To stop a list from being used, you must unassign it from every customer and branch that references it.
The price-lists.manage permission is required to view this page. Staff without it will not see Price Lists in the navigation at all.
The KPI — Price overrides count reflects only the lists currently loaded on the page. Click Load More to load additional lists and see a more complete count.

related_pages.reason: Assign price lists to customers so the POS applies their negotiated prices automatically.
Assign a default price list to a branch to apply it for all sales at that branch.
Where price list prices are applied automatically during a sale.

entries.title: Bulk Price Update Wizard

related_pages.reason: Create and manage price lists before running the bulk update wizard.

entries.overview: A four-step guided wizard for bulk-updating prices across an entire price list. Step 1 (Scope) selects the target list and whether to affect all variants or only existing items. Step 2 (Adjust) chooses the pricing strategy — percentage change, fixed add/subtract, or exact price override — and enters the value. Step 3 (Preview) is the star step: a financial impact dashboard with four stat cards (total affected, increases, decreases, average change), a Doughnut chart showing impact distribution, and a scrollable preview table where each row shows the variant name, a MetricComparison (old price arrow new price, color-coded), and a percentage change badge. Step 4 (Apply) shows a summary, a live batch progress bar, and success/failure counts after completion.

elements.name: PageHeader (hero)
elements.description: Page hero with eyebrow 'Pricing control', title 'Bulk price update wizard', subtitle, and two badges showing 'Bulk update' and the current step name. An 'ArrowLeft Back to price lists' link returns to the main price lists page.

elements.name: KPI strip (4 cards)
elements.description: Four KPI cards across the top: Current Step (ClipboardList icon, shows step name), Target Variants (Package icon, amber, shows count), Preview Rows (Eye icon, blue, shows count), Selected List (ListOrdered icon, emerald, shows list name). Each card has an icon container and hover animation.

elements.name: WizardSteps + ProgressBar
elements.description: A Card containing a ProgressBar (step X of 4 as percentage) and the WizardSteps component showing all four steps with numbered pills. Completed steps show checkmarks, the active step is highlighted.

elements.name: Scope step — List selector
elements.description: SmartSelect dropdown to choose which price list to update. A label with ListOrdered icon appears above it.

elements.name: Scope step — Option cards (ALL vs EXISTING)
elements.description: Two prominent option cards in a 2-column grid. ALL (Layers icon): 'Apply pricing changes to every variant in your catalogue, including those not yet on this list.' EXISTING (ListFilter icon): 'Only update variants that already have a price override on this list.' Selected card has gold border, ring glow, and tinted background. Both cards use nvi-press tactile click animation.

elements.name: Adjust step — Mode cards (PERCENT / ADD / SET)
elements.description: Three option cards in a 3-column grid, each centered with an icon, title, and description. PERCENT (Percent icon): 'Change by percentage'. ADD (Plus icon): 'Add or subtract a fixed amount'. SET (DollarSign icon): 'Set an exact price for all'. Selected card has gold border and ring glow.

elements.name: Adjust step — Value input
elements.description: CurrencyInput field for the adjustment value with nvi-focus-pulse animation on first focus. Below the input, a hint appears showing 'This will affect X variants' with an Info icon (animated with nvi-bounce-in).

elements.name: Preview step — Impact stat boxes (4 cards)
elements.description: Four impact metric cards in a row: Variants affected (Package icon, amber), Price increases (TrendingUp icon, red), Price decreases (TrendingDown icon, emerald), Average change (Percent icon, blue, shows both % and absolute amount). Cards use nvi-stagger entrance and nvi-card-hover.

elements.name: Preview step — Doughnut chart
elements.description: Card with 'Impact distribution' header (PieChart icon). Contains the Doughnut chart visualizing increases (green), decreases (red), and unchanged (grey) proportions. Legend at bottom.

elements.name: Preview step — Preview table
elements.description: Scrollable list of variant rows inside a Card. Each row shows variant name, MetricComparison (old price strikethrough arrow new price, color-coded up=emerald down=red), and a percentage change badge (red for increases, emerald for decreases, animated with nvi-bounce-in). Rows have colored borders — red-tinted for increases, emerald-tinted for decreases. New rows appear with nvi-stagger animation.

elements.name: Show More button (Preview)
elements.description: Centered button with ChevronDown icon, rounded-xl with nvi-press. Loads 12 more rows into the preview table. Only visible when there are more variants than currently shown.

elements.name: Apply step — Summary card
elements.description: Gold-tinted summary card with FileCheck icon showing 'Applying changes to X variants on [list name]'.

elements.name: Apply step — Batch progress bar
elements.description: Visible during application. Shows a ProgressBar tracking batch processing progress (e.g. '3 / 5' batches completed). Updates in real time as each batch of 20 variants completes.

elements.name: Apply step — Result summary
elements.description: Appears after application completes. On full success: green-tinted card with CheckCircle icon showing 'X prices updated successfully' plus nvi-save-flash green pulse animation on the entire step card. On partial failure: amber-tinted card with AlertTriangle icon showing 'X updated, Y failed'.

elements.name: Apply Now button
elements.description: Gold CTA with Zap icon, rounded-xl with nvi-press. Shows a Spinner and 'Applying...' text while processing. Disabled without price-lists.manage permission (shows tooltip). Cannot be clicked while already applying.

elements.name: Back / Next buttons
elements.description: All navigation buttons use nvi-press animation and rounded-xl styling. Back has ArrowLeft icon, outlined style. Next has ArrowRight icon, gold filled style. Both navigate between wizard steps without losing data.

before_you_start.text: You need the price-lists.manage permission to access and use this wizard.
A price list must already exist. Create one from the Price Lists page first.
If using EXISTING scope, the list must already have at least one variant price override on it — otherwise nothing is updated.

common_tasks.task: Increase all prices on a list by 10%
steps: On the Scope step, select the price list from the dropdown.
Choose the ALL scope card to include every variant in your catalogue.
Click Next (ArrowRight). On the Adjust step, select the PERCENT card and enter 10.
Click Next. Review the impact dashboard — check the stat boxes and scroll through the preview table to confirm prices are correct.
Click Next to reach the Apply step. Review the summary, then click Apply Now.

common_tasks.task: Reduce existing list items by a flat amount
steps: Select the list and choose the EXISTING scope card, then click Next.
Select the ADD card and enter the amount as a negative number (e.g. -500 to reduce each price by 500).
Click Next. Review the preview table — decreases show in emerald with green badges.
Click Next and then Apply Now. Watch the batch progress bar as changes are committed.

warnings: Applying the wizard is permanent and cannot be undone. Take note of current prices or export them before running the wizard if you need a backup.
ALL scope with SET mode will set every variant in your catalogue to the same fixed price — this is almost never what you want. Use EXISTING scope or PERCENT/ADD mode to avoid unintended overwrites.
Percentage adjustments on very small prices may produce unexpected results due to rounding. Always review the full Preview table before clicking Apply.
Changes are applied in batches of 20. If the browser is closed mid-apply, some variants may be updated while others are not. Wait for the progress bar to complete.

entries.title: Purchase Orders
entries.overview: Create, update, approve, and track purchase orders for stock replenishment. The page combines a filterable, paginated order list with an inline Create panel and an inline Update panel — no navigation required for day-to-day operations. Orders move through a defined status lifecycle: DRAFT → PENDING_APPROVAL → APPROVED → PARTIALLY_RECEIVED → FULLY_RECEIVED → CLOSED. An approval workflow is enforced when the order value exceeds the approving user's authority threshold. A separate wizard is available for guided multi-step order entry via the Open Wizard link in the page header.
before_you_start: You need the Purchases Read permission to view orders and Purchases Write permission to create, update, or approve them.
At least one active supplier and one branch must exist before you can create a purchase order. Inactive suppliers are rejected by the backend.

Approvals may be required depending on the order value and your role's approval threshold. When triggered, an approval notice banner appears and the order moves to PENDING_APPROVAL status — a manager must approve it before it can be received.

elements.label: Open Orders
elements.description: Count of purchase orders loaded in the current page view.

elements.label: Pending Approval
elements.description: Count of orders currently in PENDING_APPROVAL status on this page. These orders are blocked from receiving until a manager approves them.

elements.label: Approved & In Flow
elements.description: Count of orders in APPROVED, PARTIALLY_RECEIVED, or FULLY_RECEIVED status — orders that have been cleared and are in the receiving pipeline.

elements.label: Due in 7 Days
elements.description: Count of orders whose Expected Date falls within the next 7 days. Use this to prioritise chasing suppliers for imminent deliveries.

elements.label: Search
elements.description: Free-text search across order ID, supplier name, and branch name. Debounced 350 ms. URL-persisted.

elements.label: Status Filter
elements.description: Filter by order status: All, DRAFT, PENDING_APPROVAL, APPROVED, PARTIALLY_RECEIVED, FULLY_RECEIVED, CLOSED, or CANCELLED.

elements.label: Branch Filter
elements.description: Filter orders by receiving branch. Defaults to the active global branch. Set to All Branches to see orders across all locations.

elements.label: Supplier Filter
elements.description: Filter orders from a specific supplier. Populated from all suppliers on the business.

elements.label: From Date / To Date
elements.description: Date range filter on order creation date. Both fields are optional — use either independently or together to isolate a time window.

elements.label: Reset Filters / Advanced Toggle
elements.description: Reset Filters clears all active filters and returns to the default view. Advanced toggle shows or hides the Status, Branch, Supplier, and date filter controls.

elements.label: View Toggle (Cards / Table)
elements.description: Switches the order list between card view (default) and table view. Cards show full order detail including line items and the Approve button. Table shows Supplier, Branch, Status, and Expected Date in a compact grid.

elements.label: Create Order — Branch, Supplier, Expected Date
elements.description: Three fields at the top of the Create panel. Branch (required, defaults to active branch) and Supplier (required — shows name and status; inactive suppliers are blocked). Expected Date is optional — if left blank and the supplier has a lead time set, the backend auto-calculates the expected arrival date. When a supplier with a lead time is selected, a hint shows the days and estimated arrival date.

elements.label: Use Reorder Suggestions
elements.description: Fetches reorder suggestions for the selected branch and fills all order lines with the suggested variants, quantities, and default costs. Disabled until a branch is selected and suggestions are available. The suggestion count is shown next to the button.

elements.label: Create Order Lines
elements.description: Repeatable line rows for the new order. Each line has: Variant (async searchable, pre-loaded from up to 200 variants — unit auto-fills from the variant's sell unit), Quantity, Unit, and Unit Cost. The Remove button deletes a line. Incomplete lines (missing variant, quantity, or cost) are silently skipped at submission.

elements.label: Add Line / Create Order
elements.description: Add Line appends a new blank line row. Create Order submits the form — disabled without Purchases Write permission. Shows a spinner while in flight. On success the form resets and the list reloads. If the backend requires approval, an approval notice banner appears instead of a standard toast.

elements.label: Update Order — Order Picker & Expected Date
elements.description: Select an order from the current list to update. Orders are shown as 'Supplier • Date • Status'. A second date field sets the new Expected Date. When an APPROVED order is selected, a hint warns that saving will trigger an approval request for the edit.

elements.label: Update Order Lines
elements.description: Same line structure as create (Variant, Quantity, Unit, Unit Cost, Remove). All current lines must be re-submitted — the backend replaces them. You can add new variant lines but cannot remove existing ones (backend throws a 400 error if any existing variant is omitted from the payload).

elements.label: Update Order
elements.description: Submits the update. Disabled without Purchases Write permission. Shows a spinner while in flight. Cannot update orders in FULLY_RECEIVED, PARTIALLY_RECEIVED, CANCELLED, or CLOSED status — these return a 400 error. If the order is APPROVED, an approval request is raised and the approval notice banner appears if required.

elements.label: Approve
elements.description: Shown on each DRAFT or PENDING_APPROVAL order card (card view only). Triggers the approval workflow. If the user's role has sufficient authority, the order is immediately set to APPROVED and a success toast appears. If approval is required from a manager, the approval notice banner appears with the approval ID and the order moves to PENDING_APPROVAL.

elements.label: Order List
elements.description: The main data view. Card view shows supplier, status badge (colour-coded), branch, creation date, expected date, Approve button (on eligible orders), and line items (variant · quantity unit · unit cost). Table view shows Supplier, Branch, Status, and Expected Date columns. Orders are ordered by creation date descending.

elements.label: Pagination Controls
elements.description: Page number navigation with a page size selector. Total order count is shown (fetched on page 1). Uses cursor-based paging internally but presents numbered pages to the user. Changing filters resets to page 1.

elements.label: Open Wizard
elements.description: Link in the page header that navigates to the Purchase Orders Wizard — a guided multi-step flow for creating an order with more structure and validation feedback than the inline Create panel.

elements.name: Create order button (page header)
elements.description: Header action button with Plus icon. Opens the Create Purchase Order modal. Only shown to users with the purchases.write permission.

elements.name: Create Purchase Order modal
elements.description: Centered modal dialog opened from the Create order header button. Contains branch and supplier selectors, expected delivery date, line items, and — when reorder suggestions are available for the selected branch — an inline panel showing low-stock variants with a 'Use Suggestions' button to populate the line items. ESC or backdrop click closes without saving. Closes automatically after a successful create.

elements.name: Edit Purchase Order modal
elements.description: Centered modal dialog opened by clicking the Edit (Pencil) action on a DRAFT order row. Existing line items and expected date are pre-populated. A Banner info notice appears when the edit may require re-approval. ESC or backdrop click closes without saving.

elements.name: Reorder Suggestions section
elements.description: An expandable panel showing variants that are below their reorder point. Click 'Use Suggestions' to pre-populate a new purchase order with suggested items and quantities.

elements.name: Overdue PO highlighting
elements.description: Purchase orders past their expected delivery date that haven't been received are highlighted in red. Helps identify orders that need follow-up with suppliers.

elements.name: Supplier lead time indicator
elements.description: Shows the supplier's configured lead time next to the expected delivery date. If no lead time is set, a warning hint appears.

elements.name: StatusBadge
elements.description: Coloured status pills: DRAFT, PENDING, APPROVED, PARTIAL, RECEIVED, CLOSED, CANCELLED.

elements.name: SortableTableHeader
elements.description: Clickable column headers for sorting by Supplier, Branch, Status, Expected Date, Items, and Total.

elements.name: DueIn7Days KPI
elements.description: A KPI card showing how many purchase orders are expected to arrive within the next 7 days.

elements.name: Reference number
elements.description: Each purchase order has an auto-generated reference number (e.g. PO-001). Shown in order labels, cards, and table rows for easy identification and search.

common_tasks.title: Create a new purchase order
steps: In the Create panel, select the receiving Branch and the Supplier.
Optionally set an Expected Date. If the supplier has a lead time, the hint shows the estimated arrival.
Optionally click Use Reorder Suggestions to auto-fill lines from stock reorder recommendations.
For each line, select the Variant, enter Quantity and Unit Cost, and confirm the Unit.
Click Add Line to add more lines as needed.
Click Create Order. The order is created in DRAFT status. If an approval is required, the approval notice banner appears with the approval ID.

common_tasks.title: Approve a draft purchase order
steps: Find the order in the list (filter by Status = DRAFT or PENDING_APPROVAL if needed).
In card view, click Approve on the order card.
If your role has sufficient authority, the order moves to APPROVED immediately and a success toast appears.
If approval is required from a manager, the banner shows the approval ID — share it with the manager to action in the Approvals page.

common_tasks.title: Update lines or expected date on an existing order
steps: In the Update panel, select the order from the Order Picker dropdown.
Adjust the Expected Date if needed.
Re-enter all lines — existing lines can have quantities and costs changed; new variants can be added. Note: you cannot remove existing lines.
Click Update Order. If the order is APPROVED, an approval request is raised automatically.

common_tasks.title: Use reorder suggestions to fill an order
steps: Select a Branch in the Create panel — suggestions load automatically for that branch.
Wait for the suggestion count to appear next to the Use Reorder Suggestions button.
Click Use Reorder Suggestions. All line fields are filled with suggested variants, quantities, and default costs.
Review and adjust individual lines as needed before clicking Create Order.

common_tasks.title: Find all orders awaiting approval
steps: Click Advanced to reveal filters.
Select PENDING_APPROVAL from the Status filter.
The list shows only orders awaiting a manager decision. Check the Pending Approval KPI for the count.
