common_tasks.title: Identify orders due for delivery within the week
steps: Check the Due in 7 Days KPI card — it shows the count of orders with an expected date within 7 days.
Use the From Date and To Date filters to narrow the list to the relevant date window.
Contact suppliers for orders that are due but still in APPROVED or PARTIALLY_RECEIVED status.

warnings: Removing lines from an existing purchase order is permanently blocked — the backend rejects any update that omits a variant already on the order. To correct an over-ordered line, set the quantity to the correct value rather than removing it.
Editing an APPROVED order always triggers an approval request, even if the edit is minor. The order remains APPROVED during the approval review — it does not revert to DRAFT.
Inactive suppliers cannot be used to create purchase orders. The backend rejects the request with an error. Reactivate the supplier first if needed.

The Expected Date is not validated against the supplier lead time — you can set any date. If left blank, the backend auto-populates it from the supplier's lead time days. This auto-populated date is not shown in the UI until after the order is saved.

entries.title: Purchase Order Wizard
entries.overview: A 4-step guided form to create a purchase order and optionally receive the goods in the same session. The wizard covers: selecting a supplier and branch, adding order lines (product, quantity, unit cost), reviewing totals, then recording received quantities with optional batch codes and expiry dates. If the order total triggers an approval threshold, the PO is created but stock is held until an approver acts.

elements.name: Step indicator
elements.description: Shows your current position across the four steps: Details → Lines → Review → Receive. Completed steps show a checkmark. You can click Back to return to a previous step before the PO is created — once you click Create PO on the Review step, earlier steps are locked.

elements.name: Branch selector (Details step)
elements.description: The branch where the ordered goods will be received. Stock is added to this branch when the order is received. You cannot change the branch after the PO is created — choose carefully.

elements.name: Supplier selector
elements.description: The supplier this order is being placed with. Only ACTIVE suppliers appear. If the supplier has a lead time configured, the Expected date field will show a suggested ETA automatically.

elements.name: Expected date
elements.description: The date you expect goods to arrive. If the selected supplier has a lead time set, a suggested ETA (today + lead time days) appears automatically — adjust it if your agreed delivery date is different. This date is informational and does not trigger automatic actions.

elements.name: Line row (Lines step)
elements.description: One line per product being ordered. For each line, choose the variant (what you are ordering), enter the quantity, the unit cost (the price your supplier charges per unit), and the unit of measure. Add as many lines as needed for a single supplier order.

elements.name: Add Line button
elements.description: Adds a new blank line to the order. Use this when ordering multiple products from the same supplier in a single purchase order — one line per variant.

elements.name: Remove Line button
elements.description: Removes that line from the order before it is created. Once the PO is created on the Review step, lines cannot be removed.

elements.name: Review step
elements.description: Displays a full summary of all order lines with quantities, unit costs, line totals, and the overall order total. Review every line carefully — clicking Create PO on this step creates the purchase order permanently and locks all lines.

elements.name: Create PO button
elements.description: Creates the purchase order in the system. After clicking, the PO is locked — quantities, costs, and lines cannot be changed. The wizard advances automatically to the Receive step.

elements.name: Receive step
elements.description: After the PO is created, record the actual quantities delivered. The ordered quantities are pre-filled — adjust each to match what actually arrived. If goods are partially delivered, enter only the received amounts; the remaining balance stays open on the PO for a future receiving session.

elements.name: Batch code input (per line)
elements.description: Record the batch or lot number from the supplier's packaging. Required if batch tracking is enabled in Business Settings. Batch codes let you trace exactly which batch of stock was sold or returned later — critical for perishable goods and regulated items.

elements.name: Expiry date input (per line)
elements.description: Record the expiry date for perishable or regulated items (food, medicines, chemicals). Required if the variant has expiry tracking enabled. Items with different expiry dates from the same delivery should be split into separate lines.

elements.name: Receive button
elements.description: Records all entered quantities as received and updates stock levels at the destination branch immediately. If the PO total triggers an approval threshold, the approval banner appears and stock is held until an approver acts — clicking Receive still submits the request.

elements.name: Approval notice banner
elements.description: Appears when the PO total exceeds the approval threshold configured in Business Settings. The PO is created successfully, but stock will not be updated until a user with the approvals.write permission approves it on the Approvals page.

elements.name: Back / Next buttons
elements.description: Navigate between wizard steps. Back returns to the previous step without losing your current step's data. Note: once you click Create PO on the Review step, you cannot go back to change order lines.

elements.name: Lead time ETA display
elements.description: Shows the estimated arrival date calculated from the supplier's lead time. If no lead time is configured, shows a hint to set one.

elements.name: Reorder suggestions link
elements.description: A link within the wizard to view reorder suggestions based on current stock levels and reorder points.

elements.name: Variant images in lines
elements.description: Each line item in the Lines step shows the variant's product image as a small thumbnail for visual identification.

elements.name: Batch and expiry in receive step
elements.description: When receiving stock in the final step, you can enter batch codes and expiry dates for each line item.

elements.name: Finish Later button
elements.description: Save the current wizard progress and return to the Purchase Orders list. You can resume later.

elements.name: Visual progress bar
elements.description: A step indicator at the top showing your progress through the wizard steps with visual dots and connecting lines.

before_you_start.text: You need the purchases.write permission.
The supplier and destination branch must already exist — create them first if needed.
Enable batch tracking in Business Settings if you need to record batch codes and expiry dates on received goods.

common_tasks.task: Create a purchase order
steps: On the Details step, select the branch where goods will be received, choose the supplier, and set the expected delivery date. Click Next.
On the Lines step, add one line per product: select the variant, enter quantity, unit cost, and unit of measure. Click Next.
On the Review step, check all quantities, costs, and the total. Click Create PO to lock the order.
The wizard advances to the Receive step — receive immediately or skip to receive later from the Receiving page.

common_tasks.task: Receive goods immediately after creating the PO
steps: After creating the PO, you are on the Receive step automatically.
Adjust each quantity to match what actually arrived (the ordered quantity is pre-filled).
If batch tracking is enabled, enter the batch code and expiry date for each line.
Click Receive. Stock levels at the branch update immediately.

common_tasks.task: Handle a partial delivery
steps: On the Receive step, enter only the quantities that actually arrived for each line — leave lines at 0 if nothing arrived for that item.
Click Receive to update stock for the delivered portion.
Return to the Receiving page later to record the remaining goods when they arrive — the PO stays open.

warnings: If an approval is triggered, stock will not be added to the branch until the approval is granted on the Approvals page. The PO shows as PENDING in the meantime.
Unit cost entered here forms the cost basis for stock valuation and profit calculations in reports. Enter the correct supplier price — incorrect values affect margin reporting.
The PO cannot be edited after creation. If you made an error (wrong supplier, wrong branch, wrong cost), contact your supervisor — the PO may need to be voided and a new one raised.

entries.title: Purchases
entries.overview: Record supplier purchases, track payment progress, and manage your buying activity. Each purchase card shows cost as the hero number, supplier details, payment status with visual indicators (green check for paid, amber progress bar for partial, red circle for unpaid), and line item summaries. Payment recording is inline on each card. Works offline: purchases recorded without internet sync automatically when you reconnect.

before_you_start.text: At least one active supplier must exist before you can record a purchase.
At least one branch must be configured for stock movements to be attributed correctly.
Your role needs purchases.write to create purchases or record payments, and purchases.read to view the list.

elements.name: Total Purchases KPI
elements.description: Count of purchases with ShoppingCart icon. On the first page load the API returns the true total.

elements.name: This Month Spend KPI
elements.description: Total spend for purchases created this calendar month with TrendingUp icon. Helps track buying velocity.

elements.name: Unpaid Balance KPI
elements.description: Sum of outstanding balances across visible purchases with AlertCircle icon. Shows how much you still owe suppliers.

elements.name: Active Branch KPI
elements.description: Currently scoped branch with Building2 icon. Displays 'All branches' if no branch filter is active.

elements.name: Search / Filters Bar
elements.description: ListFilters component with 350ms debounced search, status/branch/supplier selects, date range pickers, and advanced toggle.

elements.name: Create purchase button (page header)
elements.description: Header action button with ShoppingCart icon. Opens the Create purchase modal. Only shown to users with the purchases.write permission.

elements.name: Create purchase modal
elements.description: Centered modal dialog opened from the Create purchase header button (or from Reorder, which pre-fills supplier + lines). Sections: Basic Info + Line Items. ESC or backdrop click closes without saving.

elements.name: Purchase Card (card view)
elements.description: Transaction card: cost as hero number (large, bold, tabular-nums), reference below, supplier with Building2 icon, line summary with Package icon ('3 items, 150 units'), branch with MapPin, relative time with Clock, payment status indicator (green Check/amber ProgressBar/red AlertCircle), payment history pills with CreditCard icons, inline expandable payment form with nvi-expand animation.

elements.name: Payment Status Indicator
elements.description: Visual payment status replacing text labels: paid = green circle + Check icon, partial = amber ProgressBar + percentage, unpaid = red circle + AlertCircle icon.

elements.name: Record payment modal
elements.description: Centered modal dialog opened from the Record payment action on any unpaid purchase. Shows remaining balance in the header, method select, amount input, and conditional reference / custom method label fields.

elements.name: Table View
elements.description: Wrapped in Card. Columns: Supplier (Building2 icon + ref), Branch, Status (StatusBadge), Payment (colored dot + %), Date (relative time), Total (right-aligned). SortableTableHeaders on all. Shared inline payment panel at bottom.

elements.name: Empty State
elements.description: EmptyState with floating ShoppingCart icon (nvi-float animation).

elements.name: Reorder Button
elements.description: Opens the Create purchase modal pre-filled with same supplier and lines from a previous purchase.

elements.name: Offline PIN verification
elements.description: Card with Lock icon and TextInput. PIN gate for offline purchase creation.

elements.name: Banner (notifications)
elements.description: Replaces StatusBanner. Severity-based styling, dismiss button, optional action.

common_tasks.task: Create a direct purchase online
steps: In the Create Purchase form, select a branch and supplier.
Click Add Line and enter the variant, quantity, unit, and unit cost for each item on the invoice.
Click Create Purchase.
If approved immediately, the purchase appears in the list with APPROVED status — go to the Receiving page to receive the goods and update stock.

common_tasks.task: Queue a purchase offline
steps: When offline, open the Purchases page — an Offline Status Banner appears.
If a PIN is required, enter your offline PIN in the PIN gate panel and click Unlock.
Complete the Create Purchase form as normal.
Click Create Purchase — the record is saved locally as a PURCHASE_DRAFT and you see a 'Queued for sync' confirmation.
When you go back online, the queued purchase syncs automatically.

common_tasks.task: Record a payment against a purchase
steps: In the Record Payment form, open the Purchase Selector and choose the purchase you are paying.
Select the payment method.
Enter the amount — enter only the instalment amount for partial payments.
For Bank Transfer, enter the reference number (required). For Mobile Money, a reference is strongly recommended.
Optionally enter a Method Label for 'Other' payment types.
Click Record Payment.

common_tasks.task: Filter and locate a specific purchase
steps: Type a supplier name, branch name, or purchase ID into the Search Bar.
Use the Status Filter to narrow by lifecycle stage.
Click the advanced filter toggle to reveal Branch, Supplier, and Date Range filters.
Click Reset Filters to clear all active filters and return to the full list.

common_tasks.task: Handle an approval-required response
steps: If Create Purchase returns an approval notice, the purchase has NOT been created yet.
Note the approval ID shown in the response or in-app notification.
A manager with approvals.write permission must approve the request on the Approvals page.
Once approved, the purchase is created automatically — return here to find it in the list.

common_tasks.task: Switch between card and table views
steps: Use the layout toggle above the Purchases List to switch between card and table view.
Card view shows supplier, status, payments, and total at a glance.
Table view is more compact and useful for comparing multiple purchases quickly.

warnings: Creating a purchase does NOT update stock — stock only increases after you receive the goods on the Receiving page.
If the purchase total exceeds your role's approval threshold, the purchase is not created — the server returns approval required and a manager must approve it before the purchase is saved.
Offline purchases queue as PURCHASE_DRAFT and are not visible in the list until synced online. Do not clear browser storage before reconnecting.

Bank Transfer payments require a reference number at the server level — the payment will be rejected without one. Mobile Money reference is a frontend warning only and can be bypassed, but it is strongly recommended.
Inactive suppliers will cause the create request to fail even if they appear in the supplier dropdown — reactivate the supplier on the Suppliers page before recording a purchase.

related_pages.reason: Create and manage suppliers before recording purchases.
Use purchase orders to plan procurement before converting to a purchase.
Receive goods against this purchase here — stock is only updated after receiving.
Verify stock levels after receiving goods linked to this purchase.

entries.title: Receipts & Refunds

related_pages.reason: Receipts are created at the POS when a sale is completed — start there to process new sales.
Refunds and receipt-free returns may route to Approvals instead of completing immediately.
View a customer's credit balance and outstanding sales from the Customers page.

entries.overview: View the full history of completed sales receipts, reprint receipts, process refunds, settle outstanding credit balances, and record returns where the customer has no original receipt. The page has five distinct panels: receipt list, receipt preview, credit settlement, refund, and return-without-receipt. Refunds and receipt-free returns may be intercepted by the approval workflow and will not complete until a manager approves them.

elements.name: KPI — Receipt rows on this page
elements.description: Shows how many receipt records are loaded on the current page. This reflects the active filters and page size — not the total number of receipts in the business.

elements.name: KPI — Current page
elements.description: Shows which page of the receipt list you are on. Use the pagination controls at the bottom of the list to move between pages.

elements.name: KPI — Outstanding amount
elements.description: Shows the outstanding credit amount for the currently selected receipt. Displays zero when no receipt is selected. This is not a business-wide accounts-receivable total — it reflects only the one receipt you have open.

elements.name: KPI — Selected receipt
elements.description: Shows the receipt number of the currently selected receipt, or 'None' when no receipt is open. Selecting a receipt reveals the Preview, Settlement, and Refund panels below the list.

elements.name: Connect printer button
elements.description: Opens a browser prompt to pair with a USB ESC/POS receipt printer. Requires a WebUSB-capable browser — this works in Chrome and Edge on desktop only. Safari, Firefox, and mobile browsers cannot connect USB printers. Once connected, hardware printing replaces the browser print dialogue.

elements.name: Hardware print checkbox
elements.description: Toggles between hardware ESC/POS printing and the browser print dialogue. Only becomes active after a printer has been connected. Uncheck to revert to browser printing without disconnecting the printer.

elements.name: View toggle
elements.description: Switch between card layout (one row per receipt with receipt number, date, and action buttons) and table layout (more compact, better when scanning many receipts). Your preference is not saved between sessions.

elements.name: Search bar
elements.description: Filter the receipt list by keyword. Searches receipt number, sale ID, customer name, and the customer name and phone number snapshotted at the time of sale. Snapshot search means you can find a sale even if the customer record has been edited or anonymised after the sale. Updates after 350ms as you type.

elements.name: Branch filter
elements.description: Restrict the receipt list to a specific branch. Automatically respects your branch scope if you are limited to certain branches. Leave blank to see receipts across all branches you have access to.

elements.name: Payment method filter
elements.description: Filter receipts to a specific payment method used on the sale: CASH, CARD, MOBILE_MONEY, BANK_TRANSFER, or OTHER. A sale with mixed payments appears when any of its payments match the selected method.

elements.name: From date
elements.description: Start of the date range filter. Filters on the sale creation date — not the receipt issue date. Hidden inside the Advanced filter panel; click the Advanced toggle to reveal it.

elements.name: To date
elements.description: End of the date range filter. Use together with From date to restrict results to a specific reporting period. Note: if a sale was created just before midnight and the receipt issued just after, it may fall on a different date than expected.

elements.name: Advanced toggle
elements.description: Reveals or hides the date range and payment method filters. Click to expand when you need to narrow results by period or payment type.

elements.name: Reset button
elements.description: Clears all active filters (search, branch, payment method, date range) and reloads the full receipt list from page 1.

elements.name: Receipt card
elements.description: Displays a single receipt record showing the receipt number and issue date. In table view, also shows the sale total. Click the View button to open this receipt's full preview and action panels.

elements.name: View button
elements.description: Selects a receipt and opens the Preview, Settlement, and Refund panels below the list. Only one receipt can be selected at a time. Clicking View on a different receipt replaces the current selection.

elements.name: Reprint button
elements.description: Re-issues the receipt — logs a RECEIPT_REPRINT audit event, then either sends to the connected ESC/POS printer or triggers the browser print dialogue. Requires the sales.read permission. Reprinting does not create a new receipt record.

elements.name: Pagination controls
elements.description: Cursor-based page navigation for the receipt list. Default page size is 20. The true total count is loaded on page 1 and shown alongside the page controls. Use filters first to reduce the result set before paginating.

elements.name: Receipt preview panel
elements.description: Displays the full receipt for the selected sale, rendered as it would appear when printed. Shows business name, branch, cashier, customer, line items, VAT breakdown, discounts, payment methods, and totals. Appears only when a receipt is selected.

elements.name: Preview mode toggle
elements.description: Switch the receipt preview between Compact (abbreviated, similar to a thermal receipt) and Detailed (full breakdown including VAT, discounts, and payment split). Compact is useful for quick verification; Detailed is useful for disputes or audits.

elements.name: Settlement — payment method selector
elements.description: Choose how the customer is paying off their outstanding credit balance: CASH, CARD, MOBILE_MONEY, BANK_TRANSFER, or OTHER. Selecting OTHER reveals a method label field for a custom description.

elements.name: Settlement — amount input
elements.description: The amount being paid against the outstanding credit balance. Can be a partial payment — the remaining balance stays outstanding. The Settlement panel only appears when the selected receipt has an outstanding amount greater than zero.

elements.name: Settlement — method label input
elements.description: A free-text label for the payment method, only shown when OTHER is selected as the method. Use this to describe non-standard payment arrangements — for example 'Cheque' or 'Goods exchange'.

elements.name: Settlement — reference input
elements.description: Optional transaction reference for the settlement payment — for example a mobile money confirmation code or bank transfer reference. Stored with the settlement record for reconciliation.

elements.name: Record Settlement button
elements.description: Posts the credit settlement against the selected sale. Requires the sales.credit.settle permission. After recording, the outstanding amount updates immediately and the list reloads. Partial payments can be recorded multiple times until the balance is cleared.

elements.name: Refund — reason input
elements.description: Optional text describing why the refund is being issued. Stored with the refund record and appears in audit logs. Providing a reason is recommended for accountability, especially if the business has approval thresholds on refunds.

elements.name: Refund — return to stock checkbox
elements.description: When checked, the refunded items are added back to branch stock as a positive stock movement. When unchecked, stock is not restored — use this for damaged or unsellable goods. The default state follows the business POS policy setting.

elements.name: Refund Sale button
elements.description: Processes a full refund for the selected sale. Requires the sales.write permission. Only works on COMPLETED sales — you cannot refund a DRAFT, VOIDED, or already-REFUNDED sale. If the business has a refund approval threshold, clicking this routes the request to Approvals instead of completing it immediately.

elements.name: Return button (page header)
elements.description: Header action button with RotateCcw icon. Opens the Return Without Receipt dialog. Only shown to users who have the sales.return.without-receipt permission.

elements.name: Return without receipt — dialog
elements.description: Centered modal opened from the Return button in the page header. Requires the sales.return.without-receipt permission. Fields: Branch selector (required, auto-fills from your active branch), Customer search (optional async search), Reason input (optional), and Return to stock checkbox. May also trigger the approval workflow. ESC or backdrop click closes without submitting.

elements.name: Return item row
elements.description: A repeatable row within the return-without-receipt form. Each row has a variant search field (async, searches by product and variant name), a quantity input, and a unit price input. The unit price is used to calculate the refund value — enter the price the customer originally paid. Add as many rows as needed for multi-item returns.

elements.name: Add item button
elements.description: Adds a new empty return item row to the return-without-receipt form. Use this for returns involving multiple product variants. Requires the sales.return.without-receipt permission.

elements.name: Submit Return button
elements.description: Submits the return-without-receipt form. Requires the sales.return.without-receipt permission and at least one complete item row (variant + quantity + unit price all filled). If the business has a return approval threshold, this routes to Approvals instead of completing immediately.

elements.name: Preview mode toggle
elements.description: Toggle between Compact and Detailed receipt preview modes. Compact shows a summary; Detailed shows all line items, payments, and customer information.

elements.name: Settlement custom method label
elements.description: When the settlement payment method is 'OTHER', a text field appears to enter a custom label describing the payment method.

elements.name: Keyboard shortcuts (modals)
elements.description: Both the receipt detail modal and the Return Without Receipt dialog support ESC to close, backdrop click to close, and keep keyboard focus inside the dialog while it is open.

before_you_start.text: Permissions needed: sales.read to view and reprint receipts; sales.write to process refunds; sales.credit.settle to record credit settlements; sales.return.without-receipt to submit returns without an original receipt. Most users will need at least sales.read.
Receipts only exist for COMPLETED sales. Draft, voided, and pending-approval sales do not generate a receipt until they are completed at the POS.

common_tasks.task: Find and reprint a receipt by customer name or phone
steps: Type the customer's name or phone number into the Search bar.
The search matches against both the current customer record and the snapshot taken at the time of sale — so it works even if the customer's details have changed.
Click View on the receipt to open the receipt detail modal.
Click Reprint — the receipt prints to the connected ESC/POS printer, or the browser print dialogue opens if no hardware printer is connected.

common_tasks.task: Process a refund for a completed sale
steps: Find the receipt using search or filters and click View to open the receipt detail modal.
Scroll to the Refund section inside the modal.
Optionally enter a reason.
Decide whether to restore items to stock using the Return to stock checkbox.
Click Refund Sale.
If a warning banner appears saying approval is required, the refund has been sent to Approvals — no stock or financial change has been made yet.

common_tasks.task: Settle a customer's outstanding credit balance
steps: Find the receipt for the credit sale and click View to open the receipt detail modal.
If the sale has an outstanding balance, the Settlement section appears inside the modal.
Select the payment method and enter the amount being paid.
Optionally add a transaction reference.
Click Record Settlement. The outstanding balance updates immediately.

common_tasks.task: Record a return without a receipt
steps: Click the Return button in the page header to open the Return Without Receipt dialog.
Select the branch the return is being processed at.
Optionally link a customer using the customer search.
Add at least one item row: search for the variant, enter the quantity returned, and enter the unit price the customer originally paid.
Click Add item to add more rows if the customer is returning multiple products.
Click Submit Return. If approval is required, a warning banner appears and no stock change is made yet.

common_tasks.task: Filter receipts by payment method and date range
steps: Click the Advanced toggle to reveal the date and payment method filters.
Select the payment method (e.g. MOBILE_MONEY).
Set From date and To date to cover your reporting period.
Optionally also set a Branch filter.
The list updates to show only receipts matching all active filters.

common_tasks.task: Connect an ESC/POS printer for hardware receipt printing
steps: Ensure you are using Chrome or Edge on a desktop computer — WebUSB is not supported in Safari or Firefox.
Connect the USB receipt printer to your computer.
Click Connect printer. A browser prompt appears listing available USB devices.
Select your printer from the list and confirm.
Once connected, the Hardware print checkbox becomes active — ensure it is checked.
Click Reprint on any receipt to send it directly to the printer without opening a print dialogue.

warnings: The Outstanding amount KPI shows the credit balance of the currently selected receipt only — it reads zero when no receipt is selected. It is not a business-wide accounts-receivable figure.
The date range filter operates on the sale creation date, not the receipt issue date. For sales created just before midnight, the receipt may appear on the next calendar day in the list.
Refund Sale only works on COMPLETED sales. Attempting to refund a DRAFT, VOIDED, or already-REFUNDED sale will result in an error. Check the sale status at the POS before attempting a refund.

Both Refund Sale and Submit Return may be intercepted by the approval workflow if your business has refund or return approval thresholds configured. When this happens, a warning banner appears and the action is NOT completed — it moves to the Approvals page for a manager to approve or reject.

Hardware ESC/POS printing requires a WebUSB-capable browser. This means Chrome or Edge on desktop only. Safari, Firefox, and all mobile browsers cannot connect USB printers — use the browser print dialogue instead.

entries.title: Receiving
entries.overview: Record the arrival of goods against an existing purchase order or purchase. Use this page when goods arrive after you have already created the PO — as opposed to receiving goods immediately inside the Purchase Order Wizard. Supports partial deliveries (receive what arrived, leave the rest open), batch code tracking, and expiry date recording for perishable or regulated items.

elements.name: Link type selector
elements.description: Choose whether to receive goods against a Purchase Order or a Purchase. This controls which documents appear in the Document selector below. Most receiving is done against a Purchase Order.

elements.name: Document selector
elements.description: Select the specific purchase order or purchase you are receiving goods against. Only documents in a receivable state appear (e.g. approved POs with outstanding quantities). The document's supplier, branch, and order lines load automatically once you select it.

elements.name: Receiving line row
elements.description: One row per item being received. Shows the variant being received, a quantity field (pre-filled with the outstanding ordered quantity), the unit cost, and the unit of measure. Optionally shows batch code and expiry date fields if batch tracking is enabled. Adjust the quantity to match what actually arrived.

elements.name: Quantity received (per line)
elements.description: Enter the actual quantity delivered for this line. You can enter less than the outstanding quantity to record a partial delivery — the remaining balance stays open on the PO for a future receiving session. Enter 0 if nothing arrived for that item.

elements.name: Batch code input (per line)
elements.description: Enter the batch or lot number from the supplier's packaging label. Required if batch tracking is enabled in Business Settings. Batch codes let you trace which specific batch of stock was sold, transferred, or returned — critical for perishable goods and regulated items.

elements.name: Expiry date input (per line)
elements.description: Enter the expiry date for perishable or regulated items. Required if the variant has expiry tracking enabled. Stock with different expiry dates from the same delivery should be split into separate receiving lines so each batch has its own expiry recorded.

elements.name: Add Line button
elements.description: Adds a new receiving line for an additional item. Use this if you need to receive a variant that was not on the original purchase order — for example, a bonus item or a substitution sent by the supplier.

elements.name: Remove Line button
elements.description: Removes that line from the receiving session before you submit. Use this to correct a mistake — lines cannot be removed after submission.

elements.name: Submit button
elements.description: Records all receiving lines and updates stock levels at the branch immediately. This action cannot be undone — verify all quantities, batch codes, and expiry dates before clicking Submit.

elements.name: Approval notice banner
elements.description: Appears when the receiving total triggers an approval threshold configured in Business Settings. The receiving record is submitted, but stock levels are not updated until a user with the approvals.write permission approves it on the Approvals page.

elements.name: Receive stock button (page header)
elements.description: Header action button with PackageCheck icon. Opens the Receive stock modal. Only shown to users with the purchases.write permission.

elements.name: Receive stock modal
elements.description: Centered modal dialog opened from the Receive stock header button. Hosts the full receiving form (source type, document, override reason, line items with scanner). ESC or backdrop click closes without saving. Closes automatically after successful recording.

elements.name: Barcode scanner
elements.description: Scan barcodes to quickly find and add variants to the receiving form. Uses the device camera.

elements.name: Quality check notes
elements.description: An optional text field per receiving line for recording quality observations about the received goods.

elements.name: Dual-source display
elements.description: Each receiving entry shows its source: either a Purchase Order or a direct Purchase. The source type and reference are displayed as a badge.

elements.name: Override reason field
elements.description: When overriding the expected quantity, provide a reason for the discrepancy (e.g. partial delivery, damaged items).

elements.name: SortableTableHeader
elements.description: Clickable column headers for sorting by Variant, Quantity, Unit Cost, Source, and Date.

before_you_start.text: You need the purchases.write permission.
A purchase order or purchase must already exist and have outstanding quantities — you cannot receive against a PO that is already fully received or cancelled.
Enable batch tracking in Business Settings before receiving if you need to record batch codes and expiry dates.

common_tasks.task: Receive a full delivery against a purchase order
steps: Set Link type to Purchase Order and select the PO from the Document selector.
The lines from the PO are pre-filled with the outstanding quantities. Verify each quantity matches what arrived.
If batch tracking is enabled, enter batch codes and expiry dates for each line.
Click Submit. Stock levels at the branch update immediately.

common_tasks.task: Record a partial delivery
steps: Select the PO from the Document selector.
For lines that arrived, enter the actual quantity received. For lines that did not arrive, enter 0 or leave them.
Click Submit. The received quantities are added to stock. The PO stays open with the remaining balance for a future receiving session.

common_tasks.task: Receive an unlisted or bonus item
steps: Select the related PO or Purchase from the Document selector.
Click Add Line and select the variant that arrived.
Enter the quantity, unit cost, and any batch details.
Click Submit to record the receiving and update stock.

warnings: Submitted receiving records update stock immediately and cannot be edited or deleted. Verify all quantities, batch codes, and expiry dates before clicking Submit.
Partial receiving leaves the PO open — come back to this page and select the same PO when the remaining goods arrive to complete the receiving.
Batch codes must be unique within a branch and variant combination. If you enter a duplicate batch code, the system will warn you before allowing submission.

entries.title: Reports Dashboard

related_pages.reason: Sales data powers the Revenue, Cost, and VAT figures across all report sections.
Stock count records feed the variance data in the Operations section.
Adjustments produce the loss records shown in the top-losses panel.
Expenses appear in the P&L totals and the Expenses KPI card.
Customer names and balances appear in the Customers section customer sales and outstanding tables.

entries.overview: The Reports dashboard is the analytics centre of the business. It displays live financial, inventory, customer, and operational data grouped into five sections: Overview, Sales & Profit, Customers, Inventory, and Operations. A shared filter bar at the top of every section lets you narrow all data to a specific branch and/or date range. Five KPI cards — Revenue, Cost, Expenses, Net Profit, and VAT Total — are pinned at the top and update whenever the filters change. The page redirects automatically from /{locale}/reports to /{locale}/reports/overview on load; you can navigate to any section by clicking the section tabs.

before_you_start.text: You need the reports.read permission to view any section of the Reports dashboard.
Exporting the customer sales CSV also requires the customers.export permission. The Export CSV button is disabled if you do not have it.
All report data is live — it reflects the current state of your database at the moment you load or refresh the page. There is no caching layer.
If no data appears for a section, confirm that sales, stock counts, or other transactions have actually been recorded for the selected branch and date range.
The Low Stock threshold and Expiry look-ahead inputs are session-only — they reset to the defaults (5 and 30) when you reload the page.

common_tasks.task: Navigate to a specific report section
steps: Open the Reports page — the browser redirects automatically to the Overview section.
Click any of the five section tabs (Overview, Sales & Profit, Customers, Inventory, Operations) to switch sections.
The URL updates to /{locale}/reports/{section}. You can bookmark or share a section URL directly.

common_tasks.task: Filter all report data to a specific branch
steps: Find the Filters panel below the section tabs.
Open the Branch dropdown and select the branch you want to analyse.
All KPI cards and charts on the current section reload automatically.
To remove the filter and see all branches, open the dropdown again and clear the selection.

common_tasks.task: Filter report data by date range
steps: In the Filters panel, click the From Date input and pick a start date using the date picker.
Click the To Date input and pick an end date.
All data on the current section reloads automatically for that date window.
To clear the range, delete both date values — data reverts to all-time.

common_tasks.task: Refresh report data without changing filters
steps: Click the Refresh button on the right side of the Filters panel.
The button shows a spinner while reloading. All sections update in the background.
This is useful after recording a sale, completing a stock count, or posting an expense — the dashboard does not update in real time on its own.

common_tasks.task: Read the P&L breakdown (Sales & Profit section)
steps: Click the Sales & Profit tab.
The six summary stats at the top show Revenue, Cost, Expenses, Gross Profit, Losses, and Net Profit for the selected period.
The line chart below shows Revenue, Cost, and Net Profit plotted day by day.
The P&L Breakdown bar chart compares Gross Profit, Losses, Expenses, and Transfer Fees side by side.
The P&L by Day panel lists the last 7 days of net profit with colour-coded rows: green = profitable day, red = loss day.
The VAT Breakdown panel shows total VAT split by rate and by the last 7 days.

common_tasks.task: Find top-spending customers and export the data
steps: Click the Customers tab.
The Customer Sales table lists all customers ranked by total spend, with a transaction count.
To export the full list as a CSV file, click the Export CSV button in the top-right corner of the table. The download starts immediately.
Note: the Export CSV button requires the customers.export permission. If it is greyed out, ask an admin to grant that permission.

common_tasks.task: Identify products that are running low on stock
steps: Click the Inventory tab.
Find the Low Stock panel. By default it shows items with fewer than 5 units.
To adjust the threshold, change the number in the Threshold input next to the Low Stock heading. The table updates immediately.
The table shows Variant, Branch, and Quantity. Quantities of 1 or less are red; 2–3 are amber.
Use the Low Stock by Branch bar chart above to see which branch has the most low-stock items.

common_tasks.task: Check for batches expiring soon
steps: Click the Inventory tab.
Find the Expiry panel on the right. By default it shows batches expiring within 30 days.
To look further ahead, change the number in the Days input. The chart and list refresh.
The trend chart shows how many batches expire each day. The list below it shows up to 12 items with their expiry dates.

common_tasks.task: Review stock count discrepancies
steps: Click the Operations tab.
The Stock Count Variance table lists every completed count where the counted quantity differed from the expected quantity.
Positive variance (more than expected) is highlighted green. Negative variance (less than expected) is highlighted red.
Use the branch and date filters to narrow the results to a specific audit window.

common_tasks.task: Review cashier sales performance
steps: Click the Operations tab.
The Staff Performance bar chart shows the top 8 cashiers by total revenue sold.
Below the chart, the ranked list shows all staff with gold/silver/bronze medals for the top 3 and a progress bar showing their revenue relative to the top performer.

elements.name: Section tabs
elements.description: Five navigation buttons at the top of the page: Overview, Sales & Profit, Customers, Inventory, Operations. Clicking a tab loads that section's data and updates the URL. The active tab is highlighted with an accent border.
elements.notes: The URL changes to /{locale}/reports/{section}. You can link directly to a section.

elements.name: Branch filter
elements.description: Dropdown listing all branches in your business. Selecting a branch narrows every chart, table, and KPI card on the current section to data for that branch only. Leave blank to see all branches combined.
elements.notes: Branch filter changes trigger an automatic data reload. No need to click Refresh.

elements.name: Start date (From Date)
elements.description: Date picker input. Sets the beginning of the reporting period. All data on the page is filtered to transactions on or after this date.
elements.notes: Leave empty to include all historical data from the earliest record.

elements.name: End date (To Date)
elements.description: Date picker input. Sets the end of the reporting period. All data on the page is filtered to transactions on or before this date.
elements.notes: Leave empty to include data up to and including today.

elements.name: Refresh button
elements.description: Reloads all report data for the current section using the currently active filters. Shows a spinner while loading. Use this after recording new transactions to pull in the latest figures without changing the date range or branch.
elements.notes: Refreshing does not clear filters.

elements.name: Revenue KPI card
elements.description: Total gross sales revenue in the selected period and branch. This is the sum of all completed sale totals before deducting any costs.
elements.notes: Appears at the top of every section. Updates when filters change.

elements.name: Cost of Goods KPI card
elements.description: Total cost of goods sold (COGS) in the selected period. Calculated from the cost price recorded on each product variant at the time of sale.
elements.notes: Appears at the top of every section.

elements.name: Expenses KPI card
elements.description: Total expenses recorded in the selected period. Sourced from the Expenses module — miscellaneous business costs not tied to individual sales.
elements.notes: Appears at the top of every section.

elements.name: Net Profit KPI card
elements.description: Net profit = Revenue − Cost − Losses − Expenses − Transfer Fees. The card border and text turn green when positive and red when negative, making it easy to spot a loss period at a glance.
elements.notes: Appears at the top of every section. This is the single most important summary number on the dashboard.

elements.name: VAT Total KPI card
elements.description: Total VAT collected across all sales in the selected period. Useful for tax filing and for verifying that VAT rates are configured correctly on products.
elements.notes: Appears at the top of every section.

elements.name: Sales Trend line chart (Overview)
elements.description: Line chart in the Overview section plotting daily total revenue over the selected period. A live counter in the top-right corner shows the number of transactions included. Displays a 'no data' message if no sales exist for the selected filters.
elements.notes: Overview section only.

elements.name: Inventory Valuation bar chart (Overview)
elements.description: Horizontal bar chart in the Overview section showing the top stock items by current on-hand unit quantity. A total units counter is shown in the header. Useful for a quick snapshot of which variants hold the most stock.
elements.notes: Overview section only. Shows unit quantities, not monetary values.

elements.name: Revenue stat (Sales & Profit)
elements.description: Displays the total revenue figure for the selected period inside the P&L summary grid. Matches the Revenue KPI card value.
elements.notes: Sales & Profit section only.

elements.name: Cost stat (Sales & Profit)
elements.description: Displays the total cost of goods sold inside the P&L summary grid.
elements.notes: Sales & Profit section only.

elements.name: Gross Profit stat (Sales & Profit)
elements.description: Displays gross profit (Revenue − Cost) inside the P&L summary grid. This is the profit before expenses and losses are deducted.
elements.notes: Sales & Profit section only.

elements.name: Losses stat (Sales & Profit)
elements.description: Displays the total recorded stock losses in the selected period. Losses come from stock adjustments marked as losses or write-offs.
elements.notes: Sales & Profit section only. A high losses figure can indicate theft, damage, or miscounted stock.

elements.name: Expenses stat (Sales & Profit)
elements.description: Displays total expenses (from the Expenses module) for the selected period inside the P&L summary grid.
elements.notes: Sales & Profit section only.

elements.name: Net Profit stat (Sales & Profit)
elements.description: Displays the full net profit figure inside the P&L summary grid. Same calculation as the Net Profit KPI card at the top.
elements.notes: Sales & Profit section only.

elements.name: Revenue vs Cost vs Net Profit line chart (Sales & Profit)
elements.description: Three-line chart plotting Revenue (gold), Cost (grey), and Net Profit (green) on the same axis, day by day. Lets you see exactly when costs rose above revenue or when a big sale day occurred.
elements.notes: Sales & Profit section only. Shows 'no data' if no sales exist in the period.

elements.name: Stock Purchase Costs stat (Sales & Profit)
elements.description: Total cost of stock added through purchase-type adjustments (Unrecorded Purchase, Initial Stock) and opening stock imports during the selected period. This amount is deducted from gross profit in the net profit calculation.
elements.notes: Sales & Profit section only.

elements.name: P&L Breakdown bar chart (Sales & Profit)
elements.description: Bar chart comparing components side by side: Gross Profit (green), Losses (red), Stock Count Shortages (red), Stock Purchase Costs (amber), Expenses (amber), and Transfer Fees (grey). Useful for identifying which cost category is the largest drag on net profit.
elements.notes: Sales & Profit section only.

elements.name: VAT Breakdown panel (Sales & Profit)
elements.description: Two-column panel. The left column shows VAT collected by tax rate — each rate has a proportional bar showing its share of total VAT. The right column shows VAT collected for each of the last 7 days with proportional bars.
elements.notes: Sales & Profit section only. Only appears if VAT-rated products have been sold.

elements.name: P&L by Day table (Sales & Profit)
elements.description: Lists the last 7 days of P&L data. Each row shows a date, revenue, cost, and net profit. Rows with positive net profit have a green border and background; rows with negative net profit have a red border and background. A proportional bar fills each row to show relative scale.
elements.notes: Sales & Profit section only.

elements.name: Top Customers bar chart (Customers)
elements.description: Bar chart showing the top customers by total revenue spent in the selected period. Helps identify VIP customers and high-value accounts.
elements.notes: Customers section only. Shows 'no data' if no customer-linked sales exist.

elements.name: Sales vs Outstanding donut chart (Customers)
elements.description: Doughnut chart comparing total customer sales (green) against total outstanding (unpaid) balances (amber). The summary below the chart shows exact amounts. Useful for seeing what proportion of sales have been paid vs are still owed.
elements.notes: Customers section only.

elements.name: Customer Sales table (Customers)
elements.description: Paginated table listing all customers ranked by total spend. Columns: rank, customer name (with a proportional gold bar), transaction count, and total sales amount. Clicking through the pages shows lower-ranked customers.
elements.notes: Customers section only. Pagination controls appear below the table when results exceed the page size.

elements.name: Export CSV button (Customers)
elements.description: Downloads a CSV file of the customer sales data. The file includes customer name, transaction count, and total sales for the selected filters. Requires the customers.export permission — the button is greyed out and non-clickable without it.
elements.notes: Customers section only. The CSV reflects the current branch and date filters.

elements.name: Refunds by Customer list (Customers)
elements.description: Lists customers who have received refunds in the selected period, with the total refunded amount per customer. Shows a 'no refunds' message if there are none.
elements.notes: Customers section only.

elements.name: Outstanding Balances table (Customers)
elements.description: Paginated list of sales where an outstanding (credit) balance is still owed by the customer. Each row shows the customer name and the outstanding amount, with a heat-coloured bar — the more owed, the more intensely the bar is highlighted. Includes a total outstanding amount in the header.
elements.notes: Customers section only. Outstanding balances come from sales completed with an unpaid credit amount.

elements.name: Stock on Hand by Branch bar chart (Inventory)
elements.description: Bar chart showing the total stock on hand per branch (in units). Useful for comparing stock levels across locations and spotting branches that are over- or under-stocked.
elements.notes: Inventory section only. Shows 'no stock' message if no stock snapshots exist.

elements.name: Low Stock by Branch bar chart (Inventory)
elements.description: Bar chart showing the number of low-stock items per branch. An item counts as low-stock if its quantity is below the threshold value set in the Low Stock threshold input.
elements.notes: Inventory section only.

elements.name: Low stock threshold input (Inventory)
elements.description: Number input that sets the minimum acceptable stock quantity. Any variant with a quantity below this number appears in the Low Stock table. Default is 5. Changing the value re-queries the table immediately without a full page reload.
elements.notes: Inventory section only. Session-only — resets to 5 on page reload.

elements.name: Low Stock table (Inventory)
elements.description: Paginated table listing variants whose on-hand quantity is below the threshold. Columns: Variant name (product + variant), Branch, and Quantity. Quantity is colour-coded: red for 1 or less, amber for 2–3, white otherwise. Includes pagination controls when results exceed the page size.
elements.notes: Inventory section only.

elements.name: Expiry look-ahead input (Inventory)
elements.description: Number input that sets how many days ahead to search for expiring batches. Default is 30. Changing the value refreshes the expiry trend chart and list immediately.
elements.notes: Inventory section only. Session-only — resets to 30 on page reload. Only relevant if you use batch/expiry tracking on your products.

elements.name: Expiring Batches trend chart (Inventory)
elements.description: Line chart plotting how many batches expire on each day within the look-ahead window. Peaks in the chart indicate days with many simultaneous expirations — useful for planning stock rotation or reorders.
elements.notes: Inventory section only. Shows 'no data' if no expiring batches exist.

elements.name: Expiring Batches list (Inventory)
elements.description: Shows up to 12 batch records that are expiring within the look-ahead window. Each entry displays the variant name, the branch where the batch is held, and the expiry date. Ordered by nearest expiry first.
elements.notes: Inventory section only.

elements.name: Staff Performance bar chart (Operations)
elements.description: Bar chart in the Operations section showing the top 8 cashiers by total sales revenue in the selected period. Useful for comparing cashier output at a glance.
elements.notes: Operations section only.

elements.name: Stock Count Variance trend chart (Operations)
elements.description: Line chart plotting the net variance recorded by stock counts each day. A consistently negative variance trend may indicate systematic undercounting, theft, or stock recording errors.
elements.notes: Operations section only. Shows 'no data' if no stock counts have been completed.

elements.name: Stock Count Variance table (Operations)
elements.description: Paginated table listing every stock count record where the counted quantity differed from the expected quantity. Columns: Branch, Variant, Expected Qty, Counted Qty, and Variance. Variance is displayed in green (surplus) or red (deficit). If the count included a reason note, it appears next to the variance value.
elements.notes: Operations section only. Page size is adjustable using the pagination controls below the table.

elements.name: Staff Performance ranked list (Operations)
elements.description: Full ranked list of all cashiers with their total revenue. Each entry shows a rank number with gold (1st), silver (2nd), or bronze (3rd) colour coding, the cashier name, a proportional progress bar, and the total sales figure.
elements.notes: Operations section only.

elements.name: Top Losses panel (Operations)
elements.description: Shows the variants with the highest total recorded loss costs over the last 30 days (fixed window, not affected by date filters). Displays up to 6 items with a bar chart and a ranked list. Each entry shows the variant name, total cost of losses, and a proportional red bar. Useful for identifying high-value shrinkage problems.
elements.notes: Operations section only. The 30-day window is fixed and does not change with the date range filter.

warnings.text: Date range and branch filters are NOT saved between sessions. Every time you open the Reports page, filters reset to blank (all branches, all time). Set filters again each visit if you need a specific view.
The Low Stock threshold and Expiry look-ahead inputs reset to their defaults (5 and 30) on every page reload. They are not persisted.

The Top Losses panel always shows the last 30 days regardless of the date range filter. This is intentional — it gives a rolling recent-loss picture — but it means the date filter does not affect that panel.
If a sale was completed without a customer linked, that sale still appears in Revenue and P&L figures but is not reflected in the Customer Sales table or the Outstanding Balances list.

Report data is read-only. You cannot edit, delete, or correct transactions from the Reports page. Use the Sales, Purchases, Stock Adjustments, or Expenses pages to correct records, then refresh the report.

entries.title: Reports Dashboard
entries.overview: Business analytics dashboard with five reporting sections. Each section shows KPI cards and charts relevant to that area of the business. All data can be filtered by date range and branch. Use this page for performance reviews, end-of-period analysis, and operational monitoring.

elements.name: Section tabs
elements.description: Five tabs switch between reporting areas. Overview — business-wide summary of revenue, profit, transactions, and active customers for the period. Sales & Profit — revenue trends over time, gross margin analysis, top-selling products, and payment method breakdown. Customers — new vs returning customers, top customers by spend, outstanding credit balances, and customer activity trends. Inventory — current stock value by branch, stock movement summary (sales out, stock received, adjustments, transfers), and low-stock alerts. Operations — shift variance summary, transfer activity and status, approval queue totals, and expense breakdown by category.

elements.name: Date range filter
elements.description: Sets the reporting period for all KPI cards and charts on the current section. Changing the date range reloads all data. Common presets include Today, This Week, This Month, and Last Month — or set a custom From/To range for any period.

elements.name: Branch filter
elements.description: Restricts all report data to a specific branch. If no branch is selected, data from all branches is aggregated into a business-wide view. Use this to compare branches or review a specific location's performance.

elements.name: KPI cards (Overview section)
elements.description: Summary metrics for the selected period: total revenue, gross profit, net transactions, average sale value, and number of active customers. Use these as the headline figures for any performance review.

elements.name: KPI cards (Sales & Profit section)
elements.description: Revenue and margin metrics: total sales by period, gross margin percentage, cost of goods sold, and revenue from top product categories.

elements.name: KPI cards (Customers section)
elements.description: Customer activity metrics: total customers, new customers in the period, returning customer rate, and total outstanding credit balance across all customers.

elements.name: KPI cards (Inventory section)
elements.description: Stock health metrics: total stock value across all branches, number of variants with low or zero stock, total stock received in the period, and total stock adjustments.

elements.name: KPI cards (Operations section)
elements.description: Operational metrics: total shift variance (cash discrepancy across all closed shifts), number of pending approvals, total transfers in transit, and total expenses for the period.

elements.name: Charts (per section)
elements.description: Visual breakdowns that complement the KPI cards. Examples: revenue over time (line chart), top products by units sold (bar chart), stock movement categories (pie chart), customer spending tiers (bar chart). Charts update automatically when date range or branch filters change.

elements.name: Export report button
elements.description: Downloads the current section's data as a CSV file, reflecting the date range and branch filter currently applied. Available on most sections. Use this for sharing data with external tools or for deeper analysis in a spreadsheet.

elements.name: Export PDF button
elements.description: Generates a branded, print-ready PDF of the currently active section. The PDF includes a header with the business name, period, branch, and currency; the workspace KPI strip; and full detail tables for that section (top products, customer sales, low stock, expiring batches, staff performance, stock count variance, etc., depending on section). The library is loaded only when you click the button, so it does not slow down the page. Use this for board reports, audits, or sharing snapshots with people who do not log in to the system.

before_you_start.text: You need the reports.read permission to access the Reports dashboard.
Your subscription tier must include access to reports — lower tiers may have restricted access to some sections.
Data reflects completed transactions only — pending, draft, or approval-held records are not included in report totals.

common_tasks.task: View sales summary for the current month
steps: Go to the Sales & Profit section tab.
Set the date range to This Month.
Review the KPI cards for total revenue and gross margin, and the charts for revenue trends and top products.

common_tasks.task: Check stock value by branch
steps: Go to the Inventory section tab.
Set Branch filter to the branch you want to review (or leave blank for all branches).
The Stock Value KPI card shows the current total inventory value for the selected branch.

common_tasks.task: Export a report section to CSV
steps: Navigate to the section you want to export.
Set the date range and branch filter as needed.
Click Export report. A CSV file downloads to your device with the section's data.

warnings: Reports show data from completed transactions only — real-time figures at the POS may differ slightly from what appears here until transactions are fully processed.
Changing the date range or branch filter reloads all data automatically — give the page a moment to update before reading figures.

entries.title: Search
entries.overview: Global search that queries products, variants, receipts, customers, and stock transfers simultaneously from a single input. Type at least two characters to see live auto-suggest results. Submit the search to see full results grouped by entity type. Each result links directly to the relevant record — clicking it navigates you to that page.

elements.name: Search input
elements.description: Type your search query here. After two characters, live suggestions appear showing matching variant names, SKUs, customer names, and receipt numbers. Press Enter or click Search to run a full search across all entity types.

elements.name: Search button
elements.description: Runs a full search for the current query across all entity types. Use this when you want complete results beyond the auto-suggest preview, or after refining your query.

elements.name: Total matches count
elements.description: Total number of records matched across all entity types combined. If this is very high, refine your query to be more specific — for example, include a SKU or receipt number.

elements.name: Products section
elements.description: Lists products whose name matches the query, showing each product's name and its variants. Click a product to navigate to its catalogue detail page where you can view pricing, stock levels, and settings.

elements.name: Variants section
elements.description: Lists product variants matching the query by SKU or variant name. Click a variant to open its detail page. Use this when you have a SKU from a physical label or a supplier document and need to find the exact variant in the system.

elements.name: Receipts section
elements.description: Lists receipts whose receipt number matches the query. Click a receipt to open its full detail page, where you can reprint it or initiate a refund.

elements.name: Customers section
elements.description: Lists customers whose name matches the query. Click a customer to open their profile, which includes their credit balance, purchase history, and contact details.

elements.name: Transfers section
elements.description: Lists stock transfer records matching the query, showing the source and destination branch names. Click a transfer to view its full detail and current status.

elements.name: Barcode scanner
elements.description: Scan a barcode using your device camera to instantly search for the matching product or variant.

elements.name: Recent searches
elements.description: Shows your recent search terms stored locally. Click a recent search to re-run it. Clear all with the clear button.

elements.name: Popular items
elements.description: Shows your most frequently sold items with their sales count.

elements.name: Saved searches
elements.description: Save a search query for quick access later. Saved searches persist across sessions.

elements.name: Save this search button
elements.description: Saves the current search query with a custom name.

elements.name: Type filter tabs
elements.description: Filter results by type: All, Products, Customers, Receipts, Transfers. Each tab shows a count badge.

elements.name: Results grid layout
elements.description: Search results displayed in a two-column grid on larger screens. Products and Variants in separate sections.

elements.name: KPI cards (result counts)
elements.description: Three KPI cards showing count of matching Products, Customers, and Transfers.

elements.name: Typeahead suggestions
elements.description: As you type, matching suggestions appear in a dropdown below the search input.

before_you_start.text: No special permissions are required to use Global Search — all users can access it.
Type at least 2 characters to activate the auto-suggest. Single-character queries return no results.

common_tasks.task: Find a product by name or SKU
steps: Type the product name or SKU into the search input.
Results appear in the Products section (by name) or Variants section (by SKU).
Click the result to navigate directly to the product or variant detail page.

common_tasks.task: Find and reprint a receipt by number
steps: Type the receipt number (or part of it) into the search input.
Click the receipt in the Receipts section.
The receipt detail page opens — use the reprint or refund options there.

warnings: Results are limited to your current business only — you cannot search across multiple businesses.

If your role restricts access to a module (e.g. you cannot access Customers), results from that module may still appear in search — but clicking them will show a permission error rather than the record.

entries.title: Branch Settings
entries.overview: Create and manage the physical locations (branches) of your business. Each branch has its own name, address, phone number, and optionally a price list that overrides your default product pricing for sales made at that location. Branches are fundamental to the system — stock levels, sales, transfers, shifts, and reports are all tracked per branch.

elements.name: Total branches KPI
elements.description: Total number of branches currently loaded. Your subscription plan sets a maximum branch limit — if you are near the limit, a usage warning appears in the Subscription panel on the Business Settings page.

elements.name: With price list KPI
elements.description: How many loaded branches have a price list assigned. Branches without a price list use your default product prices at POS. If this is lower than expected, check which branches still need a list assigned.

elements.name: More pages KPI
elements.description: Indicates whether additional branch records exist beyond the current page. Click Load More at the bottom to fetch the next batch.

elements.name: Can write KPI
elements.description: Confirms whether your current role has permission to create or edit branches. If this shows No, the Create Branch button and Edit controls are visible but disabled.

elements.name: Name input (create form)
elements.description: Required. The display name for the new branch — shown in branch selectors throughout the system including POS, stock movements, transfers, and reports. Must be unique within your business.

elements.name: Address input (create form)
elements.description: Optional. The physical street address of the branch. Shown on printed receipts and in some reports.

elements.name: Phone input (create form)
elements.description: Optional. The branch's contact phone number. If entered, it prints on receipts issued at this branch and appears in the branch directory. Can be added or changed later by editing the branch record.

elements.name: Price List selector (create form)
elements.description: Optionally link an existing price list to this branch. When assigned, all sales made at this branch will use the custom prices from that list instead of default product prices. Price lists must be created first on the Price Lists page.

elements.name: Create Branch button
elements.description: Saves the new branch immediately. The branch becomes available across the system — in POS branch selectors, stock management, transfer forms, and shift tracking — as soon as it is created.

elements.name: Branch card
elements.description: Displays the branch name, address, phone, and linked price list. One card per branch. If a field is blank, it was left optional during creation.

elements.name: Edit button (per branch)
elements.description: Enters edit mode for that branch card, enabling the name, address, phone, and price list fields for editing.

elements.name: Save / Cancel (edit mode)
elements.description: Save commits your edits to the branch immediately. Cancel reverts all fields to the last saved state without making any changes.

elements.name: Load More button
elements.description: Loads the next page of branch records when you have more branches than the initial display.

elements.name: Operating Hours fields
elements.description: Opening Time and Closing Time input fields in edit mode. Set the daily operating hours for each branch. These times are displayed on branch cards and can be used for shift management reference.

elements.name: Branch performance panel
elements.description: Click 'View Performance' on a branch card to see today's sales total, sale count, and stock units held at that branch. Helps compare branch activity at a glance.

elements.name: Active user count
elements.description: Shows how many users are currently assigned and active at each branch, displayed in both card and table views.

elements.name: Table view (ViewToggle)
elements.description: Toggle between card view and table view using the Cards/Table buttons. Table view shows columns: Name, Address, Phone, Price List, Status. Added as part of the UI redesign.

elements.name: SortableTableHeader
elements.description: Clickable column headers in table view for sorting by Name, Address, Phone, and Status.

before_you_start.text: You need the business.write permission to create or edit branches.
Price lists must be created on the Price Lists page before they can be assigned to a branch here.

common_tasks.task: Create a new branch
steps: Enter the branch name in the Name input (required). Optionally add an address and phone.
Select a price list from the Price List selector if this branch uses custom pricing.
Click Create Branch. The branch is immediately available across the system.

common_tasks.task: Assign a price list to an existing branch
steps: Find the branch card and click Edit.
Select the price list from the Price List selector.
Click Save. New sales at this branch will now use the custom prices from the assigned list.

warnings: Branch names must be unique within your business. Duplicate names are rejected when you try to save.
You cannot delete a branch from this page. Branches with any transaction history cannot be deleted — contact support if a branch needs to be permanently removed or archived.
Changing a branch's price list takes effect on the next new sale, not retroactively. Past receipts at that branch are not affected.

entries.title: Business Settings
entries.overview: The central control panel for your business account, organised into seven tabs accessible from the sidebar: General, Subscription, Approvals, Stock, POS & Receipts, Notifications, and Data & Access. Configure approval workflows, stock policies, POS behaviour, locale settings, notification routing, and subscription management. Also provides tools to manage measurement units, expense categories, review support access requests, and — if needed — permanently delete the business account.
warnings: Deleting the business is irreversible — all data including sales history, stock records, and user accounts will be permanently erased. There is no undo.
Changing the stock valuation method (FIFO / LIFO / Average) affects cost-of-goods calculations on all future stock movements. Only change this in consultation with your accountant.
Enabling the BLOCK expiry policy will prevent cashiers from selling expired batches entirely. Make sure your team understands this before activating it.
Enabling FEFO dispatch requires batch tracking to be turned on. If batch tracking is off, the system falls back to FIFO regardless of this setting.
Unsaved settings changes will be lost if you navigate away. The browser will warn you, but always click Save Settings before leaving this page.
Offline limits (max hours, max sales, max value) cap what POS devices can do during a connectivity outage. Set them too low and cashiers may be unable to complete sales offline.

before_you_start.text: The page is organised into 7 tabs in the sidebar: General, Subscription, Approvals, Stock, POS & Receipts, Notifications, and Data & Access. Click a tab to navigate to that section.

Five tabs (General, Approvals, Stock, POS & Receipts, Notifications) have an edit bar at the top. Click 'Edit Settings' to unlock fields, make changes, then click 'Save Settings'. The Subscription and Data & Access tabs have their own inline actions.
You need the 'settings.write' permission (settings.write) to edit or save any settings on this page. Without it, all fields are read-only.
You need the 'subscription.request' permission (subscription.request) to submit subscription change requests.
You need the 'business.delete' permission (business.delete) to access the Delete Business panel.
Approval threshold amounts are in the business currency (TZS). Discuss threshold values with your accountant or operations manager before enabling approval workflows.
Stock valuation method changes take effect on the next stock movement. They do not retroactively recalculate past transactions.
WhatsApp notifications are only available on the Enterprise subscription tier. The toggle is visible on lower tiers but will show an 'Upgrade required' badge.
Custom units created here become available in the Base Unit and Sell Unit selectors on the Variants page. Unit codes are auto-uppercased and must be unique.
The browser warns you if you try to navigate away with unsaved settings changes. Always click Save Settings or Cancel before leaving.

common_tasks.task: Enable approval workflows for operations
steps: Click the 'Approvals' tab in the sidebar.
Click 'Edit Settings' in the edit bar at the top.
Toggle on any combination of: Stock Adjustment approval, Refund approval, Purchase approval, Transfer approval, or Expense approval.

For each toggled approval, enter a threshold amount in the labelled input to its right. Operations at or below this amount bypass approval — only operations above the threshold trigger an approval request.
Leave the threshold at 0 to require approval on every operation of that type regardless of amount.
Click 'Save Settings'. The system immediately creates or updates ApprovalPolicy records in the database for all enabled approvals.

common_tasks.task: Set discount limits for cashiers
steps: Click the 'Approvals' tab in the sidebar.
Click 'Edit Settings' in the edit bar.
Scroll down to the 'Require approval above' section.
Enter a value in 'Discount threshold %' to set the maximum discount percentage a cashier can apply without triggering an approval.
Optionally enter a value in 'Discount threshold amount' to set a maximum discount amount in TZS.
Click 'Save Settings'. Cashiers applying discounts above either limit will need an approved authorisation.

common_tasks.task: Choose FIFO or FEFO stock dispatch order
steps: Click the 'Stock' tab in the sidebar.
Click 'Edit Settings' in the edit bar.
Change 'FIFO mode' to FIFO (dispatch oldest batches first) or FEFO (dispatch soonest-to-expire batches first).
If switching to FEFO, ensure 'Batch tracking enabled' is also turned on — FEFO requires batches to have expiry dates recorded.
Click 'Save Settings'.

common_tasks.task: Change the inventory valuation method
steps: Click the 'Stock' tab in the sidebar.
Click 'Edit Settings' in the edit bar.
Change 'Valuation method' to FIFO (cost of oldest stock), LIFO (cost of most recently received stock), or Average (running weighted average cost).
Consult your accountant before changing this on a live business — it affects cost-of-goods figures in all reports going forward.
Click 'Save Settings'.

common_tasks.task: Enable batch tracking
steps: Click the 'Stock' tab in the sidebar.
Click 'Edit Settings' in the edit bar.
Toggle 'Batch tracking enabled' on.
Click 'Save Settings'.
Once enabled, receiving stock will prompt you to assign batch numbers and expiry dates. These are then used for FEFO dispatch and expiry alerts.

common_tasks.task: Configure the expiry policy and alert threshold
steps: Click the 'Stock' tab in the sidebar.
Click 'Edit Settings' in the edit bar.
Set 'Expiry policy': ALLOW (sell expired batches silently), WARN (show a warning but allow override), or BLOCK (prevent the sale entirely).
Set 'Expiry alert days' to the number of days before expiry that the system should flag batches as expiring soon (default: 30).
Click 'Save Settings'.

common_tasks.task: Set the low stock threshold
steps: Click the 'Stock' tab in the sidebar.
Click 'Edit Settings' in the edit bar.
