Enter a number in 'Low stock threshold'. Any variant whose quantity falls at or below this number will be flagged as low stock in reports and alerts.
Click 'Save Settings'.

common_tasks.task: Allow or block negative stock
steps: Click the 'Stock' tab in the sidebar.
Click 'Edit Settings' in the edit bar.
Toggle 'Allow negative stock' on to allow sales and adjustments to push stock quantities below zero, or off to block them.
Allowing negative stock is useful when stock counts lag behind actual sales. Blocking it enforces strict inventory discipline.
Click 'Save Settings'.

common_tasks.task: Configure POS receipt layout and content
steps: Click the 'POS & Receipts' tab in the sidebar.
Click 'Edit Settings' in the edit bar.
Set 'Receipt template' to Thermal or A4.
Enter text in 'Receipt header' (appears at the top of every receipt, e.g. your tagline or address).
Enter text in 'Receipt footer' (appears at the bottom, e.g. 'Thank you for your business' or refund policy).
Toggle 'Show branch contact' on to print the branch phone number and address on receipts.
Click 'Save Settings'.

common_tasks.task: Enable or disable credit sales at POS
steps: Click the 'POS & Receipts' tab in the sidebar.
Click 'Edit Settings' in the edit bar.
In the Sales Controls section, toggle 'Allow credit' on to let cashiers complete sales where the customer pays later, or off to require full payment at the time of sale.
Click 'Save Settings'.

common_tasks.task: Enable shift tracking at POS
steps: Click the 'POS & Receipts' tab in the sidebar.
Click 'Edit Settings' in the edit bar.
In the Sales Controls section, toggle 'Require shift' on. When enabled, cashiers must open a shift before making any POS sales, and close it at the end of their session.
Set 'Shift variance threshold' (in TZS) — the maximum acceptable difference between expected and actual cash in the till when closing a shift. Variances above this amount are flagged.
Click 'Save Settings'.

common_tasks.task: Configure offline session limits for POS devices
steps: Click the 'POS & Receipts' tab in the sidebar.
Click 'Edit Settings' in the edit bar.
Scroll to the 'Offline limits' section at the bottom.
Set 'Max offline duration hours' — the maximum time a POS device can operate without syncing to the server.
Set 'Max offline sales count' — the maximum number of sales a device can record offline before it must reconnect.
Set 'Max offline total value' — the maximum total sales value (TZS) allowed offline before reconnection is required.
Set 'Offline price variance %' — the maximum percentage a price can drift from server-side prices while offline.
Note: these limits are capped by your subscription tier. STARTER: offline disabled. BUSINESS: up to 72h / 200 sales. ENTERPRISE: up to 168h / 2000 sales.
Click 'Save Settings'.

common_tasks.task: Change the business timezone and date format
steps: Click the 'General' tab in the sidebar.
Click 'Edit Settings' in the edit bar.
In the Localization section, select the correct 'Timezone' from the dropdown. African timezones appear first. This affects all timestamps shown across the app.
Select the preferred 'Date format': DD/MM/YYYY (day first), MM/DD/YYYY (US format), YYYY-MM-DD (ISO standard), DD-MM-YYYY, or D MMM YYYY (e.g. 5 Mar 2026).
Click 'Save Settings'. Changes are applied immediately to your session.

common_tasks.task: Set up notification channels and recipients
steps: Click the 'Notifications' tab in the sidebar.
Click 'Edit Settings' in the edit bar.
In the Notification Channels section, toggle on 'Email' to enable email notifications. Toggle on 'WhatsApp' if available on your tier (Enterprise only).
In the Recipients section, select which roles should receive notifications by checking role checkboxes. The business owner always receives notifications automatically and is not shown in the list.

Below the roles, select any specific individual users who should also receive notifications. Both selections are combined — anyone matching a selected role OR listed as a specific user will be notified.
Optionally toggle 'Branch-only recipients' to restrict notifications to users assigned to the branch where the event occurred.

In the Event Notifications table, use the 'Enabled' checkbox to turn individual events on or off. Use the Email and WhatsApp checkboxes at the group level to set defaults for all events in that group, then override per event if needed. Each event shows a short description of what it does.
Click 'Save Settings'.

common_tasks.task: Add a custom unit of measure
steps: Click the 'Data & Access' tab in the sidebar.
In the Units section, enter a label (e.g. 'Kilogram'), a code (e.g. 'KG' — auto-uppercased), and select a unit type (e.g. 'WEIGHT').
Click 'Add Unit'. The unit is immediately available in the Base Unit and Sell Unit selectors on the Variants page.
If you see an error, check that the code is not already in use — search existing units before creating.

common_tasks.task: Edit or delete a custom unit
steps: Click the 'Data & Access' tab in the sidebar.
In the Units section, find the custom unit you want to modify (custom units show an 'Custom' badge).
Click the pencil icon to edit the unit's label or code inline. Click the check icon to save or the X icon to cancel.
Click the trash icon to delete the unit. Note: units currently in use by variants cannot be deleted.
System units (marked with 'System' badge) cannot be edited or deleted.

common_tasks.task: Add a custom expense category
steps: Click the 'Data & Access' tab in the sidebar.
In the Expense Categories section, enter a category code (e.g. 'MARKETING' — auto-uppercased, spaces become underscores) and a display name (e.g. 'Marketing & Advertising').
Click 'Add Category'. The category is immediately available in the expense creation form.

common_tasks.task: Edit or delete a custom expense category
steps: Click the 'Data & Access' tab in the sidebar.
In the Expense Categories section, find the custom category you want to modify.
Click the pencil icon to edit the category's code or label inline. Click the check icon to save or the X icon to cancel.
Click the trash icon to delete the category.
System categories cannot be edited or deleted.

common_tasks.task: Subscribe to the system or upgrade your subscription
steps: Click the 'Subscription' tab in the sidebar.
In the Subscription Actions section, you will see available actions based on your current status:
If on trial or expired: click 'Subscribe' to choose a tier and duration. Your current tier is pre-selected — you can keep it or choose a higher tier.
If active and not on Enterprise: click 'Upgrade' to move to a higher tier. Only tiers above your current one are shown.
If on Enterprise: a notice will inform you that you are already on the highest tier and no upgrade is available.
Select a duration (1, 3, 6, or 12 months), optionally enter a reason, and click the action button.
Payment is handled outside the system. After submitting, you will be contacted with payment instructions.
Check the Request History section below for status updates and any admin response notes.
Note: you can only have one pending request at a time. Wait for it to be reviewed before submitting another.

common_tasks.task: Cancel your subscription
steps: Click the 'Subscription' tab in the sidebar.
In the Subscription Actions section, click 'Cancel'.
Enter a reason for the cancellation.
Click the 'Cancel' action button. A warning explains that your data will be retained for 30 days after cancellation.
The request will be reviewed by the platform team. Check the Request History section for status updates.

common_tasks.task: Approve or reject a platform support access request
steps: Click the 'Data & Access' tab in the sidebar.
In the Support Access Requests section, review the request details: the reason the support team provided, the scope of access they are requesting, and the duration.
Click 'Approve' to grant access for the specified duration, or 'Reject' to deny it.
Resolved requests remain visible in the panel with their outcome recorded.

common_tasks.task: Delete the business account permanently
steps: Click the 'Data & Access' tab in the sidebar.
Scroll to the Danger Zone section at the bottom (red-bordered area).
Read the warning carefully — this action is irreversible and deletes all data including sales history, stock records, products, users, and branches.
Enter your business ID in the first confirmation field (visible in the General tab under Business Profile).
Enter your account password in the second confirmation field.
Type the word DELETE (in capitals) in the third confirmation field.
Click 'Delete Business'. Your session will be cleared and you will be redirected to the login page.

elements.name: Sidebar tabs
elements.description: Seven tabs in a vertical sidebar: General, Subscription, Approvals, Stock, POS & Receipts, Notifications, and Data & Access. On mobile, these appear as a horizontal scrollable tab bar. Click a tab to navigate to that section.
elements.notes: On desktop, the sidebar is fixed at 200px width on the left. On mobile, tabs scroll horizontally above the content.

elements.name: Edit bar
elements.description: A sticky bar at the top of the content area for editable tabs (General, Approvals, Stock, POS & Receipts, Notifications). Shows an 'Edit Settings' button in view mode, or 'Cancel' and 'Save Settings' buttons in edit mode. Displays an 'Editing' badge and 'Unsaved changes' indicator when changes have been made.
elements.notes: Only visible on editable tabs. The Subscription and Data & Access tabs have their own inline action buttons instead.

elements.name: KPI — Business Name
elements.description: Shows your registered business name as stored in the system.
elements.notes: Read-only display.

elements.name: KPI — Subscription
elements.description: Shows your current subscription tier and status (e.g. 'ENTERPRISE / ACTIVE'). Colour-coded: Starter (amber), Business (blue), Enterprise (yellow).
elements.notes: Status updates automatically when your subscription changes.

elements.name: KPI — Roles
elements.description: Shows the total number of roles defined in this business.
elements.notes: Read-only count.

elements.name: KPI — Users
elements.description: Shows the total number of user accounts in this business.
elements.notes: Read-only count.

elements.name: General tab — Business Info
elements.description: Shows the business name and unique ID. The ID is useful when contacting support.
elements.notes: Read-only. Located in the General tab.

elements.name: General tab — Localization
elements.description: Configure timezone and date format. Currency is shown but read-only (multi-currency support coming soon). Timezone dropdown prioritises African zones. Date format options: DD/MM/YYYY, MM/DD/YYYY, YYYY-MM-DD, DD-MM-YYYY, D MMM YYYY.
elements.notes: Editable when in edit mode. Changes apply to all timestamps across the app.

elements.name: General tab — Changelog
elements.description: History of all settings changes, showing what action was taken and when.
elements.notes: Read-only. Sorted by most recent first.

elements.name: Subscription tab — Overview
elements.description: Shows subscription expiry date, days remaining, and usage progress bars for Users, Branches, Products, and Devices. Bars turn amber above 70% usage and red above 90%. Unlimited limits show as 'X (Unlimited)'.
elements.notes: Includes trial expiry notice with 30-day data retention warning when on trial.

elements.name: Subscription tab — Actions
elements.description: Three action buttons appear based on your subscription status. Subscribe (green): available during trial or when expired — choose a tier and duration. Upgrade (blue): available when active and below Enterprise — choose a higher tier. Cancel (red): available when active — request cancellation. Each action expands a dedicated form with relevant fields. If you are on Enterprise, a notice explains no upgrade is available. If you have a pending request, all actions are disabled with a notice.
elements.notes: Only one pending request allowed at a time. Payment is handled outside the system.

elements.name: Subscription tab — Request History
elements.description: List of all past subscription requests showing type (Subscribe/Upgrade/Cancel), requested tier, status (Pending/Approved/Rejected), reason, admin response notes, and timestamp.
elements.notes: Colour-coded badges: Pending (amber), Approved (green), Rejected (red).

elements.name: Approvals tab — Toggle rows
elements.description: Five toggle rows for Stock Adjustment, Refund, Purchase, Transfer, and Expense approvals. Each row has a toggle to enable/disable and a labelled threshold amount input. When the toggle is on and a threshold is set, only operations exceeding the threshold require approval.
elements.notes: Threshold inputs are disabled when the toggle is off. Reset button reverts to defaults.

elements.name: Approvals tab — Discount thresholds
elements.description: Separate section for discount approval thresholds: a percentage input and an amount input. Discounts exceeding either limit trigger an approval request.
elements.notes: Located below the main approval toggles, separated by a divider.

elements.name: Stock tab — Policies
elements.description: Eight stock policy controls: Allow negative stock (toggle), Batch tracking enabled (toggle), FIFO/FEFO mode (select), Valuation method (select: FIFO/LIFO/Average), Expiry policy (select: Allow/Warn/Block), Transfer batch policy (select: Preserve/Recreate), Expiry alert days (number), Low stock threshold (number).
elements.notes: Reset button available. FEFO requires batch tracking enabled.

elements.name: POS tab — Receipt settings
elements.description: Configure receipt template (Thermal/A4), header text, footer text, and branch contact toggle.
elements.notes: Reset button available.

elements.name: POS tab — Sales controls
elements.description: Four toggles: Allow credit sales, Allow price edit, Require shift tracking, Refund return to stock default. Plus a shift variance threshold number input.
elements.notes: These control cashier behaviour at the point of sale.

elements.name: POS tab — Maximum discount
elements.description: POS-specific discount limits: maximum discount percentage and maximum discount amount that cashiers can apply without triggering approval.
elements.notes: Separate from the Approvals tab discount thresholds — this is the POS enforcement limit.

elements.name: POS tab — Offline limits
elements.description: Four number inputs: Max offline duration (hours), Max offline sales count, Max offline total value, and Offline price variance percentage. Controls are disabled if your subscription tier does not support offline mode. A hint shows your tier's maximum caps.
elements.notes: Limits are enforced as maximums — you can set lower values but not higher than your tier allows.

elements.name: Notifications tab — Channels
elements.description: Three channel cards: In-App (always active, no toggle), Email (toggle on/off), WhatsApp (toggle on/off, Enterprise only — shows 'Upgrade required' on lower tiers).
elements.notes: Disabling a channel here disables it for all notification events.

elements.name: Notifications tab — Recipients
elements.description: Configure who receives notifications. Roles and specific users are shown together in a single unified view — both selections are combined. Anyone matching a selected role OR listed as a specific user will be notified. The business owner always receives notifications automatically and is not shown in the role list. A 'Branch-only recipients' toggle restricts notifications to users assigned to the branch where the event occurred.
elements.notes: Roles like 'System Owner' are excluded from the selectable list because the owner is auto-included.

elements.name: Notifications tab — Event matrix
elements.description: A table of all notification events grouped by category (Security & Access, Approvals, Inventory, Sales, Purchases, Transfers, System). Columns: Event name and description, Enabled checkbox, Email checkbox, WhatsApp checkbox. Group header rows set default channel selections for all events in the group. Individual event rows can override the group default. Each event shows a short hint explaining what the notification does.
elements.notes: Event channel checkboxes are disabled if: the global channel is off, the event itself is disabled, or WhatsApp is unavailable on your tier. 29 events across 7 groups.

elements.name: Data tab — Units
elements.description: Create, edit, and delete custom units of measure. The create form has label, code (auto-uppercased), and unit type fields. The unit list shows all units with System or Custom badges. Custom units have pencil (edit) and trash (delete) icons for inline editing and deletion.
elements.notes: System units cannot be edited or deleted. Units in use by variants cannot be deleted. No need to enter edit mode — the form is always active.

elements.name: Data tab — Expense Categories
elements.description: Create, edit, and delete custom expense categories. The category list shows code and label with System badge where applicable. Custom categories have pencil (edit) and trash (delete) icons for inline editing and deletion. The create form has code (auto-uppercased, spaces become underscores) and label fields.
elements.notes: System categories cannot be edited or deleted. No need to enter edit mode — the form is always active.

elements.name: Data tab — Support Access Requests
elements.description: View and respond to platform support access requests. Each request shows the reason, status, scope of access, and duration. Pending requests have Approve and Reject buttons.
elements.notes: Only visible when there are requests. Requires settings.write permission to approve/reject.

elements.name: Data tab — Danger Zone
elements.description: Permanently delete the business account. Requires three confirmations: entering the business ID, entering your account password, and typing 'DELETE' in capitals. This is irreversible — all data is permanently erased.
elements.notes: Requires business.delete permission. Red-bordered section at the bottom of the Data & Access tab.

entries.title: My Profile
entries.overview: View your personal account details, the roles assigned to you, and every permission you currently hold within the business. If a feature is blocked for you, check the Permissions panel here to see if the required permission is missing — then use the Request Access form to ask an administrator for it.

elements.name: Roles KPI
elements.description: The number of roles currently assigned to you. Each role grants a bundle of permissions. If this shows 0, you likely have very limited access to the system — contact your administrator.

elements.name: Permissions KPI
elements.description: Total number of individual permissions you hold across all your roles. Use this as a quick reference — if a feature is blocked and this count seems low, you may be missing a required permission.

elements.name: Status KPI
elements.description: Your account status: ACTIVE means you have normal access. SUSPENDED means your account has been temporarily disabled by an administrator — contact them to resolve it.

elements.name: Membership KPI
elements.description: Your membership status within this business. ACTIVE means you are a current team member. PENDING means your invitation has not yet been fully accepted or confirmed.

elements.name: Profile card
elements.description: Read-only display of your full name, email address, phone number, account status, last login timestamp, and account creation date. To change any of these details, contact your administrator — they cannot be edited from this page.

elements.name: Roles panel
elements.description: Lists each role assigned to you with its name and branch scope (if applicable). A branch-scoped role means your access is restricted to data from that specific branch only — you cannot see other branches' sales, stock, or reports.

elements.name: Permissions panel
elements.description: All permissions you hold displayed as labelled chips, grouped by module. If a button or page is blocked for you, check here first — the permission you need should be visible if you have it, or absent if you do not.

elements.name: Permission selector (Request Access form)
elements.description: Lists only the permissions you do not currently hold. Select the one you need. If the permission you are looking for is not in the list, you may already have it — check the Permissions panel above.

elements.name: Reason input (Request Access form)
elements.description: Explain why you need this permission. A specific, clear reason helps the administrator approve your request faster. Example: 'I need to process refunds for customers who contact our support line.'

elements.name: Submit button (Request Access form)
elements.description: Sends your access request to an administrator with the approvals.write permission. You will receive a notification when the request is approved or rejected.

elements.name: Login history section
elements.description: Shows the last 5 login timestamps for your account. Useful for verifying that no unauthorized access has occurred.

elements.name: Change Password link
elements.description: Navigates to the Change Password page where you can update your account password.

elements.name: Notification Preferences link
elements.description: Navigates to Business Settings where you can configure your notification preferences.

elements.name: Edit Profile mode
elements.description: Click Edit to switch to edit mode where you can update your name and phone number. Click Save to persist changes or Cancel to discard.

elements.name: Permission risk badges
elements.description: Each permission in the permissions grid shows a coloured risk badge: red for HIGH risk, amber for MEDIUM, green for LOW. Helps understand the sensitivity of your granted permissions.

elements.name: Branch scope indicator
elements.description: Next to each role assignment, shows which branches the role applies to. If the role is not branch-scoped, shows 'All Branches'.

elements.name: All Permissions Granted message
elements.description: If you already have all available permissions, the access request form is replaced with a message confirming full access.

before_you_start.text: All users can view their own profile — no special permission is required.
Access requests require approval from a user with the approvals.write permission. If no one in your business has this permission, requests cannot be processed — contact support.

common_tasks.task: Check which permissions you have
steps: Open the Permissions panel on this page.
Browse the chips grouped by module. If a specific permission is missing, it will not appear here — use the Request Access form below.

common_tasks.task: Request a new permission
steps: Select the permission you need from the Permission selector in the Request Access form.
Enter a clear, specific reason in the Reason input explaining your use case.
Click Submit. An administrator will be notified and will approve or reject your request.

warnings: You cannot edit your own profile details (name, email, phone) from this page. Contact your administrator to update your account information.
Access requests go to administrators with the approvals.write permission. If your request sits unanswered, check with your administrator or contact support if no one has that permission.

entries.title: Roles & Permissions
entries.overview: Create and manage roles that control what actions users can perform across the system. Each role holds a set of individual permissions drawn from nine functional modules. Roles also carry an approval tier that determines which pending approvals a role-holder can process. System roles are pre-defined and locked; only custom roles can be edited.
before_you_start: You must have the Manage Roles permission to create or edit roles.
System roles (including System Owner) are read-only — their permissions and approval tier cannot be changed.
Saving permissions replaces the entire permission set for the role in one atomic operation; partial updates are not possible.
Permission changes take effect immediately for every user currently assigned to the role.
The Approval Tier controls which approval requests a role can process: None means the role cannot process any approvals, Staff handles lower-tier requests, and Management handles all tiers.

elements.label: Visible Roles
elements.description: Total number of roles currently loaded in the list based on active filters. Updates as you apply or clear search and scope filters.

elements.label: Permissions Available
elements.description: Total number of individual permissions defined in the system across all nine modules. This count is static and does not change with filters.

elements.label: Selected Role
elements.description: Displays the name of the role whose permissions are currently open in the editor panel. Shows 'None' when no role is selected.

elements.label: Assigned Permissions
elements.description: Number of permissions currently assigned to the selected role. Updates live as you check or uncheck permissions in the editor before saving.

elements.label: Search Roles
elements.description: Debounced text search that filters the role list by name. Clears automatically when the Reset Filters button is used.

elements.label: Scope Filter
elements.description: Filters roles by origin. 'All' shows every role; 'System' shows only pre-defined locked roles; 'Custom' shows only roles you have created.

elements.label: Permission Count Filter
elements.description: Filters roles by whether they have any permissions assigned. 'Has Permissions' shows roles with at least one permission; 'No Permissions' shows empty roles.

elements.label: Advanced Filters
elements.description: Expands or collapses the advanced filter row containing the Scope and Permission Count filters.

elements.label: Reset Filters
elements.description: Clears all active filters — search text, scope, and permission count — and reloads the full role list from the first page.

elements.label: Role Name
elements.description: Text input for the name of the new role. Must be unique within the business. Required before the Create Role button is enabled.

elements.label: Approval Tier (Create)
elements.description: Sets the approval authority for the new role. None: cannot process any approvals. Staff: can process tier-1 approval requests. Management: can process all approval tiers.

elements.label: Create Role
elements.description: Submits the new role form (POST /roles). The role is created with the given name and approval tier and immediately appears in the list. The form clears on success.

elements.label: Role List
elements.description: Displays all roles matching current filters. Each row shows the role name, a 'System' badge for pre-defined roles, and an Edit Permissions button. The System Owner row shows a locked indicator instead of an Edit button.

elements.label: Edit Permissions
elements.description: Loads the selected role's current permissions via GET /roles/:id/permissions and opens the permission editor panel. Only visible for roles that can be edited (not System Owner).

elements.label: Load More
elements.description: Fetches the next page of roles using cursor-based pagination and appends them to the current list. Hidden when all roles matching the current filters are loaded.

elements.label: Permission Panel Header
elements.description: Read-only header at the top of the permission editor showing 'Permissions for [role name]'. Confirms which role's permissions you are currently editing.

elements.label: Close Panel
elements.description: Closes the permission editor panel without saving. Unsaved permission changes are discarded. The Selected Role KPI resets to 'None'.

elements.label: Approval Tier (Edit)
elements.description: Changes the approval authority of the currently open role. Visible only for non-system roles. Options: None, Staff, Management. Saved independently with the Save Approval Tier button.

elements.label: Save Approval Tier
elements.description: Saves the approval tier change for the current role (PUT /roles/:id with approvalTier). Takes effect immediately for all users assigned to this role.

elements.label: Permission Search
elements.description: Filters the visible permissions by code, label, or description. Useful when you know the name of a specific permission and want to locate it quickly without scrolling through all modules.

elements.label: Module Filter
elements.description: Narrows the permission list to a single functional module. Available modules: Business, Users, Catalog, Stock, Sales, Customers, Purchases, Reports, System. Select 'All' to show every module.

elements.label: Read Preset
elements.description: Applies the Read preset to the currently filtered module: checks all permissions at the 'read' level for that module and unchecks any permissions above that level. Does not affect other modules.

elements.label: Standard Preset
elements.description: Applies the Standard preset to the current module: checks all permissions at 'read' and 'standard' levels and unchecks any 'full' level permissions for that module. Does not affect other modules.

elements.label: Full Preset
elements.description: Applies the Full preset to the current module: checks all permissions in the module regardless of level. Use with caution — this includes high-risk permissions. Does not affect other modules.

elements.label: Permission Checkboxes
elements.description: Grid of all permissions for the current module view. Each permission shows its title, a color-coded risk badge (low / medium / high), a short description, and its internal code. Check or uncheck each box to include or exclude it from the role.

elements.label: Permission Guide
elements.description: Read-only sidebar panel explaining permission risk levels and module groupings. Low risk (green): view-only actions. Medium risk (yellow): create and edit actions. High risk (red): delete, billing, or sensitive system actions.

elements.label: Save Permissions
elements.description: Saves the full permission selection for the current role (PUT /roles/:id/permissions with permissionIds array). Replaces the entire existing permission set atomically. Changes take effect immediately for all users assigned to this role.

elements.name: Clone Role button
elements.description: Creates a duplicate of the selected role with all its permissions copied. The new role is named '[Role Name] (Copy)'. Useful for creating variations of existing roles quickly.

elements.name: Risk summary display
elements.description: Shows a count of high, medium, and low risk permissions assigned to the selected role. Displayed in the permission editor panel. Helps assess the risk level of a role at a glance.

elements.name: Permission progress bar
elements.description: A visual bar showing how many permissions are assigned out of the total available. Updates live as you check or uncheck permissions.

elements.name: User count badge
elements.description: Shows how many users are assigned to each role, displayed on the role card or list item.

elements.name: Tier badge styling
elements.description: Approval tier shown as a coloured badge: grey for None, amber for Staff, green for Management, blue for System Owner.

elements.name: System Owner locked indicator
elements.description: The System Owner role displays a locked icon and cannot be edited or cloned. Its permissions are fixed by the system.

elements.name: CollapsibleSection (New Role form)
elements.description: The create form is hidden behind a 'New Role' button. Enter a role name and select an approval tier. Closes after creation.

common_tasks.title: Create a new custom role
steps: Enter a unique name in the Role Name input.
Select the appropriate Approval Tier from the dropdown (None, Staff, or Management).
Click Create Role — the role appears in the list immediately.
Click Edit Permissions on the new role to open the permission editor and assign permissions.

common_tasks.title: Assign permissions to a role using module presets
steps: Click Edit Permissions on the role you want to configure.
Use the Module Filter to select the module you want to configure (e.g., Sales).
Click Read, Standard, or Full preset button to apply that level for the module.
Repeat for each module you want to configure.
Click Save Permissions when done — changes take effect immediately.

common_tasks.title: Manually select individual permissions
steps: Click Edit Permissions on the target role.
Use Module Filter and Permission Search to locate the specific permission.
Check or uncheck the permission checkbox.
Click Save Permissions to apply the change.

common_tasks.title: Change a role's approval tier
steps: Click Edit Permissions on the target role to open the editor.
In the Approval Authority section, select the new tier from the Approval Tier (Edit) dropdown.
Click Save Approval Tier — the change takes effect immediately.

common_tasks.title: Search for a specific permission
steps: Click Edit Permissions on any role.
Type the permission name or code into the Permission Search input.
The permission checkboxes grid filters in real time — locate and check the permission.
Click Save Permissions to apply.

common_tasks.title: Filter the role list by scope or permission status
steps: Toggle Advanced Filters on to reveal the Scope and Permission Count filter dropdowns.
Set Scope to 'System' or 'Custom' to narrow by origin.
Set Permission Count to 'Has Permissions' or 'No Permissions' to find roles by assignment status.
Click Reset Filters to restore the full list.

common_tasks.title: View which permissions a system role has
steps: Locate the system role in the Role List.
Click Edit Permissions — the permission editor opens in read mode showing the role's current permissions.
Use the Module Filter to browse permissions by module.
Note: you cannot save changes to a system role.

common_tasks.title: Remove all permissions from a role
steps: Click Edit Permissions on the target role.
Uncheck all checkboxes across all modules (or use the search and module filter to work through them systematically).
Click Save Permissions — the role will have zero permissions.

common_tasks.title: Close the permission editor without saving
steps: Click the Close Panel button in the permission editor header.
Unsaved permission changes are discarded — the role's permissions remain unchanged.
The Selected Role KPI resets to 'None'.

common_tasks.title: Load additional roles beyond the first page
steps: Scroll to the bottom of the Role List.
Click Load More to fetch and append the next page of roles.
Continue clicking Load More until all roles matching the current filters are visible.

warnings: Saving a permission set replaces all existing permissions for the role atomically — there is no merge. Always review the full selection before clicking Save Permissions.
The System Owner role cannot be edited or deleted. Its row shows a locked indicator instead of an Edit Permissions button.
Removing a permission from a role revokes it immediately for all users assigned to that role — there is no undo from the UI. Check Audit Logs to review what changed.
High-risk permissions (red badge) grant destructive or sensitive operations such as deleting records, managing billing, or accessing all tenant data. Assign them only to roles with a clear need.
A role with no permissions assigned still appears in the list and can still be assigned to users, but those users will have minimal or no access to protected features.

entries.title: Users
entries.overview: Invite new staff members and manage all user accounts for your business. Each user is given one or more role assignments that define what they can see and do. Role assignments can be global (apply to all branches) or scoped to a specific branch. Notification preferences are also managed per user from this page.

before_you_start.text: Roles must exist before you can invite users or assign roles — create them first at /settings/roles.
Branches must exist if you want to scope a role assignment to a specific branch — set them up at /settings/branches.
users.create is required to send invitations.
users.update is required to edit user profiles and manage role assignments.
users.deactivate is required to deactivate a user account.

elements.name: KPI – Visible Users
elements.description: Count of users currently shown in the list based on active filters. If filters are applied, this number may be less than the total user count in the system. Clear all filters to see the full headcount.

elements.name: KPI – Active Users
elements.description: Count of users in the current filtered list whose status is ACTIVE. Only ACTIVE users can log in and perform actions in the system. If this number is lower than expected, check the Status filter or look for users in SUSPENDED or DEACTIVATED state.

elements.name: KPI – Roles
elements.description: Total number of roles loaded from the system. This is the pool of roles available for assignment on this page. If a role is missing from the assignment dropdown, go to /settings/roles to create it.

elements.name: KPI – Branches
elements.description: Total number of branches loaded. Branches are used when scoping a role assignment to a specific location. If a branch is missing from the assignment dropdown, go to /settings/branches to add it.

elements.name: Search Input
elements.description: Filter the user list by name or email address. Results update automatically after a short pause as you type. Use this to quickly find a specific user before editing their profile or role assignments. Clear the field to return to the full list.

elements.name: Status Filter
elements.description: Filter users by account status. Options: All (default), ACTIVE (can log in), SUSPENDED (temporarily blocked), DEACTIVATED (permanently removed from access), PENDING (invited but has not yet accepted). Use PENDING to see which invitations have not been accepted yet.

elements.name: Role Filter
elements.description: Filter the user list to show only users who have a specific role assigned. Useful when auditing who holds a particular role, such as checking which users are Managers or Cashiers. Selecting 'All roles' removes the filter.

elements.name: Advanced Filters Toggle
elements.description: Shows or hides the Status Filter and Role Filter controls. Off by default — enable when you need to narrow the list by status or role. Closing the panel does not clear the filter values that are already applied.

elements.name: Reset Filters Button
elements.description: Clears all active filters (search text, status, role) and returns the list to the default view showing all users. Use this when multiple filters are active and you want to start fresh.

elements.name: Invite Email Input
elements.description: Email address of the person to invite. The system sends an invitation email to this address with a link the recipient uses to set up their account. The email must not already belong to a user in this business. Required — the Send Invite button is disabled until a valid email is entered.

elements.name: Invite Role Selector
elements.description: The initial role to assign to the invited user. The System Owner role is excluded from this list — that role can only be held by the business owner. Additional roles and branch-scoped assignments can be added after the user accepts the invitation.

elements.name: Send Invite Button
elements.description: Sends the invitation email and creates a PENDING user account. The button is disabled if you lack users.create permission. After sending, the invited user appears in the list with PENDING status until they accept. If the invite email is not received, check the email address is correct and ask the user to check spam.

elements.name: User Info Display
elements.description: Read-only row showing the user's name, email address, phone number (if set), and current status. This is the collapsed view for each user. Click Edit to expand the inline form for that user. The status shown here reflects the live account state — refresh the page if you expect a recent change.

elements.name: Edit Button
elements.description: Expands the inline edit form for this user. Also loads the user's current role assignments from the API. Only one user can be in edit mode at a time — opening a second user's form automatically discards any unsaved changes to the first. Requires users.update permission.

elements.name: Deactivate Button
elements.description: Triggers a confirmation dialog asking you to confirm deactivation. On confirm, calls the deactivate endpoint which revokes the user's access to this business. The user's account is not deleted — it moves to DEACTIVATED status and can be reviewed in the list. Requires users.deactivate permission.

elements.name: Name Input (edit form)
elements.description: The user's display name as it appears in the system — on audit logs, assignment records, and in the user list. Update this if a staff member's name has changed. Changes are saved when you click Save.

elements.name: Email Input (edit form)
elements.description: The user's login email address. Changing this updates the address the user logs in with and receives system notifications at. Ensure the new address is correct and belongs to the user — an incorrect email will prevent the user from logging in.

elements.name: Phone Input (edit form)
elements.description: Optional mobile phone number for the user. Used as a secondary contact reference. Not used for SMS notifications on the current plan — enter it for record-keeping purposes or future notification channel support.

elements.name: Status Selector (edit form)
elements.description: Changes the user's account status inline. Options available here are ACTIVE and DEACTIVATED. Use ACTIVE to re-enable a user whose status was changed in error; use DEACTIVATED to remove access without going through the Deactivate button flow. The change takes effect after clicking Save.

elements.name: WhatsApp Language Selector
elements.description: Sets the language in which WhatsApp notification messages are sent to this user. Options are English and Swahili. The default is English. Change this to Swahili for users who prefer to receive notifications in Swahili. Takes effect after clicking Save.

elements.name: Notification Event Checkboxes
elements.description: A grid of 32 event toggles controlling which business events this user is notified about. Events span six categories: inventory (low stock, adjustments, counts), sales (drafted, completed, voided, refunded), purchases (created, approved, received, returns, expenses), transfers (created, in-transit, received, cancelled), approvals (approved, rejected, access requests, subscription updates), and security (refresh token reuse, unusual login). All events are enabled by default for new users. Uncheck events the user does not need to reduce notification noise. Changes take effect after clicking Save.

elements.name: Assign Role Selector
elements.description: Chooses the role to grant this user in the Assign Role action. The System Owner role is excluded. Roles define what permissions the user has — select the role that matches the user's responsibilities. After choosing a role, optionally select a branch before clicking Assign Role.

elements.name: Assign Branch Selector
elements.description: Optional. Scopes the role assignment to a specific branch. If left blank (All branches), the role applies across all branches. If set, the user has the selected role's permissions only when operating in that branch. Use branch-scoped assignments to give a user manager access at one location without granting it everywhere.

elements.name: Assign Role Button
elements.description: Creates the role assignment combining the selected role and branch. The new assignment appears immediately in the Current Role Assignments list below. Duplicate assignments (same role and branch) are handled gracefully — the system will not create a second identical record. Requires users.update permission.

elements.name: Current Role Assignments List
elements.description: Read-only list of all roles currently assigned to this user. Each row shows the role name and the branch scope ('All branches' if not branch-scoped). If the list is empty, the user has no roles and will have no permissions after logging in. Add at least one role before saving.

elements.name: Remove Role Button
elements.description: Removes the specific role-branch combination from this user immediately — no Save required. The assignment is deleted and the list updates in place. If you remove all roles from a user, they will be able to log in but will have no permissions. Requires users.update permission.

elements.name: Save Button
elements.description: Saves all changes made in the edit form: name, email, phone, status, WhatsApp language, and notification event preferences. Does not save role assignments — those are applied immediately when you click Assign Role or Remove Role. After saving, the form collapses back to view mode.

elements.name: Cancel Button
elements.description: Closes the edit form and discards any unsaved changes to name, email, phone, status, and notification preferences. Role assignment changes (assign and remove actions) made while the form was open are already saved — Cancel does not undo them.

elements.name: Load More Button
elements.description: Appends the next page of 25 users to the list using cursor-based pagination. Appears only when more users exist beyond the currently loaded set. If the button does not appear, all users matching the current filters are already shown.

elements.name: Online status indicator
elements.description: A green dot next to the user's name in both card and table views, indicating the user is currently online with an active session.

elements.name: Tier badge
elements.description: A coloured badge next to each role assignment showing the approval tier: grey for None, amber for Staff, green for Management, blue for System Owner. Helps quickly identify permission levels.

elements.name: Activity panel
elements.description: Click 'View Activity' on a user card to see their sales count, last action performed, and when it was performed. Useful for monitoring staff activity.

elements.name: Resend Invitation button
elements.description: Appears on users with a pending password reset flag (mustResetPassword). Click to resend the invitation email so they can set their password.

elements.name: WhatsApp notification locale selector
elements.description: When editing a user's notification preferences, choose whether WhatsApp notifications are sent in English or Swahili.

elements.name: CollapsibleSection (Invite User form)
elements.description: The invite form is hidden behind an 'Invite User' button. Enter the user's email and assign a role. The form closes after a successful invitation.

elements.name: StatusBadge
elements.description: Coloured status pills on each user. Green for ACTIVE, amber for SUSPENDED, grey for DEACTIVATED, blue for PENDING.

elements.name: SortableTableHeader
elements.description: Clickable column headers in table view for sorting by Name, Email, Role, Status.

common_tasks.task: Invite a new user
steps: In the Invite user panel, enter the new user's email address in the Invite Email Input.
Select their initial role from the Invite Role Selector.
Click Send Invite.
The user receives an email with a link to set up their account.
The user appears in the list with PENDING status until the invitation is accepted.
After they accept, assign additional roles or branch-scoped access from their edit form if needed.

common_tasks.task: Edit a user's profile (name, email, or phone)
steps: Find the user in the list using Search or filters.
Click Edit on their row.
Update the Name, Email, or Phone fields in the edit form.
Click Save. The form collapses and the list refreshes with the updated details.

common_tasks.task: Change a user's account status
steps: Click Edit on the user's row.
In the Status Selector, choose ACTIVE to restore access or DEACTIVATED to remove it.
Click Save.
The updated status appears in the user's row in view mode.

common_tasks.task: Deactivate a user (quick path)
steps: Find the user in the list.
Click the Deactivate button on their row.
Read the confirmation dialog and click Deactivate to confirm.
The user's status changes to DEACTIVATED and they immediately lose access to this business.

common_tasks.task: Assign a role to a user
steps: Click Edit on the user's row.
In the Role Assignments section, select the role from the Assign Role Selector.
To scope it to a specific branch, select that branch from the Assign Branch Selector. Leave blank for all branches.
Click Assign Role.
The assignment appears in the Current Role Assignments list immediately.
Click Save to save any profile changes, or Cancel if you only needed to change roles.

common_tasks.task: Remove a role from a user
steps: Click Edit on the user's row.
In the Current Role Assignments list, find the role-branch row to remove.
Click the Remove button on that row.
The assignment is deleted immediately — no Save is required.
If all roles are removed, add at least one new role before the user next logs in.

common_tasks.task: Configure a user's notification preferences
steps: Click Edit on the user's row.
Scroll to the Notification Preferences section.
Check or uncheck the Notification Event Checkboxes for the events this user should or should not receive.
Click Save. The updated preferences take effect for the next notification the system sends to this user.

common_tasks.task: Change a user's WhatsApp notification language
steps: Click Edit on the user's row.
In the Notification Preferences section, open the WhatsApp Language Selector.
Choose English or Swahili.
Click Save. Future WhatsApp notifications for this user will be sent in the selected language.

common_tasks.task: Filter users by status
steps: Enable the Advanced Filters Toggle if the Status Filter is not visible.
Select the desired status from the Status Filter (e.g. PENDING to see outstanding invitations).
The list updates immediately.
Click Reset Filters to return to the full list.

common_tasks.task: Filter users by role
steps: Enable the Advanced Filters Toggle if the Role Filter is not visible.
Select a role from the Role Filter dropdown.
The list narrows to users who hold that role.
Click Reset Filters to return to the full list.

warnings: Removing all roles from a user leaves them able to log in but with no permissions — always ensure at least one role is assigned.
The Cancel button does not undo role assignment or removal actions — those take effect immediately when you click Assign Role or Remove.
Deactivating a user is immediate and revokes access at once — they will be logged out on their next request. Use this only when access should be removed right away.
Changing a user's email address updates their login credential — confirm the new address is correct before saving or the user will not be able to log in.
The System Owner role cannot be assigned from this page — it is held by the business account owner and managed separately.

related_pages.reason: Create and configure roles before assigning them to users here.
Branches must exist to scope role assignments to specific locations.
All user management actions (invites, edits, deactivations, role changes) are recorded in the audit log.
View the notifications a user has received after configuring their event preferences here.
Users manage their own profile details and password on the Profile page — admins manage other users' profiles here.

permissions_required: users.update (edit profiles, manage role assignments, update notification preferences)

entries.title: Shifts
entries.overview: Open and close POS cash shifts for each branch. When shift tracking is enabled in Business Settings, a shift must be open at a branch before staff can process sales there. At shift close, the system records the expected cash based on opening cash plus all cash sales, then compares it to the physical count — any variance is logged and large variances can trigger an approval request for a manager to review.

elements.name: Shift records KPI
elements.description: Total shift records loaded across all branches you have access to, including both open and closed shifts.

elements.name: Open KPI
elements.description: Count of shifts currently open. If this is higher than the number of active branches, an old shift was likely never closed — locate and close it to avoid reconciliation issues.

elements.name: Closed KPI
elements.description: Count of closed shifts in the loaded record set. Use this with a date filter to review daily reconciliation across branches.

elements.name: Branches KPI
elements.description: Number of distinct branches represented in the loaded shift records. If you are reviewing activity across all branches, this confirms how many locations have shift data in the current date range.

elements.name: Branch selector (Open Shift form)
elements.description: Choose which branch you are opening the shift for. Only branches your role has shift access to appear here.

elements.name: Opening cash input
elements.description: Enter the total cash in the till drawer at the start of the shift. This figure is the baseline for calculating the expected closing cash — sales cash is added to it automatically.

elements.name: Notes input (Open Shift)
elements.description: Optional free-text notes about the shift opening. Use this to record any float discrepancies, handover notes, or special circumstances at shift start.

elements.name: Open Shift button
elements.description: Creates and opens the shift for the selected branch. POS sales can now be completed at that branch. If a shift is already open at the same branch, this button is blocked — you must close the existing shift first.

elements.name: Shift selector (Close Shift form)
elements.description: Lists only shifts that are currently open. Select the shift you want to close — typically the open shift at your branch.

elements.name: Closing cash input
elements.description: Enter the physical cash you counted in the till at shift end. The system automatically calculates the expected closing cash (opening cash + all cash sales) and computes the variance when you submit.

elements.name: Variance reason input
elements.description: If closing cash differs from the expected amount, explain why — for example 'Counted short due to change given from manager's float'. A reason is required when variance exceeds the configured threshold in Business Settings.

elements.name: Close Shift button
elements.description: Closes the selected shift and records the variance. If the variance exceeds the approval threshold, an approval request is generated automatically for a manager to review — the POS is not blocked while the request is pending.

elements.name: Shift history list
elements.description: Shows past shifts with branch name, opened and closed timestamps, opening cash, closing cash, and variance amount. Use this for daily reconciliation review or audit purposes.

elements.name: Load More button
elements.description: Loads additional shift records beyond the initial page.

elements.name: CollapsibleSection (Open/Close forms)
elements.description: Two collapsible sections: 'Open Shift' to start a new shift with opening cash amount, and 'Close Shift' to end a shift with closing cash, variance reason, and handover notes.

elements.name: FlipCounter / FlipClock
elements.description: KPI cards use animated FlipCounter numbers. Open shifts display a live FlipClock showing the elapsed duration in real-time.

elements.name: ProgressBar for shift duration
elements.description: A coloured progress bar showing how long the shift has been open. Green for under 8 hours, amber for 8-10 hours, red for over 10 hours.

elements.name: Shift performance panel
elements.description: Click 'View Performance' on a shift card to see metrics: number of sales, total sales amount, and average transaction value during that shift.

elements.name: Handover notes field
elements.description: An optional text field when closing a shift to leave notes for the next shift (e.g. issues encountered, cash discrepancies, things to follow up).

elements.name: Variance colour coding
elements.description: Cash variance (closing cash minus expected cash) is colour-coded: red for negative variance (cash short), green for positive (cash over), gold for zero.

elements.name: Table view (ViewToggle)
elements.description: Toggle between card and table views. Table shows columns: Branch, Opened At, Closed At, Duration, Opening Cash, Closing Cash, Variance, Status.

elements.name: SortableTableHeader
elements.description: Clickable column headers for sorting in table view.

elements.name: StatusBadge
elements.description: Coloured status pills: green for OPEN shifts, grey for CLOSED.

elements.name: Reference number
elements.description: Each shift has an auto-generated reference number (e.g. SHF-001). Shown under the branch name in both card and table views so you can distinguish between shifts at the same branch.

before_you_start.text: You need the shifts.open permission to open a shift and shifts.close to close one. These are separate permissions — a cashier may have open but not close.
A branch must exist before a shift can be opened for it. Create branches on the Branch Settings page.
Only one shift can be open per branch at a time. If a previous shift was not closed, you must close it before opening a new one.

common_tasks.task: Open a shift at the start of the day
steps: Select your branch from the Branch selector.
Count the opening cash in the till and enter the amount in Opening cash.
Click Open Shift. POS is now enabled at this branch.

common_tasks.task: Close a shift at end of day
steps: Count the physical cash remaining in the till.
Select the open shift from the Shift selector.
Enter the counted amount in Closing cash. If it differs from the expected amount, enter a reason in Variance reason.
Click Close Shift. The shift is closed and any approval workflow triggers automatically.

warnings: Only one shift can be open per branch at a time. If a previous shift was never closed, the Open Shift button is blocked for that branch — close the old shift first.
If shift tracking is enabled in Business Settings, POS is blocked for a branch until a shift is open. Staff will see an error at checkout until someone opens the shift.

Large cash variances at close may trigger an approval request. The POS at that branch remains usable while the approval is pending — but the variance is flagged for manager review in the Approvals page.

entries.title: Create Business Account
entries.overview: Use this page to register your business on the system. Fill in your business name, your own name, your email, a password, and choose a subscription plan. After submitting, you will be sent a verification email — you must verify it before you can log in.

before_you_start.text: You need a valid email address that you can access — a verification link will be sent there.
If you were invited by an existing business, do not use this page. Use the Accept Invite page instead.

elements.name: Business Name field
elements.description: The official name of your business. This is what will appear across the system and on receipts.

elements.name: Owner Name field
elements.description: Your full name as the business owner. This becomes your display name in the system.

elements.name: Email field
elements.description: Your email address. This is your login username and where verification and notification emails will be sent.

elements.name: Password field
elements.description: Choose a strong password. Must meet the minimum requirements shown below the field (length, complexity).

elements.name: Show / Hide Password button
elements.description: Toggles whether your password is visible or hidden as you type.

elements.name: Subscription Plan dropdown
elements.description: Choose the plan that fits your business: Starter (basic features), Business (full features), or Enterprise (advanced multi-branch). You can change this later.
elements.notes: This is a trial — you will not be charged immediately.

elements.name: Create Account button
elements.description: Submits your details and creates the business account. You will be redirected to the email verification page.

elements.name: KPI preview cards
elements.description: The three cards at the top (Business Name, Owner, Status) update live as you fill in the form to confirm what you have set.

elements.name: Trial hint text
elements.description: Shows which subscription tier the trial will start with and what it includes.

elements.name: Password show/hide toggle
elements.description: Toggle password visibility in the password field to verify what you typed.

elements.name: Real-time KPI status
elements.description: KPI cards update in real-time as you fill out the form, showing status like 'set', 'pending', 'creating', 'ready'.

common_tasks.task: Register a new business
steps: Fill in your Business Name, Owner Name, and Email.
Set a password that meets the requirements.
Choose a subscription plan from the dropdown.
Click Create Account.
Check your email for the verification link and follow it.

warnings: You cannot log in until you verify your email address. Check your inbox (and spam folder) after registering.
Each email address can be registered under multiple businesses, but each business needs its own registration.
Your subscription plan choice here is for the free trial only — you can change it later in Settings.

related_pages.reason: After registering, you are redirected here to verify your email.
After verification, log in here.

entries.title: Stock on Hand
entries.overview: Monitor current stock levels using redesigned stock health cards with three-stat layout (on-hand, in-transit, total) in color-tinted containers, progress bars, stock values, and color-coded days remaining (red critical, amber warning, green healthy). KPI cards feature icons (Database, AlertCircle, Lightbulb, Building2) with tinted backgrounds and FlipCounter animations. Reorder suggestions appear in a Card panel with Lightbulb icon header. Reorder form uses labeled TextInput fields inside a collapsible Card.
before_you_start: Snapshots only exist for variants with 'Track Stock' enabled — variants with tracking turned off will not appear in this list.
The Branch filter pre-fills with the active branch from the branch scope switcher at the top of the app. Clear it to see stock across all branches.
Reorder suggestions are computed automatically and appear only when a variant's on-hand plus in-transit quantity falls below its configured minimum.
Reorder points are per branch-and-variant combination — the same variant can have different reorder thresholds at different branches.
You need stock.read to view snapshots and suggestions. You need stock.write to save or update reorder points.

elements.label: Stock Snapshots
elements.description: Icon-card with Database icon in blue-tinted rounded container. Total number of stock snapshot records for the current filters. FlipCounter animates the number on load.

elements.label: Reorder Points
elements.description: Icon-card with AlertCircle icon in amber-tinted container. Count of configured reorder rules in scope. FlipCounter animates the count.

elements.label: Reorder Suggestions
elements.description: Icon-card with Lightbulb icon in emerald-tinted container. Count of variants currently below their configured minimum quantity.

elements.label: Active Branch
elements.description: Icon-card with Building2 icon in gold-tinted container. Name of the branch currently selected in the branch scope switcher, or 'Global' when no branch is active.

elements.label: View Toggle
elements.description: Switches between stock health card grid (three-stat boxes, progress bar, days remaining) and table view (sortable columns wrapped in Card with image thumbnails and AlertTriangle icons for low stock).

elements.label: Search
elements.description: Debounced text search (350 ms) that filters snapshots by variant name. Updates the list automatically as you type.

elements.label: Advanced Filters
elements.description: Expands or collapses the advanced filter row containing Branch, Variant, Status, and Category filters.

elements.label: Reset Filters
elements.description: Clears all active filters — search text, branch, variant, status, and category — and reloads the full snapshot list from page 1.

elements.label: Branch Filter
elements.description: Filters snapshots to a single branch. Automatically pre-fills with the active branch if one is set. Select 'Global' to see stock across all branches your role has access to.

elements.label: Variant Filter
elements.description: Typeahead selector that filters the list to a single variant. Searches variant names live as you type.

elements.label: Status Filter
elements.description: Filters by the variant's catalog status — Active, Inactive, or Archived. Note: this is the variant's status, not the snapshot's. An Archived variant still has a snapshot but can no longer receive adjustments.

elements.label: Category Filter
elements.description: Typeahead selector that filters snapshots by the product category of the variant. Useful for reviewing stock for a specific product line.

elements.label: Stock health indicator
elements.description: Redesigned card layout with three-stat row: on-hand (blue tint), in-transit (amber tint), total (emerald tint) in rounded containers. Product image with nvi-img-zoom, stock value, color-coded days remaining with AlertTriangle icon for critical, progress bar for stock health. Low-stock cards have a red left border accent.

elements.label: Days remaining warning
elements.description: Color-coded indicator on each card and table row. Red with AlertTriangle icon and nvi-bounce-in animation for critical (7 days or fewer), amber for warning (8-30 days), muted for healthy (over 30 days).

elements.label: Stock Health Cards
elements.description: Card view using redesigned stock health cards. Each card shows product thumbnail, branch name, three-stat boxes (on-hand/in-transit/total), stock value, days remaining, progress bar, and adjust button with Pencil icon.

elements.label: Stock Snapshot Table
elements.description: Table view wrapped in Card component. Columns: rounded-lg image thumbnail with nvi-img-zoom, branch, variant name, on-hand quantity with AlertTriangle icon for low stock, in-transit, total, stock value, color-coded days remaining, status, and adjust action with Pencil icon. Low stock rows have red/amber tinted backgrounds.

elements.label: Pagination Controls
elements.description: Cursor-based navigation with previous and next page buttons and a page size selector. The default page size for this page is 50 (larger than most other pages). Changing page size resets to page 1.

elements.label: Reorder Branch Selector
elements.description: Labeled branch selector in the reorder form Card. Independent of the filter branch selector. Required — pre-fills with the active branch if one is set.

elements.label: Reorder Variant Selector
elements.description: Labeled typeahead variant selector in the reorder form Card. Required. Searches variant names live as you type.

elements.label: Min Quantity
elements.description: TextInput with label for the minimum stock level. When on-hand plus in-transit falls below this value, a reorder suggestion is generated. Required — numeric, minimum 0.

elements.label: Reorder Quantity
elements.description: TextInput with label for the default reorder quantity. The suggested quantity will be the larger of the deficit or this value. Required — numeric, minimum 0.

elements.label: Save Reorder Point
elements.description: Saves the reorder point with Save icon, nvi-press animation, and rounded-xl styling. This is an upsert. Requires stock.write permission.

elements.label: Reorder Points List
elements.description: Clean mini-table inside a Card (glow disabled) listing all configured reorder points. Columns: branch, variant, minimum quantity, reorder quantity, status.

elements.label: Reorder Suggestions Panel
elements.description: Card panel with Lightbulb icon header showing variants below their minimum stock level in a clean table grid. Columns: branch, variant, on hand, in transit, minimum quantity, suggested quantity. Empty when all stock levels are above minimums.

elements.label: FlipCounter KPI animation
elements.description: KPI icon-cards use animated FlipCounter numbers that roll up when data loads. Each KPI has a distinct icon and color-tinted container for visual identity.

elements.label: Stock value display
elements.description: Calculated as quantity times default cost. Shown on each stock health card with currency formatting and in the table view column.

elements.label: ProgressBar stock level
elements.description: Color-coded visual bar on each stock health card. Red for 5 or fewer units, amber for 6-20, green for above 20. Provides at-a-glance stock health assessment.

elements.label: Low stock colour coding
elements.description: Stock cards with 5 or fewer units show a red left border accent. Table rows use red/amber tinted backgrounds. AlertTriangle icon appears inline for critically low quantities.

elements.label: Bulk Stock Count button
elements.description: A button in the page header with rounded-xl styling and nvi-press animation that navigates to the Stock Count Wizard.

elements.label: Image thumbnails
elements.description: Variant images in both card and table views with rounded-lg corners and nvi-img-zoom hover effect. Cards show 48px thumbnails; table shows 32px versions.

elements.label: Adjust button (per card/row)
elements.description: A quick action button with Pencil icon on each stock card and table row. Uses nvi-press animation and rounded-xl styling.

elements.label: SortableTableHeader
elements.description: Clickable column headers for sorting by Variant, Branch, Quantity, Stock Value, Days Remaining, and Status.

elements.label: In-transit quantity
elements.description: Shows the quantity of stock currently in transit. Displayed in its own amber-tinted stat box in card view and as a separate column in table view.

common_tasks.title: View stock on hand for a specific branch or variant
steps: Open Advanced Filters.
Select the branch from the Branch Filter, or type the variant name into the Variant Filter.
The snapshot list reloads showing only matching records.
Switch to Table view for a denser view including unit labels and in-transit quantities.

common_tasks.title: Search for a specific variant's stock level
steps: Type the variant name into the Search box.
The list filters automatically after 350 ms.
If the variant appears in multiple branches, all its snapshots are shown — use the Branch Filter to narrow to one branch.

common_tasks.title: Switch between card and table view
steps: Click the View Toggle in the page header.
Table view adds a thumbnail column and shows the base unit alongside the on-hand quantity.
Cards view is better for a quick at-a-glance overview per variant/branch combination.

common_tasks.title: Create a reorder point for a variant and branch
steps: Scroll to the Reorder Points panel.
Select the branch from the Reorder Branch Selector (pre-fills with your active branch).
Type the variant name in the Reorder Variant Selector and select it.
Enter the Min Quantity — the level below which a suggestion will be generated.
Enter the Reorder Quantity — the default amount to order when restocking.
Click Save Reorder Point. The new rule appears in the Reorder Points List below.

common_tasks.title: Update an existing reorder point
steps: In the Reorder Points form, select the same branch and variant as the existing reorder point.
Enter the new Min Quantity and Reorder Quantity values.
Click Save Reorder Point — the existing record is overwritten (upsert). No duplicate is created.

common_tasks.title: Check which variants need restocking
steps: Scroll to the Reorder Suggestions panel at the bottom of the page.
Any variant shown here has on-hand plus in-transit stock below its configured minimum.
The Suggested Quantity column shows how much to order — the larger of the deficit or the configured reorder quantity.
If the panel is empty, all stock levels are above their minimums.

common_tasks.title: Filter stock by category or variant status
steps: Click Advanced Filters to reveal the full filter row.
Select a category from the Category Filter to see only variants in that product line.
Use the Status Filter to show only Active, Inactive, or Archived variants.
Click Reset Filters to clear all filters and return to the full list.

common_tasks.title: Change the active branch to see branch-specific stock
steps: Use the branch scope switcher in the top navigation bar to select a branch.
The Branch Filter and Reorder Branch Selector both pre-fill with the selected branch automatically.
Stock snapshots, reorder points, and suggestions all reload scoped to that branch.

warnings: The Status filter applies to the variant's catalog status, not the snapshot. An Archived variant still has a snapshot record but can no longer receive new adjustments or counts.
Saving a reorder point for a branch and variant that already has one will silently update it — there will be no confirmation prompt and no duplicate entry.
Reorder points cannot be deleted from this page. There is no delete button or backend delete endpoint — contact support to remove a reorder point you no longer need.

In Transit quantity represents stock in an approved transfer that has not yet physically arrived at the branch. It counts toward the total and toward the reorder suggestion calculation but is not available for sale.
Batch management (creating and browsing batches with expiry dates) has no dedicated frontend page. Batches can be referenced in stock adjustments but must be managed via the API or support.

entries.title: Stock Adjustments
entries.overview: Record positive (add) and negative (remove) manual stock adjustments for any variant at any branch. Each adjustment requires a reason that determines its financial impact — losses reduce profit, purchased stock is recorded as a cost, sold-outside-POS and corrections have no financial effect. The page works fully offline — adjustments made without connectivity are queued in IndexedDB and synced automatically when the device reconnects. A separate panel allows batch records to be created before adjustments are recorded against them.

before_you_start.text: stock.write is required to submit adjustments and create batches.
Variants must exist before adjustments can be recorded — create them at /catalog/variants.
Branches must be configured; the adjustment form defaults to the active branch scope but can be changed per adjustment.

For negative adjustments a loss reason is mandatory. Options: DAMAGED, LOST, STOLEN, EXPIRED, SHRINKAGE (recorded as losses), SOLD_OUTSIDE_POS (no financial impact — stock was sold), CORRECTION (no financial impact). For positive adjustments a gain reason is mandatory. Options: UNRECORDED_PURCHASE, INITIAL_STOCK (recorded as stock purchase costs), FOUND_STOCK, RETURN_NOT_LOGGED, CORRECTION (no financial impact).
Batches are optional — only create and assign them if your business tracks stock by lot number or expiry date.

elements.name: KPI – Recent Adjustments
elements.description: Count of adjustment movements currently loaded in the history panel based on active filters. Shows up to 50 entries. If this number is lower than expected, check whether date range or type filters are narrowing the results. Clear all filters to see the broadest recent set.

elements.name: KPI – Catalog Variants
elements.description: Count of variants loaded into the adjustment form's variant dropdown. If a variant you need is missing from the dropdown, it may not exist yet — create it at /catalog/variants first, then return here.

elements.name: KPI – Open Branches
elements.description: Count of branches loaded into the form's branch selector. If a branch is missing, check that it is active in /settings/branches. Adjustments must always be assigned to a specific branch — the global context is not valid.

elements.name: KPI – Batch Tracking
elements.description: Shows 'Active' when batches are loaded for the currently selected branch and variant combination in the adjustment form, or 'None' when no batches exist for that combination. Use this as a quick signal for whether batch selection is available before submitting an adjustment.

elements.name: Offline PIN Input
elements.description: Visible only when the device is offline, an offline PIN has been configured, and the PIN has not yet been verified in this session. Enter the PIN to unlock offline adjustment submission. The field is a password input — entered characters are hidden. If you have forgotten the PIN, go to /offline to reset it while online.

elements.name: Unlock Button
elements.description: Verifies the offline PIN entered in the PIN Input. On success, unlocks the Submit Adjustment button for offline use and shows a confirmation message. On failure, shows an invalid PIN message — re-enter the correct PIN. The PIN session remains verified until the page is reloaded.

elements.name: Branch Selector (adjustment form)
elements.description: Required. Selects the branch where this stock adjustment will be recorded. Defaults to the active branch scope when the page loads. Changing the branch clears the Batch Selector since batches are branch-specific. All stock movements created from this form are scoped to this branch.

elements.name: Variant Selector (adjustment form)
elements.description: Required. Async typeahead search — type part of the product or variant name and matching options appear. When a variant is selected, the Unit Selector is automatically populated with the variant's sell unit (falling back to base unit). Also triggers loading of batches for the selected branch and variant combination.

elements.name: Quantity Input
elements.description: Required. The number of units to add (POSITIVE) or remove (NEGATIVE). Must be a positive number — the direction is set by the Adjustment Type selector, not the sign of this value. The quantity is recorded in the selected unit.

elements.name: Unit Selector (adjustment form)
elements.description: The unit of measure for the quantity entered. Auto-populated when a variant is selected (uses the variant's sell unit, falling back to base unit). Can be manually overridden if the adjustment is being recorded in a different unit. Ensure the unit matches what you physically counted or measured.

elements.name: Adjustment Type Selector
elements.description: Sets the direction of the adjustment. POSITIVE adds stock to the branch balance (e.g. receiving a delivery not through a purchase order, opening stock entry) and reveals the Gain Reason selector. NEGATIVE removes stock (e.g. recording a loss, theft, damage, or expiry) and reveals the Loss Reason selector. Both directions require a reason that determines the financial impact.

elements.name: Batch Selector (adjustment form)
elements.description: Optional. Links this adjustment to a specific stock batch (e.g. a lot with an expiry date).
