Only shows batches that match both the selected branch and selected variant — if no batches appear, either none exist for this combination or the variant/branch has not been selected yet. Leave blank if batch tracking is not in use.

elements.name: Loss Reason Selector
elements.description: Visible and required only when Adjustment Type is NEGATIVE. Options: DAMAGED, LOST, STOLEN, EXPIRED, SHRINKAGE (all recorded as losses that reduce profit), SOLD_OUTSIDE_POS (no financial impact — the stock was sold outside the POS), CORRECTION (no financial impact — just fixing records), OTHER (recorded as a loss). A hint below the selector explains the financial consequence of the selected reason.

elements.name: Gain Reason Selector
elements.description: Visible and required only when Adjustment Type is POSITIVE. Options: UNRECORDED_PURCHASE, INITIAL_STOCK (both recorded as stock purchase costs that reduce profit), FOUND_STOCK, RETURN_NOT_LOGGED (no financial impact — stock appeared without cost), CORRECTION (no financial impact — just fixing records), OTHER (no financial impact). A hint below the selector explains the financial consequence of the selected reason.

elements.name: Financial Impact Hint
elements.description: A small text line below the reason selector that explains the financial consequence of the selected reason. For example: 'This will be recorded as a loss in your reports' or 'This will be recorded as a stock purchase cost in your reports' or 'This will not affect your profit or loss reports'. Updates dynamically as the reason selection changes.

elements.name: Reason Input
elements.description: Optional free-text note providing additional context for the adjustment (e.g. 'Opening balance as at 01-Mar-2026' or 'Damaged during transit from supplier'). Stored alongside the movement record. This is separate from the Loss Reason — use it to add detail that the loss reason category alone does not capture.

elements.name: Submit Adjustment Button
elements.description: Submit button inside the Adjustment modal. Posts the adjustment to the backend (POST /stock/adjustments), refreshes the adjustment list, closes the modal, and resets the form on success. If the device is offline, the adjustment is enqueued to IndexedDB and synced when connectivity returns. Disabled if stock.write permission is absent, required fields are empty, or (offline) the PIN has not been verified or sync is blocked.

elements.name: Adjustment modal
elements.description: Centered modal opened from the 'Submit adjustment' button in the page header. Hosts the full adjustment form: branch, variant, quantity, unit, type (POSITIVE/NEGATIVE), batch, loss/gain reason, financial-impact hint, and reason notes. ESC or backdrop click closes without saving.

elements.name: Batch modal
elements.description: Centered modal opened from the 'Create batch' button in the page header. Hosts the batch form: branch, variant, batch code (with auto-generate wand button), expiry date. ESC or backdrop click closes without saving.

elements.name: Batch Branch Selector
elements.description: Selects the branch this batch belongs to. Required to create a batch. Defaults to the active branch scope. Batches are branch-specific — a batch created here will only appear in the adjustment form when the same branch is selected.

elements.name: Batch Variant Selector
elements.description: Async typeahead search for the variant this batch belongs to. Required to create a batch. The same search behaviour as the adjustment form's Variant Selector — type part of the product or variant name to find it.

elements.name: Batch Code Input
elements.description: Required. A human-readable identifier for this batch — typically a lot number, supplier batch reference, or internal code (e.g. 'LOT-2026-003'). Must be unique within the business. This code appears in the Batch Selector dropdown when recording adjustments against this batch.

elements.name: Batch Expiry Date Picker
elements.description: Optional. The expiry date for this batch. Displayed alongside the batch code in the Batch Selector dropdown when recording adjustments (e.g. 'LOT-2026-003 (expires 2026-06-30)'). Cannot be changed after the batch is created — if entered incorrectly, create a new batch with the correct date.

elements.name: Create Batch Button
elements.description: Creates the batch record via POST /stock/batches and resets the batch form on success. Disabled if Branch, Variant, or Code are empty, or if stock.write permission is absent. After creation, the new batch immediately appears in the Batch Selector in the adjustment form when the same branch and variant are selected.

elements.name: View Toggle (history panel)
elements.description: Switches the Recent Adjustments history between card view and table view. Card view shows each adjustment as a compact row with variant name, type, quantity, branch, datetime, and reason. Table view presents the same data in a sortable grid and adds a variant image thumbnail column.

elements.name: Search Input (history filters)
elements.description: Filters the adjustment history by variant name. Debounced at 350 ms — results update shortly after you stop typing. Use this to find all adjustments for a specific product quickly. Clear the field to show all adjustments matching the other active filters.

elements.name: Advanced Filters Toggle (history)
elements.description: Expands or collapses the Branch Filter, Type Filter, Reason Filter, From Date, and To Date controls in the history panel. Off by default. Turn on when you need to audit adjustments for a specific branch, time window, or loss category. Closing the panel does not clear active filter values.

elements.name: Reset Filters Button (history)
elements.description: Clears all active history filters (search, branch, type, reason, from date, to date) and reloads the adjustment list with no filters applied. Use this to return to the default 50-entry view after a focused audit query.

elements.name: Branch Filter (history)
elements.description: Filters the adjustment history to a specific branch. Defaults to the active branch scope. Includes a 'Global/All branches' option to show adjustments across all branches. Combine with the date range filters to audit stock changes at a specific location over a period.

elements.name: Adjustment Type Filter
elements.description: Filters the history to show only positive adjustments (stock added), only negative adjustments (stock removed), or all adjustment types. Use ADJUSTMENT_NEGATIVE to audit losses and shrinkage for a branch or time period.

elements.name: Reason Filter
elements.description: Filters the adjustment history by the free-text reason field. Partial matches are supported — typing 'opening' will match any adjustment whose reason contains that word. Use this to find all adjustments made for a specific stated reason across the filtered date range.

elements.name: From Date Filter
elements.description: Sets the start date for the adjustment history date range filter. Only adjustments created on or after this date are shown. Use in combination with the To Date filter to scope results to a specific period (e.g. a weekly or monthly audit window).

elements.name: To Date Filter
elements.description: Sets the end date for the adjustment history date range filter. Only adjustments created on or before this date are shown. Use together with the From Date filter to define a precise audit window.

elements.name: Recent Adjustments History
elements.description: Displays the 50 most recent stock adjustment movements matching the current filters. Card view shows: variant name and type, quantity with unit, branch name, datetime, and reason. Table view adds a variant image column and presents the same fields in a scannable grid. The list is read-only — adjustments cannot be edited or deleted after submission; corrections must be made by recording a new adjustment in the opposite direction.
elements.notes: Adjustments are immutable. To reverse a positive adjustment, record a negative adjustment of the same quantity. To reverse a negative, record a positive.

elements.name: Unit conversion hint
elements.description: A dynamic hint below the unit selector that shows the conversion calculation when a variant has different base and sell units. For example: '2 boxes = 10 pieces in stock'. Hidden when no conversion applies.

common_tasks.task: Record a positive stock adjustment (add stock)
steps: Select the Branch where stock is being added.
Search for and select the Variant in the Variant Selector.
Enter the Quantity to add.
Confirm the Unit is correct (auto-populated from the variant).
Ensure Adjustment Type is set to POSITIVE.
Optionally enter a Reason (e.g. 'Opening balance 01-Mar-2026').
Click Submit Adjustment. The form resets and the new entry appears in the history.

common_tasks.task: Record a negative stock adjustment (remove stock / log a loss)
steps: Select the Branch and Variant.
Enter the Quantity to remove.
Set Adjustment Type to NEGATIVE — the Loss Reason selector appears.
Select the appropriate Loss Reason (DAMAGED, LOST, STOLEN, EXPIRED, SHRINKAGE, or OTHER).
Optionally add a free-text Reason for additional detail.
Click Submit Adjustment.

common_tasks.task: Record a stock adjustment against a specific batch
steps: Ensure the batch exists — if not, create it in the Batch Creation panel first.
Select the Branch and Variant in the adjustment form.
The Batch Selector populates with batches for that combination — select the correct batch.
Enter Quantity, Unit, and Adjustment Type as normal.
Click Submit Adjustment. The movement is recorded against that specific batch.

common_tasks.task: Create a new batch
steps: In the Batch Creation panel, select the Branch and Variant the batch belongs to.
Enter the Batch Code (e.g. a lot number or supplier reference).
Optionally set an Expiry Date.
Click Create Batch.
The batch is now available in the Batch Selector when adjusting the same variant and branch.

common_tasks.task: Record a stock adjustment while offline
steps: If an offline PIN is required, enter it in the Offline PIN Input and click Unlock.
Complete the adjustment form as normal.
Click Submit Adjustment — the adjustment is queued in IndexedDB and a 'Queued' confirmation appears.
When the device reconnects, the queued adjustment syncs automatically to the server.
Go to /offline to check the sync queue status if needed.

common_tasks.task: Verify the offline PIN
steps: When the PIN panel appears (offline with PIN required), type the PIN in the Offline PIN Input.
Click Unlock.
On success, a confirmation message appears and the Submit Adjustment button becomes active.
If the PIN is rejected, re-enter it — check /offline if you need to reset the PIN.

common_tasks.task: Filter adjustments by branch and date range
steps: Enable the Advanced Filters Toggle in the history panel.
Select the target branch from the Branch Filter.
Set a From Date and To Date to define the audit window.
The history updates to show only adjustments for that branch within the date range.
Click Reset Filters to return to the default view.

common_tasks.task: Filter adjustments by type or reason
steps: Enable the Advanced Filters Toggle.
Select ADJUSTMENT_NEGATIVE from the Adjustment Type Filter to see losses only, or ADJUSTMENT_POSITIVE to see additions only.
Optionally type a keyword in the Reason Filter to narrow further.
The history updates immediately.
Click Reset Filters to clear all filters.

common_tasks.task: Switch to table view for the adjustment history
steps: In the Recent Adjustments panel header, click the View Toggle to switch to Table view.
The history renders as a sortable table with image, variant, type, quantity, unit, branch, reason, and datetime columns.
Switch back to Cards view using the same toggle.

common_tasks.task: Correct a mistaken adjustment
steps: Locate the incorrect adjustment in the history panel to confirm its details.
Record a new adjustment in the opposite direction with the same quantity (e.g. if 10 units were incorrectly added as POSITIVE, record a NEGATIVE adjustment for 10 units).
Add a free-text Reason explaining the correction (e.g. 'Reversing erroneous entry from [date]').
Click Submit Adjustment.

warnings: Negative adjustments require a loss reason — the system blocks submission with a warning message if this field is left empty when type is NEGATIVE.
Adjustments are immutable once submitted — they cannot be edited or deleted. To correct a mistake, record a new adjustment in the opposite direction.

Offline adjustments are queued locally and will not appear in the history panel until the device reconnects and syncs. If sync is blocked (shown by a warning banner), queued actions will not upload until the block is cleared at /offline.
If an offline PIN is required and not yet verified in this session, adjustment submission is blocked even when all form fields are complete — enter the PIN to unlock.
Batch expiry dates cannot be changed after creation — if a date was entered incorrectly, create a new batch with the correct date and use that going forward.

related_pages.reason: View current stock balances for all variants after recording adjustments.
Variants must exist before adjustments can be recorded against them.
Full movement history including all movement types — not just adjustments.
Use stock counts for systematic inventory reconciliation instead of individual adjustments.
Manage offline mode settings, PIN configuration, and sync queue for queued adjustments.

entries.title: Stock Counts
entries.overview: Reconcile physical inventory with system quantities. The redesigned page centres on variance — each card and table row makes it instantly obvious whether a count found a shortage (red), surplus (green), or exact match (muted). KPIs show total counts, shortage total, surplus total, and exact-match count. Submit single-item spot-checks via the collapsible form, or open the Count Wizard for guided multi-item sessions. Stock counts are blocked in offline mode and may trigger an approval workflow depending on your role's approval tier.

elements.name: KPI — Total Counts
elements.description: Total STOCK_COUNT_VARIANCE movement records across all pages (from API total). Replaces the old 'Recent Counts' KPI which only showed current-page count.

elements.name: KPI — Shortage Total
elements.description: Sum of all shortage (negative) quantities on the current page. Displayed in red with an AlertTriangle icon. A high number indicates frequent under-counts that may warrant investigation.

elements.name: KPI — Surplus Total
elements.description: Sum of all surplus (positive) quantities on the current page. Displayed in green with an ArrowUp icon. Persistent surpluses may indicate unrecorded purchases or returns.

elements.name: KPI — Exact Matches
elements.description: Number of counts on the current page where variance is exactly zero. Displayed with a Check icon. A higher ratio means better inventory accuracy.

elements.name: Open Wizard button
elements.description: Link in the page header with a Wand2 icon. Navigates to /{locale}/stock/counts/wizard for guided multi-item stock takes. Use the single-item form for quick spot-checks.

elements.name: ViewToggle (Cards / Table)
elements.description: Switches between variance-hero card view and dense table view for the count history.

elements.name: CollapsibleSection — New Count form
elements.description: Expandable form for submitting a single stock count. Contains Branch selector, Variant async search, Counted Quantity (TextInput with label), Unit selector, unit conversion hint, Expected Quantity (read-only TextInput), Batch selector, contextual shortage/surplus reason dropdowns, Reason notes (TextInput), and Submit button. Closes automatically after successful submission.

elements.name: Branch selector
elements.description: Required. The branch where the physical count took place. Auto-populated from active branch scope. The Expected Quantity shown is specific to this branch.

elements.name: Variant search (async)
elements.description: Required. Async typeahead search across all variants. Select the exact variant including size/flavour. Changing the variant auto-sets the Unit field.

elements.name: Counted Quantity (TextInput)
elements.description: Number input with label. Enter the physical quantity you found. The variance is calculated automatically against Expected Quantity. Counts are irreversible.

elements.name: Unit selector
elements.description: Auto-set to the variant's sell unit or base unit. The server converts to base units before computing variance.

elements.name: Unit conversion hint
elements.description: Dynamic hint showing base-unit equivalent of the entered quantity for the selected variant.

elements.name: Expected Quantity (read-only TextInput)
elements.description: Read-only TextInput with muted styling showing the system's current on-hand quantity. Sourced from the stock snapshot loaded at page startup.

elements.name: Batch selector
elements.description: Optional. Links count to a specific batch code. Loaded from GET /stock/batches for the selected branch and variant.

elements.name: Shortage Reason dropdown
elements.description: Appears only when counted < expected. Options: DAMAGED, LOST, STOLEN, EXPIRED, SHRINKAGE, SOLD_OUTSIDE_POS, CORRECTION, OTHER. Contextual hint explains financial impact.

elements.name: Surplus Reason dropdown
elements.description: Appears only when counted > expected. Options: UNRECORDED_PURCHASE, FOUND_STOCK, RETURN_NOT_LOGGED, CORRECTION, OTHER. Contextual hint explains financial impact.

elements.name: Reason Notes (TextInput)
elements.description: Optional free-text note attached to the movement record. Stored in audit log and visible in Stock Movements history.

elements.name: Submit Count button
elements.description: Posts to POST /stock/counts. Uses ClipboardCheck icon and rounded-xl styling. Requires stock.write permission. On success, form resets and list refreshes.

elements.name: Variance-hero count card
elements.description: Card view redesign. Each card leads with the variance as the hero — a large coloured label ('+5 surplus' green, '-3 shortage' red, '0 match' muted). Below: variant name, stat boxes for quantity and unit, a colour-coded reason badge, and a footer with branch (MapPin icon) and relative time (Clock icon). Recount button appears on hover (RotateCcw icon).

elements.name: Recount button
elements.description: Appears on hover on each card (always visible on mobile). Pre-fills the count form with the same variant and branch, opens the form, and auto-switches to card view if in table view. Uses RotateCcw icon.

elements.name: Table view (Card-wrapped)
elements.description: Sortable table wrapped in a Card component. Columns: Variant, Quantity (with variance icon + colour coding), Unit, Branch, Reason (colour-coded badge), Date, Recount action. Variance uses AlertTriangle/ArrowUp/Check icons.

elements.name: EmptyState
elements.description: Shown when no count records exist. Floating ClipboardCheck icon, title, and action button to open the form.

elements.name: Banner (success / error)
elements.description: Replaces StatusBanner. Shows success or error messages after form submission with dismiss button and severity-based styling.

elements.name: Wizard — KPIs
elements.description: The Count Wizard at /{locale}/stock/counts/wizard shows four KPIs: Draft Lines (total rows in the form including incomplete), Valid Lines (rows with branch, variant, and quantity all filled — these are the only lines that submit), Current Step (Counts or Review), and Branches (total available).

elements.name: Wizard — Step indicator
elements.description: Visual pill row at the top of the wizard showing the two steps: Counts (entry) and Review (confirmation). The active step is highlighted. Steps are linear — you must click Next to advance and Back to return. You cannot skip the review step.

elements.name: Wizard — Count line entry row
elements.description: Each count line in the wizard has two sub-rows. Row 1: Branch select, Variant async search, Counted Quantity input, Remove button (deletes this line). Row 2: Unit select (auto-set from variant), Batch select (loaded per branch+variant pair), Reason input. An Expected Quantity hint is shown below each line from the stock snapshot. Lines are independent — each has its own branch and variant.

elements.name: Wizard — Add Line button
elements.description: Appends a new blank count line to the wizard form. The new line inherits the active branch scope if set. There is no limit on the number of lines — add one per variant you need to count in this session.

elements.name: Wizard — Next button
elements.description: Advances the wizard from the Counts step to the Review step. Disabled until at least one valid line exists (branch + variant + quantity all filled). Incomplete lines are not shown in the review — they are silently excluded from submission.

elements.name: Wizard — Review summary
elements.description: Step 2 of the wizard. Shows all valid lines in read-only summary format: variant label, counted quantity, unit, and branch. Review carefully before submitting — this is the last chance to catch errors. Use Back to return to the Counts step and correct any line.

elements.name: Wizard — Submit Counts button
elements.description: Sends all valid lines to POST /stock/counts in parallel (Promise.all). Requires stock.write. If any individual line fails (e.g. batch expired, approval triggered on one line), other lines may still commit — check the Recent Counts history after submission to verify each line.

elements.name: Wizard — Back button
elements.description: Returns from the Review step to the Counts step without losing any data. Use this to correct a line before submitting. All lines (including incomplete ones) are preserved when going back.

elements.name: CollapsibleSection (New Count form)
elements.description: The count form is hidden behind a 'New Count' button. Closes automatically after successful submission.

elements.name: Shortage / Surplus totals
elements.description: KPI values showing the total shortage and surplus quantities from recent counts. Shortages appear in red, surpluses in green.

elements.name: Recount button
elements.description: Appears on each recent count entry. Click to pre-fill the count form with the same variant, branch, and unit for a quick recount.

elements.name: Expected quantity display
elements.description: Shows the system's expected quantity for the selected variant and branch in the count form. Compare against your counted quantity to see the variance.

elements.name: Variance colour coding
elements.description: In the recent counts list, quantities are colour-coded: red for shortages (counted < expected), green for surpluses (counted > expected).

elements.name: ViewToggle (Cards / Table)
elements.description: Toggle between card and table views for the recent counts list.

elements.name: SortableTableHeader
elements.description: Clickable column headers in table view for sorting by Variant, Quantity, Unit, Branch, Reason, and Date.

elements.name: Unit conversion hint
elements.description: A dynamic hint below the unit selector showing the conversion calculation for the selected variant. Shows the base unit equivalent of the quantity entered.

before_you_start.text: You need the 'stock.write' permission to submit stock counts. Without it, the Submit Count and Submit Counts buttons are disabled.

The variant must have 'trackStock' enabled. If a count is submitted for an untracked variant, the server silently returns null — no movement is created and no error is shown. Verify the variant's trackStock setting in Catalog → Variants if counts are not appearing.
Stock counts are blocked in offline mode. Unlike stock adjustments (which can be queued), count submissions return a 403 error if the device is offline. Plan count sessions during online periods.

Counts may trigger an approval workflow depending on your role's approval tier. If approval is required, the count is queued — the stock quantity does not change until an approver acts. Check the Approvals page if a count does not appear in Recent Counts history after submission.

common_tasks.task: Submit a single-item physical count
steps: Select the branch where the count took place.
Search for and select the exact variant (including size/SKU).
Note the Expected Quantity shown in the read-only field.
Enter the physical Counted Quantity you found, select the unit, and optionally select a batch and add a reason.
Click Submit Count. The form resets on success and the Recent Counts list updates.

common_tasks.task: Record multiple counts in one session using the wizard
steps: Click 'Open Wizard' in the page header to navigate to /stock/counts/wizard.
For each variant, fill in branch, variant, and counted quantity (plus unit, batch, and reason as needed).
Click 'Add Line' to append more rows.
Click Next when all lines are filled — only valid lines advance to Review.
Review the summary on step 2, then click Submit Counts to post all lines in parallel.

common_tasks.task: Compare counted quantity against the system expected quantity
steps: Select the branch and variant in the form.
The Expected Quantity field populates automatically with the system's on-hand balance.
Compare visually before submitting — a large gap may indicate a data entry error.
If the expected value seems wrong (another user recently adjusted stock), refresh the page before counting.

common_tasks.task: Attach a batch to a stock count
steps: Select the branch and variant first — the Batch selector only populates once both are chosen.
Select the batch from the dropdown (shown as code + expiry date).
Submit the count as normal. The STOCK_COUNT_VARIANCE movement will reference the batch ID.

common_tasks.task: View recent count history for a branch
steps: Select the branch in the form — the Recent Counts list refreshes automatically.
The list shows the last 50 STOCK_COUNT_VARIANCE movements for that branch.
Use the View Toggle to switch between cards and table layout.
For the full history with filters, go to Stock → Movements and filter by type STOCK_COUNT_VARIANCE.

common_tasks.task: Understand what happens when a count requires approval
steps: Submit a count as normal — if your role requires approval for stock counts, the server queues it.
The UI may show a success toast even though the count is pending — the stock quantity has NOT changed yet.
Navigate to Approvals to confirm whether a pending count request is waiting for review.
Once an approver approves the request, the count executes and the variance is recorded.

common_tasks.task: Handle a rejected count submission (batch expiry or negative quantity)
steps: If submission fails with a batch error, check the batch's expiry date in the Batch selector.
If the expiry policy is BLOCK, expired batches cannot be counted — remove the batch selection or choose a valid batch.
If submission fails with a quantity error, ensure the Counted Quantity is 0 or greater.
Correct the field and resubmit.

common_tasks.task: Switch count history between cards and table view
steps: Scroll to the Recent Counts panel below the entry form.
Click the View Toggle buttons (Cards / Table) in the panel header.
Table view shows Variant, Quantity, Unit, Branch, Reason, and Created At in columns.
Cards view shows the same data in an expanded block format.

warnings: Stock counts are completely blocked in offline mode — the server returns a 403 error regardless of the variant or quantity entered. Plan count sessions for when devices have connectivity.

If a count triggers an approval workflow, the backend queues the request and may return a success-like response that the UI shows as a success toast. The stock quantity does NOT change until an approver acts. Always check the Approvals page if a submitted count does not appear in the Recent Counts history.

The Count Wizard submits all valid lines in parallel. If one line fails (e.g. an expired batch on line 3), other lines may still commit. After a wizard submission, verify each line appears in the Recent Counts history rather than assuming all succeeded.

Counted quantity must be 0 or greater — the server rejects negative values. A counted quantity of 0 is valid and sets the stock to zero, recording a full shortage variance equal to the previous on-hand balance.

entries.title: Stock Count Wizard
entries.overview: Submit physical stock count results for one or more product variants. Each count line records how many units you physically found for a variant at a branch. The system compares your count against the recorded stock level and creates a stock adjustment for any variance — adding stock if you found more, removing it if you found less. Large variances may trigger an approval request before the adjustment is applied.

elements.name: Step indicator
elements.description: Shows your position across the two steps: Counts → Review. Add all count lines on the Counts step, then review the full summary before submitting.

elements.name: Branch selector (per line)
elements.description: Choose which branch this count line is for. Each line is branch-specific. If you are counting across multiple branches in one session, add separate lines for each branch — the wizard supports mixed-branch submissions.

elements.name: Variant selector (per line)
elements.description: Select the product variant you physically counted. Once selected, the System quantity field loads the current recorded stock level for that variant at the selected branch so you can compare against your physical count.

elements.name: Counted quantity input
elements.description: Enter the number of units you physically counted for this variant at this branch. This is your actual physical count — enter what you found, not what the system shows. The system calculates the variance automatically.

elements.name: Unit selector (per line)
elements.description: The unit of measure for the counted quantity. Select the same unit the variant is tracked in — usually the base unit (e.g. Case or Piece).

elements.name: Reason input (per line)
elements.description: Explain why you are submitting this count — for example 'Annual stock take', 'Post-delivery count', 'Shrinkage investigation', or 'Spot check'. The reason is stored on the resulting stock adjustment and is visible in the audit log and stock movement history.

elements.name: Batch selector (per line)
elements.description: If batch tracking is enabled in Business Settings, optionally specify which batch you are counting. Leave blank to count all stock of this variant at this branch regardless of batch.

elements.name: System quantity display
elements.description: Shows the current recorded stock level for this variant at this branch for comparison. The difference between this number and your counted quantity is the variance. A positive variance means you found more than recorded; negative means you found fewer.

elements.name: Add Line button
elements.description: Adds another count line. Add one line per variant you are counting. You can count multiple variants and multiple branches in a single wizard session.

elements.name: Remove Line button
elements.description: Removes that count line from the session. Use this to correct a mistake before advancing to the Review step.

elements.name: Next button
elements.description: Validates all count lines and advances to the Review step. Any lines with missing required fields will be highlighted for correction.

elements.name: Review step
elements.description: Full summary table showing each variant, branch, system quantity, your counted quantity, and the calculated variance. Review every line carefully — variances with a positive number will add stock; negative will remove it. Once you submit, all adjustments are applied immediately.

elements.name: Submit button
elements.description: Creates a stock adjustment record for every line with a non-zero variance. Each adjustment updates the branch stock level immediately and appears in the stock movement history and audit log. Lines where your count matches the system quantity exactly create no adjustment.

elements.name: Barcode input and lookup
elements.description: Type or scan a barcode to automatically find and add the matching variant to the count lines. Press Enter to submit the lookup.

elements.name: Per-line batch selector
elements.description: Each count line has a batch selector that loads available batches for the selected branch and variant combination.

elements.name: Variance calculation per line
elements.description: Each line shows the expected quantity, counted quantity, and calculated variance. Variance is colour-coded: green for zero, yellow for surplus, red for shortage.

elements.name: Expected quantity hint
elements.description: A small hint below each count line showing what the system expects the quantity to be. Compare against your physical count.

elements.name: Invalid lines section
elements.description: In the Review step, lines with errors (missing branch, variant, or quantity) are shown separately with error messages explaining what needs fixing.

elements.name: Partial submit
elements.description: If some lines are invalid, you can submit only the valid lines. A note explains that invalid lines will not be submitted. Use 'Submit Valid Only' to proceed.

elements.name: Visual progress bar
elements.description: A progress indicator at the top showing which step you are on (Counts or Review) with visual dots and connecting lines.

before_you_start.text: You need the stock.write permission.
The branches and variants you are counting must already exist in the system.
If batch tracking is enabled in Business Settings, you can optionally specify a batch for each count line.

common_tasks.task: Submit a stock count for multiple products
steps: Click Add Line for each variant you have physically counted.
For each line, select the branch, variant, enter the counted quantity, choose the unit, and enter a reason.
Click Next to advance to the Review step.
Review the variance column for each line — positive means found more than recorded, negative means fewer.
Click Submit. Stock adjustments are applied immediately for all lines with a variance.

common_tasks.task: Submit a count that reveals shrinkage (missing stock)
steps: Add a count line for the affected variant and branch.
Enter the quantity you physically found (which is less than the system shows).
In Reason, note 'Shrinkage investigation' or describe what may have happened.
Click Next, review the negative variance, then Submit. The stock level is reduced by the variance amount.

common_tasks.task: Count stock across multiple branches in one session
steps: Add one line per variant per branch combination — for example, add a line for 'Warehouse' and a separate line for 'Shop Front' for the same variant.
Fill in all details for each line.
Review the summary on the Review step, then Submit to apply all adjustments at once.

warnings: Each line with a variance creates an immediate stock adjustment — this affects stock levels, reports, and valuation right away. Review the Review step carefully before submitting.
Large variances may trigger approval requests depending on thresholds configured in Business Settings. The adjustment is held until the approval is granted.
You cannot undo a submitted count. If a line was submitted with an incorrect quantity, correct it with another stock count or a manual stock adjustment.

entries.title: Stock Movements
entries.overview: A complete, read-only ledger of every stock movement recorded across the business. Each row represents one inventory event — a sale, purchase, adjustment, transfer, return, stock count variance, or opening balance — with the exact quantity, movement type, batch, variant, branch, actor, and timestamp. Use this page to audit stock history, trace discrepancies, or verify that an expected movement was recorded. All filters are URL-persisted so you can share or bookmark a specific view.
before_you_start: You need the Stock Movements permission to view this page.
Movements are immutable — they cannot be edited or deleted. To correct an error, record a new adjustment.
The search box looks for text in the variant name or reason field. Use the Type and Actor filters to narrow further before searching.

elements.label: Movement Rows
elements.description: Count of movement records loaded in the current view. This reflects the page currently fetched, not the total across all pages. Load More to increase this count.

elements.label: Active Users
elements.description: Number of staff accounts available in the Actor filter. Useful for confirming that all expected users appear before filtering by actor.

elements.label: View Mode
elements.description: Shows whether the list is displayed as a table or as cards. Toggle with the View button in the page header.

elements.label: More Pages
elements.description: Indicates whether additional movement records exist beyond the current load. 'Yes' means the Load More button will fetch the next page of 50 records.

elements.label: Search
elements.description: Free-text search across variant name and reason. Input is debounced 350 ms before triggering a new fetch. Clears automatically when Reset Filters is pressed.

elements.label: Branch
elements.description: Filter movements to a specific branch. Defaults to the active branch selected in the global branch switcher. Set to 'All Branches' to view movements across all locations.

elements.label: Movement Type
elements.description: Filter by one of the 10 movement types: Opening Balance, Purchase In, Sale Out, Adjustment Positive, Adjustment Negative, Transfer Out, Transfer In, Return In, Return Out, or Stock Count Variance.

elements.label: Actor
elements.description: Filter movements to those created by a specific user. The list is populated from all staff accounts on the business.

elements.label: From Date
elements.description: Start of the date range for filtering movements. Only movements on or after this date are returned. Leave blank to include all dates.

elements.label: To Date
elements.description: End of the date range for filtering movements. Only movements on or before this date are returned. Combine with From Date to isolate a specific period.

elements.label: Reset Filters
elements.description: Clears all active filters and returns the view to the default state. The branch filter resets to the active global branch.

elements.label: Advanced / Less
elements.description: Reveals or hides the Branch, Type, Actor, From Date, and To Date filter controls. The search box is always visible.

elements.label: View Toggle (Table / Cards)
elements.description: Switches the movement list between a dense table layout (image, date, branch, variant, type, quantity, batch, actor) and a card layout showing the same fields in a larger format. Table is the default.

elements.label: Movement List
elements.description: The main data grid. Table columns are: thumbnail image, date/time, branch, variant (product + variant name), movement type, quantity, batch code, and actor. Card view shows the same fields without the image thumbnail. Rows are ordered newest first. 50 records are loaded per page.

elements.label: Load More
elements.description: Appends the next 50 movements to the current list without resetting scroll position. Only appears when more records are available (More Pages = Yes). A spinner is shown while the next page is fetching.

elements.label: Timeline view
elements.description: A third view mode alongside Cards and Table. Shows movements grouped by day in a chronological timeline. Each day header shows the date, with movements listed underneath.

elements.label: DirectionArrow indicators
elements.description: Visual arrows showing movement direction: ↑ green for stock in (purchases, positive adjustments), ↓ red for stock out (sales, negative adjustments), ↔ blue for internal (transfers, counts).

elements.label: Export Movements button
elements.description: A button in the page header that navigates to the Exports page to create a stock movements export.

elements.label: Movement type filter
elements.description: Dropdown filter with all 10 movement types: PURCHASE_IN, SALE_OUT, ADJUSTMENT_POSITIVE, ADJUSTMENT_NEGATIVE, TRANSFER_IN, TRANSFER_OUT, RETURN_IN, RETURN_OUT, OPENING_BALANCE, STOCK_COUNT_VARIANCE.

elements.label: Actor filter
elements.description: Dropdown filter to show movements by a specific user. Lists all users who have created movements.

elements.label: Total In / Total Out KPIs
elements.description: Two KPI cards showing the total quantity of stock moved in and out for the current filter period.

elements.label: SortableTableHeader
elements.description: Clickable column headers for sorting by Date, Branch, Variant, Type, Quantity, Batch, and Actor.

common_tasks.title: Trace all movements for a specific product variant
steps: Type the product or variant name in the Search box.
Wait for the debounce (350 ms) to trigger the fetch.
Use the Branch filter if the variant is stocked at multiple locations.
Scroll through results or press Load More if More Pages = Yes.

common_tasks.title: View all Sale Out movements in a date range
steps: Click Advanced to reveal the full filter panel.
Select 'Sale Out' from the Movement Type filter.
Enter the start date in From Date and the end date in To Date.
Review the Movement List. Press Load More to see additional records beyond the first 50.

common_tasks.title: Audit movements made by a specific staff member
steps: Click Advanced to reveal filters.
Choose the staff member from the Actor filter.
Optionally narrow by Movement Type or date range.
Check the movement list — Quantity and Type columns confirm what was recorded and when.

common_tasks.title: Share a filtered view with a colleague
steps: Apply any combination of filters (search, branch, type, actor, dates).
Copy the current URL from the browser address bar.
Share the URL — the recipient will see the same filtered view when they open it, provided they have the Stock Movements permission.

common_tasks.title: Switch between table and card view
steps: Click the View button in the top-right of the page header.
Select Table for the dense grid or Cards for the expanded card layout.
The preference is not persisted across sessions — it resets to Table on next visit.

common_tasks.title: Identify the cause of an unexpected stock level
steps: Search for the variant by name.
Sort through movement types to spot unexpected Adjustment or Transfer movements.
Check the Actor column to see who made the change and the Date column for when.
If a Stock Count Variance appears, cross-reference with the Stock Counts page to review the wizard submission that triggered it.

warnings: Movements are immutable. If a movement was recorded incorrectly, create a correcting adjustment — you cannot delete or edit existing rows.
The variant ID filter exists in the backend API but is not exposed in the UI. If you need to filter by exact variant ID, use the API directly or search by variant name.
Load More appends records to the current list. Changing any filter resets the list to the first page — all previously loaded pages are discarded.

entries.title: Supplier Returns
entries.overview: Record the return of goods to a supplier. Each return deducts stock from the branch immediately on submission. Returns can be linked to the original purchase or purchase order for a clean audit trail. If the return total exceeds a configured approval threshold, the stock is still deducted but the return is flagged as PENDING until an approver acts.

elements.name: Search input
elements.description: Filter the return list by keyword — searches across return reasons, supplier names, and variant names.

elements.name: Status filter
elements.description: Filter by return status. PENDING means the return was submitted but is awaiting approval (if an approval threshold applies). COMPLETED means the return is fully processed and stock has been deducted. REJECTED means the return approval was denied — check whether a stock adjustment is needed to reverse the deduction.

elements.name: Branch filter
elements.description: Restricts the return list to records initiated at a specific branch. Use this when reviewing returns for one location — important if multiple branches deal with the same supplier and you want branch-level clarity.

elements.name: Supplier filter
elements.description: Restrict the return list to returns sent to a specific supplier. Useful for reviewing your full returns history with a particular supplier.

elements.name: Date range (From / To)
elements.description: Filters the return list to records created within the selected window. Use this to reconcile supplier returns for a specific period, such as a monthly supplier review or a quarterly credit check.

elements.name: Branch selector (create form)
elements.description: The branch from which the goods are being returned. Stock is deducted from this branch immediately when the return is submitted (inside the modal).

elements.name: Supplier selector
elements.description: The supplier you are returning goods to. Only ACTIVE suppliers appear. The return is associated with this supplier for reporting and audit purposes.

elements.name: Purchase selector
elements.description: Optional. Link the return to a specific purchase record. Linking creates a direct audit trail between the original purchase and the return — recommended when the connection is clear.

elements.name: Purchase Order selector
elements.description: Optional. Link the return to a specific purchase order. If the goods were received via a PO, link to that PO here for a complete trace from order to return.

elements.name: Reason input
elements.description: Required. Explain why the goods are being returned — for example 'Items arrived with damaged packaging', 'Wrong SKU delivered', or 'Quantity over-delivered'. This reason is stored in the audit log and is visible to approvers if approval is required.

elements.name: Return line row
elements.description: One row per item being returned. For each line, select the variant, enter the quantity being returned, the unit cost, and the receiving line the goods originally came from. Each line deducts from the branch's stock when submitted.

elements.name: Receiving line selector (per row)
elements.description: Links the returned stock back to the specific receiving session it came from. Required — stock can only be returned if it was received first through this system. The selector shows only receiving lines for the chosen variant at the chosen branch.

elements.name: Add Line button
elements.description: Adds another return line for an additional variant. Use this when returning multiple different items to the supplier in the same return record (inside the modal).

elements.name: "Create return" button (header) + modal
elements.description: An accent-colored button in the page header opens a modal for creating a new supplier return. The modal contains: branch + supplier selectors, optional purchase + purchase-order link selectors, reason text input, and multi-line items builder (variant + quantity + unit + unit cost + receiving line, with per-line remove). Modal closes on success OR when backend returns `approvalRequired` (in which case the Approval Notice banner displays the pending approval ID). Previously this was an inline collapsible form — moved into a modal.

elements.name: Approval notice banner
elements.description: Appears when the return total exceeds the approval threshold set in Business Settings. Stock is deducted immediately on submission, but the return record is marked PENDING until approved on the Approvals page.

elements.name: Load More button
elements.description: Appends the next page of return records to the current list. The page does not automatically load all records — click this when you need to review returns beyond the initial batch.

elements.name: Reference number
elements.description: Each supplier return has an auto-generated reference number (e.g. RET-001) replacing the old UUID fragment display.

before_you_start.text: You need the purchases.write permission.
A receiving record must exist for the items being returned — you can only return stock that was received through this system. The Receiving line selector shows only records that match.

common_tasks.task: Return damaged goods to a supplier
steps: Fill in Branch and Supplier. In Reason, describe the issue clearly — for example 'Items arrived damaged, packaging crushed on delivery'.
Click Add Line and select the variant being returned.
Select the receiving line the goods came from (the receiving line selector shows only matching records).
Enter the quantity being returned and the unit cost.
Click Submit. Stock is deducted from the branch immediately.

common_tasks.task: Return a wrong item delivered by a supplier
steps: Set Branch and Supplier. In Reason, note 'Wrong SKU delivered — received [variant X] but ordered [variant Y]'.
Add a return line for the incorrect variant, select its receiving line, and enter the quantity and cost.
Click Submit. After the return is processed, raise a new purchase order for the correct item.

common_tasks.task: Check the status of a submitted return
steps: Use the Status filter to show PENDING returns.
If a return is PENDING, it is awaiting approval on the Approvals page — ask a manager to review it.
If a return shows REJECTED, check whether a manual stock adjustment is needed to reverse the deduction.

warnings: Stock is deducted from the branch immediately when you submit — even if the return triggers an approval workflow. If the approval is later rejected, the stock deduction is not reversed automatically — a manual stock adjustment will be needed.

The Receiving line selector only shows lines that match the selected variant at the selected branch. If the expected line is missing, the goods may have been received under a different branch, or the receiving record may not exist yet.
Returns cannot be edited after submission. If you submitted incorrect quantities or the wrong variant, contact your supervisor — a correcting stock adjustment may be required.

entries.title: Suppliers
entries.overview: Manage your supplier directory. Create and maintain vendor profiles, set lead times so the system can suggest delivery ETAs when creating purchase orders, track outstanding balances owed to each supplier, and review recent order activity. Suppliers must exist here before you can create purchase orders, record receivings, or process supplier returns against them.

before_you_start.text: You need the suppliers.write permission to create or edit supplier records. The same permission covers both creating and editing.
Set lead time days accurately on each supplier — this value is used to calculate the suggested delivery ETA when creating purchase orders.

elements.name: KPI — Suppliers
elements.description: Count of supplier records in the current page of results (up to 25). This reflects the active filter set — it is not a business-wide total. Clear all filters to see your full supplier list.

elements.name: KPI — Active
elements.description: Count of ACTIVE suppliers in the current page. Only ACTIVE suppliers can be used in purchase orders, receivings, and supplier returns — if a supplier is missing from those workflows, check their status here.

elements.name: KPI — Needs attention
elements.description: Count of INACTIVE or ARCHIVED suppliers in the current page. Review these periodically — suppliers no longer trading should be ARCHIVED so they are excluded from purchase workflows.

elements.name: KPI — Lead-time set
elements.description: Count of suppliers in the current page who have a lead time configured. Lead times drive the ETA suggestion in the Purchase Order Wizard. If this count is low, consider adding lead times to your regular suppliers for better delivery planning.

elements.name: Search bar
elements.description: Filter suppliers by name, phone, or email as you type. Results update after a short pause (350 ms debounce). Your search is saved in the page URL so you can share or bookmark filtered views.

elements.name: Status filter
elements.description: Narrow the list to ACTIVE, INACTIVE, or ARCHIVED suppliers. ARCHIVED suppliers are excluded from all purchase workflows; their historical records are preserved.

elements.name: Balance due filter
elements.description: Filter to suppliers with an outstanding balance (Yes) or a clear balance (No). 'Yes' shows suppliers who have purchases in an open or received state that may not yet be fully settled. Use this before processing supplier payments to know who to prioritise.

elements.name: Reset button
elements.description: Clears all active search and filter inputs and reloads the full supplier list.

elements.name: View toggle (Cards / Table)
elements.description: Switch between card layout (shows full details per supplier including activity and notes) and table layout (better for scanning name, contact, status, and lead time across many suppliers).

elements.name: Name field (create form)
elements.description: Required. The supplier's business or trading name. Appears in purchase order selectors, receiving forms, and reports — use the name your staff will recognise.

elements.name: Phone field (create form)
elements.description: Optional. The supplier's contact phone number. Stored for reference only.

elements.name: Email field (create form)
elements.description: Optional. The supplier's contact email address. Stored for reference and may be used for purchase order notifications if that feature is enabled.

elements.name: Address field (create form)
elements.description: Optional. The supplier's physical or postal address. Useful for delivery coordination and printed purchase orders.

elements.name: Notes field (create form)
elements.description: Optional. Free-text notes about the supplier — for example payment terms, preferred contact method, or product specialities. Visible to all staff who can see this supplier.

elements.name: Lead time days field (create form)
elements.description: Optional. The typical number of days between placing an order and receiving the goods. When set, the Purchase Order Wizard shows a suggested ETA (today + lead time days) when you select this supplier. Set this accurately for reliable planning.

elements.name: Create Supplier button
elements.description: Submit button inside the Supplier create modal. Saves the new supplier and closes the modal on success; the supplier is immediately available in purchase order, receiving, and supplier return selectors. Requires the suppliers.write permission.

elements.name: Create supplier button (page header)
elements.description: Header action button with Plus icon. Opens the Supplier create modal. Only shown to users with the suppliers.write permission.

elements.name: Supplier create modal
elements.description: Centered modal with name (required), phone, email, address, lead time, notes. ESC or backdrop click closes without saving.

elements.name: Supplier edit modal
elements.description: Centered modal opened from the Pencil action on any supplier. Same fields as create plus status (ACTIVE / INACTIVE / ARCHIVED). Save commits and refreshes the list; ESC or backdrop click cancels.

elements.name: Edit button (per supplier)
elements.description: Pencil action on each supplier card/row. Opens the Supplier edit modal with all fields plus status (ACTIVE / INACTIVE / ARCHIVED). To archive a supplier, change Status to ARCHIVED and click Save. Requires the suppliers.write permission.

elements.name: View Activity / Hide Activity toggle
elements.description: Expands or collapses a panel showing the supplier's 5 most recent purchase orders and 5 most recent purchases. Data loads on first click and is cached — toggling closed and open again does not re-fetch.

elements.name: Related Notes panel
elements.description: A collapsible shared notes thread per supplier. Team members can add timestamped notes visible to all staff — useful for recording price negotiations, delivery complaints, or contact updates. These notes are separate from the profile Notes field.

elements.name: Save / Cancel buttons (edit mode)
elements.description: Save commits all edited fields immediately. Cancel discards changes and closes the inline edit form without saving.

elements.name: Load More button
elements.description: Appends the next 25 suppliers to the list. If a supplier does not appear in the initial results, use Load More or adjust your filters — they may be on a later page.

elements.name: CollapsibleSection (New Supplier form)
elements.description: The create form is hidden behind a 'New Supplier' button. Click to expand. Closes automatically after successful creation.

elements.name: Performance panel
elements.description: Click 'View Performance' on a supplier card to see their metrics: total orders, completed orders, and completion rate percentage. Helps evaluate supplier reliability.

elements.name: RelatedNotesPanel
elements.description: A notes thread attached to each supplier card. Team members can add timestamped notes — useful for recording price negotiations, delivery complaints, or quality feedback.

elements.name: New PO quick link
elements.description: A button on each supplier card that navigates directly to the Purchase Orders page to create a new order for that supplier.

elements.name: Lead time missing warning
elements.description: An amber warning badge shown on supplier cards that have no lead time configured. Setting a lead time helps the system calculate expected delivery dates on purchase orders.

elements.name: StatusBadge
elements.description: Coloured status pills on each supplier. Green for ACTIVE, amber for INACTIVE, grey for ARCHIVED.

elements.name: SortableTableHeader
elements.description: Clickable column headers in table view for sorting by Name, Phone, Email, Lead Time, and Status.

elements.name: Contact info columns (table view)
elements.description: Phone and email are shown as separate columns in table view for quick reference without expanding the supplier card.

common_tasks.task: Add a new supplier
steps: Fill in the Name (required) and optionally phone, email, address, and notes.
If you know the supplier's typical delivery time, enter it in Lead time days.
Click Create Supplier. The supplier is immediately available in purchase workflows.

common_tasks.task: Update a supplier's contact details or lead time
steps: Find the supplier card and click Edit.
Update the relevant fields.
Click Save. The new lead time takes effect on the next purchase order for this supplier.

common_tasks.task: Archive or deactivate a supplier
steps: Find the supplier card and click Edit.
Change the Status field to INACTIVE (temporarily paused) or ARCHIVED (fully retired).
Click Save. Both statuses block new purchases, purchase orders, and supplier returns for that supplier.

common_tasks.task: View recent orders for a supplier
steps: Find the supplier card and click View Activity.
The panel shows the 5 most recent purchase orders and 5 most recent purchases for that supplier.
Click Hide Activity to collapse the panel.

common_tasks.task: Find suppliers with outstanding balances
steps: Set the Balance due filter to Yes.
The list shows suppliers with open or received purchases that may not yet be fully settled.
Use this list before processing supplier payments to prioritise who to pay.

warnings: The KPI cards at the top count records from the current page (up to 25 results), not your entire supplier database. Clear all filters before reading the counts if you need business-wide totals.

Both INACTIVE and ARCHIVED suppliers are blocked from new purchases, purchase orders, and supplier returns — not just ARCHIVED. If a supplier needs to be temporarily paused, set them to INACTIVE; if they are permanently retired, use ARCHIVED.
There is no permanent delete for suppliers — use INACTIVE to pause a supplier or ARCHIVED to retire them. Status changes are made through the Edit form.

Lead time days affects the ETA hint in the Purchase Order Wizard. If set too low, staff may expect deliveries earlier than the supplier can actually fulfil — review and update lead times when supplier agreements change.

related_pages.reason: Create and manage orders placed with suppliers.
Record and track supplier invoices and payments.
Process returns of goods back to a supplier.

entries.title: Transfers
entries.overview: Move stock between branches with a full audit trail. Create a transfer by selecting source and destination branches and adding product lines, then Approve it to dispatch stock from the source and place it in transit. The destination branch receives the goods using per-item quantity inputs that support partial receiving. An optional transfer fee automatically creates an expense record at the source branch. A guided Transfer Wizard is available for step-by-step creation and same-session receiving.

before_you_start.text: You need the transfers.write permission to create, approve, receive, and cancel transfers. The same permission covers all four actions.
Source and destination branches must be different. All variants being transferred must have stock tracking enabled — untracked variants are rejected at creation.

elements.name: KPI — Transfer rows
elements.description: Count of transfer records in the current page of results. This is a page-scoped count, not a business-wide total. Use the status and date filters to narrow results before reading this number.

elements.name: KPI — Draft items
elements.description: Number of line items currently in the create form below. Starts at 1 (one blank line). Increases as you click Add Item. Use this to track how many product lines you have added before submitting.

elements.name: KPI — Current page
elements.description: The pagination page you are currently viewing. Transfers are ordered newest first. Use the pagination controls at the bottom to move between pages.

elements.name: KPI — Source branch
elements.description: Indicates whether a source branch is selected in the create form. The source branch auto-fills from your active branch scope on page load — change it in the form if you need to create a transfer from a different branch.

elements.name: Search field
elements.description: A text input that accepts search terms, but transfer list filtering is performed by status and date range only. Use the Status filter and date pickers to locate specific transfers.

elements.name: Status filter
elements.description: Filter transfers by status: Pending (created, not yet approved), In Transit (approved, stock dispatched), Completed (all items received), or Cancelled. Use Pending and In Transit to find transfers that still need action.

elements.name: From date filter
elements.description: Show only transfers created on or after this date. Use together with the To date filter to focus on a specific period — for example, all transfers from last week.

elements.name: To date filter
elements.description: Show only transfers created on or before this date. Combined with From date, this gives you a precise date-range view of transfer activity.

elements.name: Open Wizard button
elements.description: Opens the Transfer Wizard — a 4-step guided flow (Details → Items → Review → Receive) that walks you through creating and immediately receiving a transfer in a single session. Useful for same-day branch-to-branch moves.

elements.name: View toggle (Cards / Table)
elements.description: Switch between card layout (shows all transfer details including per-item lines and action buttons) and table layout (shows source, destination, status, date, item count, and fee in a compact row).

elements.name: Source branch selector
elements.description: The branch that stock will be dispatched from. Auto-fills from your active branch scope. Must differ from the destination branch. Stock is checked against this branch's snapshot when negative stock is not allowed.

elements.name: Destination branch selector
elements.description: The branch that will receive the stock. Must differ from the source branch. The destination branch's in-transit quantity is incremented when the transfer is approved.

elements.name: Transfer line item (Variant / Quantity / Batch)
elements.description: Each line represents one product variant being transferred. Select the variant (live search), enter the quantity, and optionally select a batch (only available when batch tracking is enabled in Settings). Add multiple lines for multi-product transfers. The variant must have stock tracking enabled and must be active at the source branch.

elements.name: Add Item button
elements.description: Appends a new blank transfer line to the create form. Use this to add multiple product variants to a single transfer rather than creating separate transfers for each product.

elements.name: Fee amount field
elements.description: Optional. The cost of transporting the goods (e.g. courier or freight fee). If entered, approving the transfer automatically creates an expense record under the TRANSFER_FEE category at the source branch. Leave blank if there is no transport cost.

elements.name: Fee currency field
elements.description: Optional. The ISO currency code for the transfer fee (e.g. TZS, USD). Auto-fills from your business locale settings. Must be a valid ISO 4217 currency code — invalid codes are rejected at submission.

elements.name: Fee carrier field
elements.description: Optional. The name of the transport provider or carrier (e.g. a logistics company or driver name). Stored for reference and included in the auto-created expense record.

elements.name: Fee note field
elements.description: Optional. Any additional notes about the transfer fee — for example an invoice number or payment reference. Stored alongside the expense record for audit purposes.

elements.name: Create Transfer button
elements.description: Submits the transfer in Pending status. Stock is not moved yet — the transfer must be Approved before any stock leaves the source branch. Requires the transfers.write permission. Transfers cannot be created while the device is in offline mode.

elements.name: Approve button (per transfer)
elements.description: Dispatches stock from the source branch — records a TRANSFER_OUT stock movement, decrements the source snapshot, and increments the destination's in-transit quantity. Status changes to In Transit. If the transfer quantity exceeds your approval threshold, the system submits an approval request instead and no stock moves until a manager approves it.

elements.name: Per-item receive quantity input
elements.description: An inline quantity field shown for each line item on an In Transit transfer. Pre-filled with the remaining quantity to receive. Adjust downward to record a partial delivery — the transfer stays In Transit and you can receive the remainder later.

elements.name: Receive button (per transfer)
elements.description: Confirms receipt of goods at the destination branch using the quantities entered in the per-item fields. Records TRANSFER_IN stock movements and increments the destination snapshot. If all items are fully received, status changes to Completed. The transfer must be In Transit before receiving — Pending transfers must be approved first.

elements.name: Cancel button (per transfer)
elements.description: Cancels the transfer after a confirmation prompt. If the transfer is In Transit, stock movements are reversed — the source branch snapshot is restored and the destination's in-transit quantity is decremented. Pending transfers can be cancelled without any stock reversal.

elements.name: Pagination controls
elements.description: Navigate between pages of transfers and change the page size. The total count is fetched on page 1 and displayed alongside the page controls. Use these to browse older transfers beyond the first page.

elements.name: CollapsibleSection (New Transfer form)
elements.description: The create form is hidden behind a 'New Transfer' button. Includes source branch, destination branch, items, and optional transfer fees. Closes after creation.

elements.name: Transfer fee fields
elements.description: Optional fields to record fees associated with a transfer: fee amount, carrier name, and fee notes. Fees are tracked as expenses.

elements.name: Duplicate Transfer action
elements.description: Creates a copy of an existing transfer with the same source, destination, and items. Useful for recurring transfers between the same branches.

elements.name: Transfer fee summary KPI
elements.description: Shows the total transfer fees for the current period.

elements.name: StatusBadge
elements.description: Coloured status pills showing transfer status: REQUESTED, APPROVED, IN_TRANSIT, COMPLETED, CANCELLED.

elements.name: SortableTableHeader
elements.description: Clickable column headers for sorting by Source, Destination, Status, Date, Items, and Fee.

elements.name: Open Wizard link
elements.description: Navigates to the Transfer Wizard for a guided multi-step transfer creation process.

elements.name: Confirmation dialogs
elements.description: Approve and Cancel actions require confirmation before executing. The dialog shows the action and asks for confirmation.

elements.name: Reference number
elements.description: Each transfer has an auto-generated reference number (e.g. TRF-001). Shown as a subtitle under the branch route in both card and table views.

common_tasks.task: Create a transfer
steps: Select the Source branch (auto-filled from your active branch) and the Destination branch.
For each product, select the variant, enter the quantity, and optionally select a batch.
Click Add Item to include more product lines if needed.
Optionally enter a fee amount, currency, carrier, and note if there is a transport cost.
Click Create Transfer. The transfer is created in Pending status — stock has not moved yet.

common_tasks.task: Approve a transfer (dispatch stock from source)
steps: Find the transfer in Pending status and click Approve.
Confirm the action. Stock is dispatched from the source branch and placed in transit.
If an approval request is raised instead, a manager must approve it before stock moves.

common_tasks.task: Receive a transfer in full
steps: Find the transfer in In Transit status.
The per-item quantity fields are pre-filled with the full remaining quantity — leave them as-is to receive everything.
Click Receive. Status changes to Completed and the destination branch stock is updated.

common_tasks.task: Partially receive a transfer
steps: Find the transfer in In Transit status.
Reduce the quantity in any per-item fields to match what was actually delivered.
Click Receive. Status stays In Transit. Return later and receive the remainder using the same steps.

common_tasks.task: Cancel a transfer
steps: Find the transfer and click Cancel.
Confirm the action. If the transfer was In Transit, the source stock is restored automatically.
Cancelled transfers cannot be reopened — create a new transfer if needed.

common_tasks.task: Use the Transfer Wizard for a guided same-session flow
steps: Click Open Wizard in the page header.
Step 1 — Details: select source and destination branches and enter any fee details.
Step 2 — Items: add variants, quantities, and batches; use Add and Remove to manage lines.
Step 3 — Review: confirm your item summary and click Create Transfer.
Step 4 — Receive: approve the transfer and enter received quantities, then click Receive Transfer. Use Finish Later to return to the main transfers page and receive later.

warnings: Transfers are fully online-only. Creating, approving, receiving, and cancelling all require an active connection — these actions are blocked in offline mode with no offline queue.
Stock does not leave the source branch when a transfer is Created — it moves only when the transfer is Approved. Do not assume stock has been dispatched until the status shows In Transit.

If a fee amount is entered, approving the transfer automatically creates an expense record under the TRANSFER_FEE category at the source branch. Review your Expenses page if you see unexpected entries.
Cancelling an In Transit transfer reverses the stock movements automatically — the source branch snapshot is restored and the destination's in-transit quantity is decremented. This cannot be undone.
Only variants with stock tracking enabled can be transferred. If a variant is rejected at creation, check that its stock tracking setting is turned on in the Catalogue.

related_pages.reason: Ensure source and destination branches exist before creating a transfer.
Check stock levels at the source branch before creating a transfer.
Transfer fees are auto-created as TRANSFER_FEE expenses — view them here.

entries.title: Transfer Wizard
entries.overview: A 4-step guided form to create a stock transfer between branches and optionally receive it in the same session. Source branch stock is deducted when the transfer is created. Destination branch stock is only updated when the transfer is received. Supports optional transfer fee recording and batch-specific transfers when batch tracking is enabled.

elements.name: Step indicator
elements.description: Shows current position: Details → Items → Review → Receive. Earlier steps are locked once you click Create Transfer on the Review step — you cannot go back to change the source branch or items after that point.

elements.name: Source branch selector
elements.description: The branch that is sending the stock. Stock is deducted from this branch's inventory when the transfer is created — not when it arrives at the destination. Ensure the source branch has sufficient stock before creating the transfer.

elements.name: Destination branch selector
elements.description: The branch that will receive the stock. Stock is only added to this branch when the transfer is received and confirmed. The source and destination cannot be the same branch.

elements.name: Fee amount input
elements.description: Optional. The cost of shipping, transport, or logistics for this transfer. Recorded for expense tracking purposes only — it does not affect stock levels or the items being transferred.

elements.name: Fee currency input
elements.description: The currency the transfer fee is charged in. Defaults to your business currency. Change this if the fee is invoiced in a foreign currency.

elements.name: Fee carrier input
elements.description: Optional. The name of the transport company or carrier handling the delivery — for example 'DHL', 'Own Vehicle', or 'Third-party Courier'. Stored for reference and reporting.

elements.name: Fee note input
elements.description: Optional. Additional notes about the transfer fee — for example a waybill number, tracking reference, or any special delivery instructions.

elements.name: Item row (Items step)
elements.description: One row per item being transferred. For each row, select the variant to transfer, enter the quantity to send, and optionally select a specific batch if batch tracking is enabled. The quantity you enter is deducted from the source branch immediately on transfer creation.

elements.name: Batch selector (per item)
elements.description: Available when batch tracking is enabled in Business Settings. Select which batch the transferred stock should come from. Only batches with available stock at the source branch appear. Use this when you need to move a specific batch of perishable or regulated items.

elements.name: Add Item button
elements.description: Adds another item row. Use this when transferring multiple different variants in a single transfer — one row per variant.

elements.name: Remove Item button
elements.description: Removes this item row from the transfer before it is submitted. Use it if you added the wrong variant or need to adjust the item list. Once the transfer is created and submitted, individual lines cannot be removed — a corrective transfer in the opposite direction is required.

elements.name: Review step
elements.description: Full summary of the transfer with source and destination branches, all items and quantities, and fee details. Review carefully — clicking Create Transfer locks all details and deducts stock from the source branch immediately.

elements.name: Create Transfer button
elements.description: Creates the transfer record and immediately deducts the specified quantities from the source branch. The transfer is now in transit — the destination branch must confirm receipt separately to update their stock.

elements.name: Receive step
elements.description: After creating the transfer, optionally confirm receipt in the same session. Useful for same-day internal moves (e.g. from warehouse to shop floor in the same building). Enter the quantity received per item. To receive later, skip this step and use the Transfers page.

elements.name: Receive button
elements.description: Records the receipt and updates destination branch stock immediately. If an approval is required for the transfer total, this button is blocked until the approval is granted on the Approvals page.

elements.name: Approval notice banner
elements.description: Appears when the transfer total exceeds the approval threshold set in Business Settings. The transfer is created and stock is deducted from the source branch, but the destination cannot receive it until a user with approvals.write permission approves it.

elements.name: Copy from previous transfer
elements.description: Load items from a recent transfer as a template. Select from a list of recent transfers.

elements.name: Fee section
elements.description: Optional fields for transfer fees: amount, currency, carrier name, and notes.

elements.name: KPI cards
elements.description: Four KPI cards: Current Step, Draft Items, Valid Items, Transfer Created.

elements.name: Receive step (expected vs received)
elements.description: Enter quantities actually received at the destination. Compare against expected quantities.

elements.name: Finish Later button
elements.description: Save progress and return to the Transfers list. Resume later.

elements.name: Visual progress bar
elements.description: Step indicator: Details, Items, Review, Receive.

before_you_start.text: You need the transfers.write permission.
Both the source and destination branches must already exist.
Enable batch tracking in Business Settings before creating the transfer if you need to move stock by specific batch.

common_tasks.task: Create a transfer between two branches
steps: On the Details step, select the source branch (sending) and destination branch (receiving). Optionally add fee details. Click Next.
On the Items step, add one row per variant. Select the variant and enter the quantity to send. Click Next.
On the Review step, verify all details. Click Create Transfer — stock is deducted from the source branch immediately.
Skip the Receive step if the destination will receive it later from the Transfers page.

common_tasks.task: Create and receive a transfer in one session (same-day internal move)
steps: Complete the Details, Items, and Review steps and click Create Transfer.
On the Receive step, verify or adjust the received quantities.
Click Receive. Destination branch stock is updated immediately.

common_tasks.task: Transfer a specific batch of stock
steps: On the Items step, select the variant to transfer.
Use the Batch selector to choose the specific batch to move from the source branch.
Enter the quantity and continue to Review, then Create Transfer.
